blocks:
Surah An-Nisa (The Women)

Medinan, and it is 176 verses.

Surah An-Nisa (The Women)

**Entirely Medinan.

(1) And the number of its verses is one hundred and seventy-seven verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful) (1) ** يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ نَفْسٍ واحِدَةٍ (O mankind! Be wary of your Lord who created you from a single soul) This soul is Adam, upon our Prophet and upon him be peace and blessings. ** وَخَلَقَ مِنْها زَوْجَها (and created His mate from it) This is Eve (حواء). Al-Qummi narrated that He created her from his lowest ribs. وَبَثَّ مِنْهُما رِجالاً كَثِيراً وَنِساءً (and scattered from both of them numerous men and women). [This refers to] many sons and daughters. He ordered piety (التقوى) based on this, because it contains an indication of the Overpowering Might (القدرة القاهرة) which deserves to be feared, and the manifest favor (النعمة الظاهرة) which necessitates obedience to its Master (مولاها).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) that he said: Eve (حواء) was created from the short ribs (قصيري) of Adam's side, and the short rib (القصير) is the smallest rib. Then Allah replaced its place with flesh. In another narration: Eve (حواء) was created from Adam's side while he was asleep (راقد).

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah created Adam from water and clay (الماء والطين), so the concern (همة) of the son of Adam is in water and clay. And indeed, Allah created Eve (حواء) from Adam, so the concern (همة) of women is with men, so keep them protected (فحصنوهن) in the houses.

In al-Faqih and al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the creation of Eve (حواء), and it was said to him that some people among us say that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, created Eve (حواء) from Adam's farthest left rib. He said: "Glory be to Allah! He is far exalted above that, a great exaltation! Whoever says this claims that Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, did not have the power (القدرة) to create a wife for Adam from other than his rib, and [this] gives a way for the speaker among the people of slander (أهل التشنيع) to speak, saying that Adam was marrying a part of himself if she was from his rib. What is wrong with these people? May Allah judge between us and them." Then he said: "Indeed, when Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, created Adam from clay (طين) and commanded the angels, and they prostrated to him, He cast sleep (السبات) upon him. Then He originated Eve (حواء) for him and placed her in the hollow (النقرة) between his hips (وركيه). This was so that the woman would be subordinate (تبعاً) to the man. She began to move, and he awoke due to her movement. When he awoke, he was called upon: 'Move away from him.' When he looked at her, he saw a beautiful creation (خلق حسن) resembling his form, except that she was female. He spoke to her, and she spoke to him in his language. He asked her: 'Who are you?' She replied: 'A creation (خلق) whom Allah created as you see.' Adam (peace be upon him) then said: 'O Lord, who is this beautiful creation whose proximity and sight have brought me comfort (آنسني)?' Allah said: 'O Adam, this is My maidservant (أمتي) Eve (حواء). Do you wish for her to be with you, to comfort you, converse with you, and obey your command (تأتمر لأمرك)?' He said: 'Yes, O Lord, and upon me is the obligation of gratitude (الشكر) and praise (الحمد) for that as long as I live.' Allah, the Exalted, said: 'Then propose to Me for her, for she is My maidservant, and she may also be suitable for you as a wife for desire (للشهوة).' And Allah cast desire (الشهوة) upon him, having taught him the knowledge (المعرفة) of everything before that. Adam said: 'O Lord, I propose to You for her. What is Your satisfaction (رضاك) for that?' He said: 'My satisfaction is that you teach her the landmarks (معالم) of My religion.' Adam said: 'That is upon me for You, O Lord, if You wish that for me.' He said: 'I have wished that, and I have married her to you, so embrace her (فضمها إليك).' Adam said to her: 'Come to me (إليّ فاقبلي).' She said to him: 'No, rather, you come to me (بل أنت فاقبل إليّ).' So Allah, the Exalted, commanded Adam to go to her, and he went. Were it not for that, women would go and propose for themselves. This is the story of Eve (حواء)."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked from what thing Allah created Eve (حواء). He said: "What do these people say?" I said: "They say that Allah created her from a rib (ضلع) among Adam's ribs." He said: "They lied. Was He incapable (يعجز) of creating her from other than his rib?" Then he said: "My father informed me, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), who said: The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) said: 'Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, took a handful (قبضة) of clay (طين) and mixed it with His Right Hand (بيمينه)—and both His Hands are Right—and created Adam from it. And a remnant (فضلة) of the clay remained, and He created Eve (حواء) from it.'"

In al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, created Adam from clay (طين), and Eve (حواء) was created from its remnant (فضلته) and remainder (بقيته). In another narration: She was created from his inner being (باطنه), and from his left side (شماله), and from the clay (الطينة) that remained from his left rib (ضلعه الأيسر).

He said in al-Faqih: As for the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: ** يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ نَفْسٍ واحِدَةٍ وَخَلَقَ مِنْها زَوْجَها (O mankind! Be wary of your Lord who created you from a single soul and created His mate from it), and the report (الخبر) that was narrated: that Eve (حواء) was created from Adam's left rib (ضلع آدم الأيسر) is authentic (صحيح). Its meaning is: from the clay (الطينة) that remained from his left rib. For this reason, the ribs of men are fewer than the ribs of women by one rib.

I SAY: Thus, what was reported—that she was created from his left rib—is an indication that the physical, animalistic aspect (الجهة الجسمانية الحيوانية) in women is stronger than it is in men, and the spiritual, angelic aspect (الجهة الروحانية الملكية) is the reverse of that. This is because the Right (اليمين) is what is alluded to regarding the spiritual realm of al-Malakut (عالم الملكوت الروحاني), and the Left (الشمال) is what is alluded to regarding the physical realm of al-Mulk (عالم الملك الجسماني). Thus, clay (الطين) is an expression for the matter of the body (مادة الجسم), and the Right (اليمين) is an expression for the matter of the spirit (مادة الروح). And there is no Mulk (ملك) (1) except through Malakut (ملكوت). This is the meaning of His saying: "And both His Hands are Right (وكلتا يديه يمين)." The left rib (الضلع الأيسر) that is deficient in Adam is an allusion (كناية) to some of the desires (الشهوات) that arise from the dominance of the physicality (غلبة الجسمية), which belongs to the world of creation (عالم الخلق). This is the remnant (فضلة) of his clay (طينه) extracted from his inner being (باطنه), which became the matter (مادة) for the creation of Eve (حواء). Thus, the Hadith alerts us that the aspect of al-Malakut (الملكوت) and the Command (الأمر) in men is stronger than the aspect of al-Mulk (الملك) and Creation (الخلق), and the reverse is true for women, for the manifest (الظاهر) is the title (عنوان) of the inner being (الباطن). This is the secret (السر) behind this deficiency in the bodies of men compared to women. And the secrets of Allah are not attained except by the people of the secret (أهل السر). Therefore, the denial (التكذيب) in the speech of the Infallibles (المعصومين) only refers to what the common people (العامّة) understood by taking it literally (حمله على الظاهر), not to the essence of the Hadith (أصل الحديث).

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the beginning of the progeny (النسل) from the descendants (ذرّية) of Adam. It was said to him that some people among us say that Allah, the Exalted, revealed to Adam that he should marry his daughters to his sons, and that the origin of all this creation is from brothers and sisters. He said: "Glory be to Allah! He is far exalted above that, a great exaltation! Whoever says this claims that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made the origin of the elite of His creation (صفوة خلقه), His beloved ones (أحبائه), His prophets (أنبيائه), His messengers (رسله), the believing men and women (المؤمنين والمؤمنات), and the submitting men and women (المسلمين والمسلمات) from unlawful means (حرام), and that He did not have the power (القدرة) to create them from lawful means (الحلال), even though He took their covenant (ميثاقهم) upon lawful means (الحلال), and pure, clean goodness (الطهر الطاهر الطيب). By Allah, I have been informed that one of the beasts (البهائم) failed to recognize its sister. When he mounted her and descended, she was revealed to him, and he knew she was his sister, so he removed his penis (عزموله)..."

(1) It is said: It is Medinan, except for His saying: ** إِنَّ اللهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تُؤَدُّوا الْأَماناتِ (Indeed Allah commands you to deliver the trusts) the Ayah, and His saying: يَسْتَفْتُونَكَ فِي النِّساءِ قُلِ اللهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ (They ask you for a ruling concerning women. Say, 'Allah gives you a ruling') regarding al-Kalalah (الكلالة) the Ayah, for both of them were revealed in Mecca. "From him (منه)."

(2) Then he seized him with his teeth, then he pulled him out, then he fell down dead. And in another narration from him (peace be upon him), there is something close to this, with an eloquent emphasis on the prohibition of sisters upon brothers, and that it has always been thus in the four famous revealed Books. And that a generation of this creation turned away from the knowledge of the people of the households of the Prophets (أهل بيوتات الأنبياء) and took from where they were not commanded to take, so they arrived at the misguidance (الضلال) and ignorance (الجهل) that you now see. And at the end of it [the narration]: "He who says this and similar things intends nothing but to strengthen the arguments of the Zoroastrians (المجوس). What is wrong with them? May Allah fight them!" Then he said: "Adam had seventy births (بطناً); in every birth, there was a boy and a girl, until Abel (هابيل) was killed. When Abel was killed, Adam grieved for Abel with a grief that prevented him from approaching women, so he remained unable to approach Eve (حواء) for five hundred years. Then the grief that afflicted him cleared, and he approached Eve, and Allah bestowed upon him Seth (شيث) alone, with no second [child] accompanying him. The name of Seth is هبة الله (Gift of Allah), and he was the first successor (وصي) among the children of Adam on Earth to whom a testament was made. Then, after Seth, Japheth (يافث) was born to him, with no second [child] accompanying him. When they reached maturity, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, willed that the progeny should reach what you see, and that what the Pen had decreed regarding the prohibition of what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has forbidden—sisters upon brothers—should occur, He sent down after the afternoon prayer (العصر) on Thursday a ḥūrī from Paradise named نزلة (Nazlah). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded Adam to marry her to Seth, so he married her to him. Then, after the afternoon prayer on the following day, He sent down a ḥūrī from Paradise named منزلة (Manzilah). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded Adam to marry her to Japheth, so he married her to him. Then a boy was born to Seth, and a girl was born to Japheth. When they reached maturity, Allah the Almighty commanded Adam to marry the daughter of Japheth to the son of Seth, and he did so. And the chosen ones (الصفوة) among the Prophets and Messengers were born from their progeny. And Allah forbid that it should be as they claimed regarding the matter of brothers and sisters."

In al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Adam had Seth born to him, and his name is هبة الله (Gift of Allah), and he was the first successor (وصي) among the children of Adam to whom a testament was made. And he continued the narration until the end of what he mentioned.

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "People claim that Adam married his daughter to his son." He replied: "People have indeed said that, but did you not know that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'If I knew that Adam married his daughter to his son, I would have married Zaynab to al-Qasim, and I would not turn away from the religion of Adam.'"

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Zoroastrians (المجوس) were mentioned to him, and that they say marriage is like the marriage of the children of Adam, and they argue against us with that. He said: "As for you, they cannot argue against you with it. When Hibat Allah (Seth) reached maturity, Adam said: 'O Lord, marry Hibat Allah.' So Allah sent down a ḥūrī, and she bore him four boys. Then Allah raised her up. When the children of Hibat Allah reached maturity, he said: 'O Lord, marry the children of Hibat Allah.' So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to him that he should propose to a man from the Jinn (الجن), who was a Muslim, for his four daughters for the children of Hibat Allah. So he married them to them. Whatever beauty (جمال) and forbearance (حلم) they possessed came from the ḥūrī, and the prophethood (النبوّة) came from their lineage tracing back to Adam (peace be upon him). And whatever foolishness (سفه) or sharpness (حدة) (1) they possessed came from the Jinn."

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), said: Adam had four male children born to him, so Allah sent down to him four ḥūr (plural of ḥūrī), and He married one to each of them, and they reproduced. Then Allah raised them up and married these four [sons] to four [daughters] from the Jinn (الجن), and the progeny continued through them. Whatever forbearance (حلم) they possessed came from Adam, and whatever beauty (جمال) they possessed came from the wide-eyed ḥūr (الحور العين), and whatever ugliness (قبح) or bad character (سوء خلق) they possessed came from the Jinn. And in a narration: When Hibat Allah was born to Adam and grew up, he asked Allah to marry him. So Allah sent down a ḥūrī from Paradise for him, and he married her, and she bore him four sons. Then another son was born to Adam. When he grew up, He commanded him to marry from the Jinn, and she bore him four daughters. So the sons of the former married the daughters of the latter. Whatever beauty (جمال) they possessed came from the ḥūrī, whatever forbearance (حلم) they possessed came from Adam, and whatever lightness (خفة) they possessed came from the Jinn. When they had reproduced, the ḥūrī ascended to the heaven.

In al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down a ḥūrī from Paradise to Adam, and he married her to one of his two sons, and the other son married a daughter of the Jinn. Whatever great beauty (جمال كثير) or good character (حسن خلق) is found among people is from the ḥūrī, and whatever bad character (سوء خلق) is found among them is from Adam [and] the daughter of the Jinn.

In Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): Eve (حواء) carried Abel (هابيل) and a sister for him in one womb, then she carried Cain (قابيل) and a sister for him in the second womb. So Abel married the one who was with Cain, and Cain married the one who was with Abel. Then the prohibition (التحريم) occurred after that.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Eve, the wife of Adam, used to give birth to a boy and a girl in every womb. In the first womb, she gave birth to Cain (قابيل)—and it is said Qabin (قابين)—and his twin sister Iqlima (إقليما), the daughter of Adam. And in the second womb, Abel (هابيل) and his twin sister (2) Lawza' (لوزاء). When they all reached maturity, Allah commanded Adam to marry Cain to Abel's sister and Abel to Cain's sister. Abel agreed, but Cain refused, because his own sister was the more beautiful of the two. He said: "Allah did not command this, rather this is your own opinion." So Allah commanded them both to offer a sacrifice (قرباناً), and they agreed to that. [The narration continues] The rest of it will come in Sūrat al-Mā’idah (The Table) during the exegesis of وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ. (2) Recite to them the account of Adam’s two sons.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), relating to a man from Quraysh. He said: When Allah accepted Adam's repentance, he approached Eve, and he had not approached her since he and she were created except on Earth, and that was after Allah accepted his repentance. He said: And he used to venerate the House (البيت) and what surrounded it of the sanctity of the House (حرمة البيت). So when he intended to approach Eve, he would leave the sacred precinct (الحرم) and take her out with him. When he came near the sacred precinct, he would approach her in the non-sacred area (الحل), and then they would both perform ghusl (ritual bath) out of his veneration for the sacred precinct. Then he would return to the courtyard of the House. He said: Adam had twenty males and twenty females born to him from Eve; a male and a female were born to him in every womb. The first womb Eve bore was Abel (هابيل) and with him a girl called Iqlima (إقليما). He said: And in the second womb, she bore Cain (قابيل) and with him a girl called Lawza' (لوزاء). Lawza' was the most beautiful of Adam's daughters. He said: When they reached maturity, Adam feared temptation (الفتنة) for them, so he called them to him and said: "I want to marry you, O Abel, to Lawza', and marry you, O Cain, to Iqlima." Cain said: "I am not pleased with this! You marry me to Abel's ugly sister and marry Abel to my beautiful sister?" He said: "Then I shall cast lots between you. If your lot, O Cain, falls upon Lawza', or your lot, O Abel, falls upon Iqlima, I will marry each of you to the one upon whom his lot falls." He said: So they agreed to that and cast lots. He said: Cain's lot fell upon Iqlima, Abel's sister, and Abel's lot fell upon Lawza', Cain's sister. He said: So he married them according to what came forth for them from Allah. He said: Then Allah the Almighty prohibited the marriage of sisters after that. He said: The Qurayshi man asked him: "Did they bear children?" He said: "Yes." The Qurayshi man said to him: "This is what the Zoroastrians (المجوس) do today." He (peace be upon him) said: "The Zoroastrians only did that after the prohibition from Allah." Then he (peace be upon him) said to him: "Do not deny this; these are merely the laws (شرائع) of Allah that have occurred. Did not Allah create Adam's wife from him, and then make her lawful for him? That was a law among their laws. Then Allah sent down the prohibition after that." If it is asked how these narrations can be reconciled with the first narrations, we say: The first narrations are the sound (الصحيحة) and reliable ones, whereas the latter ones were only reported in agreement with the general public (العامة), so they are not relied upon, although it is permissible to interpret them (1) in a way that agrees with the first ones.

وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ الَّذِي تَسائَلُونَ بِهِ (1) And be wary of Allah, in Whose Name you plead with one another, meaning: some of you ask others, saying, "I ask you by Allah." The original form is تَتَساءَلُونَ (tatasā’alūna), so the tā’ (التاء) was merged into the sīn (السين). It was also recited with a lighter pronunciation (بالتخفيف) by omitting the tā’. وَالْأَرْحامَ (1) and [be wary of] the blood ties (الأرحام), meaning: be wary of the blood ties lest you sever them.

Thus it is in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). And it is said that it is derived from their saying: "I ask you by Allah and the womb (الرحم) to do such-and-such," or "I adjure you by Allah and the womb." Meaning: just as you venerate Allah with your words, venerate Him by obeying Him. The reading of it with the jarr (الجر) (genitive case) is based on this interpretation.

And al-Qummi said: You will be questioned (تساءلون) on the Day of Resurrection about piety (التقوى): whether you were pious; and about the blood ties (الرحم): whether you maintained them.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the blood ties (أرحام) of people. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded their maintenance (صلتها) and magnified them. Do you not see that He placed them alongside Him?

I say: Meaning He coupled it with His Name in the command for piety (التقوى).

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), who said: "Maintain your blood ties (أرحامكم) even if it is only by greeting (بالتسليم)." Then he recited this Ayah.

And from al-Rida (peace be upon him): The womb (رحم) of the Household of Muhammad, the Imams (may the prayers and peace of Allah be upon them), is indeed suspended from the Throne (العرش), saying: "O Allah, connect with whoever connects with me, and cut off whoever cuts me off." Then, after that, it applies to the blood ties (أرحام) of the believers. Then he recited this Ayah.

In al-Uyun, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah commanded three things coupled with three things... up to His saying: "And He commanded piety toward Allah (اتقاء الله) and the maintenance of blood ties (صلة الرحم). So whoever does not maintain his blood ties has not been pious toward Allah."

And from him, from his father, from his forefathers, from Ali (peace be upon them), who said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "When I was taken up to the heaven (أسري بي إلى السماء), I saw a womb (رحماً) suspended from the Throne (العرش), complaining about another womb to its Lord. I asked it: 'How many fathers are between you and it?' It replied: 'We meet at forty fathers.'" إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلَيْكُمْ رَقِيباً (1) Indeed Allah is watchful over you, meaning: a Preserver (حفيظاً).

(2) وَآتُوا الْيَتامى أَمْوالَهُمْ (2) Give the orphans their property, meaning: when they reach maturity and you perceive in them sound judgment (رشداً), as stated in the other Ayah. وَلا تَتَبَدَّلُوا الْخَبِيثَ بِالطَّيِّبِ (2) and do not substitute the bad for the good, meaning: do not substitute the unlawful (الحرام) part of their property for the lawful (الحلال) part of your property, by hastily taking the unlawful part of their property before the lawful provision (الرزق الحلال) decreed for you comes to you. And it was said: They used to take the valuable (الرفيع) parts of the orphans' property and put worthless (الخسيس) parts in their place, so they were forbidden from doing that. وَلا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوالَهُمْ إِلى أَمْوالِكُمْ (2) and do not consume their property along with your own property, meaning: joined to it, treating them equally, for one is lawful (حلال) and the other is unlawful (حرام). This means regarding what exceeds the amount of his wage, due to His saying, the Glorified: فَلْيَأْكُلْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (6) let him consume in a rightful manner. إِنَّهُ كانَ حُوباً كَبِيراً (2) for that is a great sin (ذنباً عظيماً).

(1) Al-Ḥiddah (الحدة): That which afflicts a person of rashness (النزق) and anger (الغضب). It is said ḥadda yaḥiddu (حدّ يحد) when he became angry (غضب) (Majma').

(2) Al-Taw'am (التوأم) refers to any animal born with another in the same womb, from two upwards, whether male or female, or male and female. Its plural is tawā’im (توائم) and tu’ām (توآم), like rikhāl (رخال). Taw’am (توأم) is said for the male and taw’amah (توأمة) for the female. If they are combined, they are taw’amān (توأمان) and tu’am (توأم). The mother has given birth to twins (it’amat), and she is mut’im (متئم), and her habit is mut’im (متئم). He was born with his brother (ta’ama akhāhu), and he is ti’mah (تئمة) (with a kasrah), taw’amah (توأمة), and tiyāmah (تيامة) (Qāmūs).

(1) His saying: although it is permissible to interpret them: The reason (علل) intended by this is that the twin birth (التوأمة) in every womb is that Allah the Almighty sometimes sent down from the clay (طينة) of the ḥūrī into Eve's womb something that was in the position of semen (النطفة), but not from Adam's semen (نطفة), similar to what He did with Mary (مريم); and at other times, from the clay of the Jinn (الجان) in the same manner. Or, what was denied in the first [narrations] was marriage from a single womb, so it does not contradict the second [narrations], and other interpretations that the reflective person (المتأمل) may find.

(٣) وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تُقْسِطُوا فِي الْيَتامى فَانْكِحُوا ما طابَ لَكُمْ مِنَ النِّساءِ (3) If you fear that you may not observe justice in the case of the orphans, then marry such women as are good to you It was said that it means: If you fear that you will not deal justly with the orphaned women if you marry them, then marry such women as are good to you from others. This is because a man would find an orphaned girl possessing wealth and beauty and marry her, being protective (ضناً) (1) of her [wealth/beauty]. Perhaps a number of them would gather under him, and he would not be able to fulfill their rights.

And al-Qummi and others mentioned other aspects regarding the reason for its revelation, the manner of its resulting arrangement (نظام محصوله), and the connection of its sections (اتصال فصوله), but none of them is free from strain (تعسف).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said to one of the heretics (الزنادقة) in a narration: "As for your observation of the incongruity (تناكر) of the Almighty's saying: ** وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تُقْسِطُوا فِي الْيَتامى فَانْكِحُوا ما طابَ لَكُمْ مِنَ النِّساءِ ** (3) If you fear that you may not observe justice in the case of the orphans, then marry such women as are good to you — for justice (القسط) concerning orphans does not resemble marrying women, nor are all women orphans — this is due to what I previously mentioned regarding the omission (إسقاط) by the hypocrites (المنافقين) from the Quran. Between the discourse concerning orphans and the marriage of women, there is, in terms of speech (الخطاب) and stories (القصص), more than a third of the Quran [that was omitted]. This and similar instances are what revealed the innovations (حوادث) of the hypocrites to those who observe and reflect, and allowed the deniers (المعطلون) and the followers of religions opposing Islam a way (مساغاً) to criticize the Quran. If I were to explain to you everything that was omitted, altered (حرّف), and substituted (بُدِّل) that follows this pattern, it would be lengthy, and what al-Taqiyyah (التقية) forbids revealing concerning the virtues (مناقب) of the Awliya' (الأولياء) and the faults (مثالب) of the enemies would become manifest."

مَثْنى وَثُلاثَ وَرُباعَ two, three, or four. Two by two, three by three, and four by four, offering a choice in number for everyone, up to four.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If a man marries four women and divorces one of them, he must not marry a fifth until the waiting period (عدة) of the woman he divorced has ended. And He said: A man must not gather his water (مَاءَهُ) in five [wombs].

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Him (peace be upon him): It is not lawful for a man's water (ماء الرجل) to flow into more than four wombs belonging to free women (الحرائر).

فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تَعْدِلُوا But if you fear that you will not be able to observe justice, between these numbers, فَواحِدَةً (then [marry] one), so marry one and abandon plurality, أَوْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ (or what your right hands possess), even if they are numerous, due to the lightness of their maintenance (مئونتهن) and the absence of the obligation of equal division (القسم) between them. Al-Mut'ah (المتعة) is under their ruling (حكمهن).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in more than one narration: That they [women married by Mut'ah] are not counted among the four, nor among the seventy, and that they are in the position of slave-women (الإماء) because they are hired (مستأجرات); they are not divorced, nor do they inherit, nor are they inherited from. And that a slave (العبد) is not permitted to marry more than two free women (حرتين) or four slave-women (إماء), and he may take as many concubines (يتسرى) as he wishes with the permission of his master (مولاه).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): Jealousy (الغيرة) belongs only to men, and as for women, that [which appears as jealousy] is merely envy (حسد). And Allah is too Generous (أكرم) to afflict them with jealousy while permitting a man three [other wives] alongside her.

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): ** فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تَعْدِلُوا (But if you fear that you will not be able to observe justice) means concerning the expenditure (النفقة). As for the Almighty's saying: وَلَنْ تَسْتَطِيعُوا أَنْ تَعْدِلُوا بَيْنَ النِّساءِ وَلَوْ حَرَصْتُمْ (129) You will not be able to observe justice between women, even if you are eager to do so, it means affection (المودة).

And Al-Ayyashi narrated from Him (peace be upon him): There is extravagance (إسراف) in everything except in women. Allah the Almighty said: فَانْكِحُوا ما طابَ لَكُمْ مِنَ النِّساءِ مَثْنى وَثُلاثَ وَرُباعَ ذلِكَ أَدْنى أَلَّا تَعُولُوا (then marry such women as are good to you, two, three, or four. That is more likely to prevent you from acting unjustly). أَدْنى أَلَّا تَعُولُوا (more likely to prevent you from acting unjustly) means closer to you not inclining (تميلوا), derived from ‘āla al-mīzān (عال الميزان) when the scale inclines (مال), or [closer to you] not having to maintain [too many] (تمونوا), derived from ‘āla al-rajul ‘iyālahu (عال الرجل عياله) when the man maintains his dependents (مانهم). This is supported by the variant reading (قرائة) allā ta‘īlū (ألا تعيلوا) found in the irregular readings (الشواذ), derived from ‘āla al-rajul (عال الرجل) when his dependents become numerous. And al-Qummi [said it means]: that he does not marry what he is unable to maintain (يعول).

(4) وَآتُوا النِّساءَ صَدُقاتِهِنَ مهورهن نِحْلَةً. (4) And give the women their dowries as a gift.

Al-Qummi (may Allah have mercy upon him) said: meaning a gift (هبة). And it is said: a grant from Allah and a favor from Him upon them, or a debt (ديناً) from Allah which He legislated and made obligatory. The apparent meaning of the verse is that the address is directed to the husbands (الأزواج).

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever marries a woman and does not intend to pay her dowry (صداقها) in full, he is considered an adulterer (زان) in the sight of Allah." And the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him) said: "Indeed, the most deserving of conditions to be fulfilled are those by which you make lawful the private parts (الفروج)."

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Indeed, the address therein is directed to the guardians (الأولياء), because when a man among them married off a woman who had no guardian (أيمة), he would take her dowry (صداقها) instead of her. So Allah forbade them from doing that."

فَإِنْ طِبْنَ لَكُمْ عَنْ شَيْءٍ مِنْهُ من الصداق نَفْساً But if they remit any of it to you, being agreeable to it,

— from the dowry (من الصداق) — meaning they gifted it to you willingly (عن طيب نفس). The verb was made transitive using (عن) because it implies the meaning of relinquishing (التجاوز) and waiving (التجافي).

** فَكُلُوهُ هَنِيئاً مَرِيئاً** consume it as wholesome and palatable [food].

— meaning palatable (سائغاً) without choking (من غير غص). And sometimes a distinction is made between the two by specifying al-hani' (الهنيء) as that which a person finds pleasurable (يلذه الإِنسان), and al-mari' (المريء) as that whose consequence is praiseworthy (يحمد عاقبته). It was narrated that some people felt sinful (يتأثمون) about accepting anything from their wives of what they had given them (as dowry), so this verse was revealed.

In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi: A man came to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and said: "I am suffering from a pain in my stomach." He (peace be upon him) said: "Do you have a wife?" He replied: "Yes." He said: "Ask her to willingly grant you something from her wealth (مالها), then buy honey (عسلاً) with it, then pour upon it some water from the sky (ماء السماء), then drink it. For I heard Allah, the Glorified, say in His Book: ** وَنَزَّلْنا مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً مُبارَكاً ** (And We sent down from the sky blessed water) and He said: يَخْرُجُ مِنْ بُطُونِها شَرابٌ مُخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوانُهُ فِيهِ شِفاءٌ لِلنَّاسِ (There issues from their bellies a drink of diverse hues, in which there is a cure for the people), and He said: ** فَإِنْ طِبْنَ لَكُمْ عَنْ شَيْءٍ مِنْهُ نَفْساً فَكُلُوهُ هَنِيئاً مَرِيئاً (But if they remit any of it to you, being agreeable to it, consume it as wholesome and palatable [food]). If the blessing (البركة), the cure (الشفاء), the wholesomeness (الهنيء), and the palatability (المريء) are combined, you will be cured, if Allah the Almighty wills." So he did that and was cured.

(5) وَلا تُؤْتُوا السُّفَهاءَ أَمْوالَكُمُ الَّتِي جَعَلَ اللهُ لَكُمْ قِياماً (5) Do not give the feeble-minded your property which Allah has made a means of support for you, through which you establish yourselves and thrive. That by which establishment (القيام) occurs was named قياماً (support) for the sake of emphasis (للمبالغة). And it was recited قيماً. ** وَارْزُقُوهُمْ فِيها وَاكْسُوهُمْ (and provide for them out of it, and clothe them,) Make it (الأموال) a place for their provision (رزقهم) and clothing (كسوتهم), by obtaining from it what they need. ** وَقُولُوا لَهُمْ قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً (and speak to them honourable words.) A beautiful promise (عدة جميلة) by which their souls are pleased. And the honorable (المعروف) is that which the Sacred Law (الشرع) or the intellect (العقل) defines as good (بالحسن).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the orphans (اليتامى). Do not give them [their property] until you recognize maturity (الرشد) in them. It was asked: "How can their property be our property (أموالنا)?" He replied: "If you are their heir (الوارث)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "He whom you do not trust." And in another narration: "Everyone who drinks intoxicants (الخمر) is feeble-minded (سفيه)."

In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "Do not give it (الأموال) to the drinkers of intoxicants (شرّاب الخمر) nor to women." Then he said: "And what feeble-minded person (سفيه) is more feeble-minded than the drinker of intoxicants?"

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "The feeble-minded (السفهاء) are women and children. If a man knows that his wife is feeble-minded and corruptive (مفسدة), and his child is feeble-minded and corruptive (مفسد), it is not proper for him to give authority to either of them over his property which Allah has made for him ** قِياماً (a means of support)." He says: "A livelihood (معاشاً)." He said: وَارْزُقُوهُمْ فِيها وَاكْسُوهُمْ وَقُولُوا لَهُمْ قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً (and provide for them out of it, and clothe them, and speak to them honourable words.) The honorable (المعروف) is the promise (العدة).

(٦) ** وَابْتَلُوا الْيَتامى (Test the orphans) Test them before puberty by following up on their conditions regarding religion and their good management (حُسْن التَّصَرُّف) of wealth. ** حَتَّى إِذا بَلَغُوا النِّكاحَ (until they reach the age of marriage) they reach a limit where marriage is possible for them. فَإِنْ آنَسْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ رُشْداً فَادْفَعُوا إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوالَهُمْ (If you perceive in them maturity, hand over their property to them.)

In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Perceiving maturity (إِيْنَاس الرُّشْد) is the preservation of his wealth.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) concerning the interpretation (تَفْسِير) of this Ayah: If you see them loving the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them all), then raise them a degree.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Maturity (الرُّشْد) is intellect (الْعَقْل) and the proper management (إِصْلَاح) of wealth.

And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, said: Whoever has the wealth of some orphans in his hand, it is not permissible for him to give it to him until he reaches the age of marriage (بُلُوغ النِّكَاح) and has a wet dream (يَحْتَلِم). When he has a wet dream, the legal punishments (الْحُدُود) and the performance of obligatory duties (إِقَامَة الْفَرَائِض) become incumbent upon him, and he must not be wasteful (مُضَيِّعًا), nor a drinker of wine (شَارِب خَمْر), nor an adulterer (زَانِيًا). If maturity (الرُّشْد) is perceived in him, the wealth is handed over to him, and witnesses are called upon it. And if they do not know that he has reached puberty, then he is tested by the smell of his armpit or the growth of his pubic hair (نَبْت عَانَتِه). If that occurs, then he has reached puberty, and his wealth is handed over to him if he is mature (رَشِيدًا). It is not permissible for him to withhold his wealth from him and excuse himself by saying that he has not yet grown up. ** وَلا تَأْكُلُوها إِسْرافاً وَبِداراً أَنْ يَكْبَرُوا (Do not consume it extravagantly and hastily, lest they grow up.) being extravagant (مُسْرِفِين) and hasty (مُبَادِرِين). ** وَمَنْ كانَ غَنِيًّا فَلْيَسْتَعْفِفْ (Whoever is rich should abstain) from consuming it. ** وَمَنْ كانَ فَقِيراً فَلْيَأْكُلْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (and whoever is poor should eat reasonably) according to his need and the wage for his effort (أُجْرَة سَعْيِه).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) concerning this Ayah: Whoever manages something for the orphans while he is needy and does not have what sustains him, and he collects their wealth and manages their estate (ضَيْعَتِهِم), then let him eat in moderation (بِقَدْر) and not be extravagant. But if their estate does not distract him from what he manages for himself, then he should not diminish (يَرْزَأ) (1) their wealth at all.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The reasonable amount (الْمَعْرُوف) is sustenance (الْقُوت), and what is meant is the executor (الْوَصِيّ) or the guardian (الْقَيِّم) regarding their wealth and what benefits them.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): That is a man who restricts himself from earning a livelihood (الْمَعِيشَة), so there is no harm in him eating reasonably (بِالْمَعْرُوف) if he is managing their wealth for them. But if the wealth is small, he should not eat anything from it.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the guardian (الْقَيِّم) of orphans regarding camels (الْإِبِل) and what is permissible for him from them. He said: If he plasters (لَاط) (1) their watering trough (حَوْضَهَا), seeks their lost animals (ضَالَّتَهَا), and treats (هَنَأ) (2) their mange (جَرْبَاهَا) (3), then he may take some of their milk, provided it does not exhaust the udder (نَهْك لِضَرْع) or corrupt the offspring (فَسَاد لِنَسْل).

And in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi: what is close to it.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) concerning this Ayah: This is a man who dedicates himself to the orphan for cultivation (حَرْث) or livestock (مَاشِيَة) and occupies himself with it. ** فَلْيَأْكُلْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (then he should eat reasonably). And this is not permissible for him regarding the dirhams (الدَّرَاهِم) and dinars (الدَّنَانِير) that are deposited with him.

And in another narration from him (peace be upon him), he said: My father used to say: It is abrogated (مَنْسُوخَة).

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever is poor should take from the orphan's wealth the amount of need and sufficiency (الْكِفَايَة) by way of a loan (قَرْض), and then return what he took when he finds the means. ** فَإِذا دَفَعْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوالَهُمْ فَأَشْهِدُوا عَلَيْهِمْ (When you hand over their property to them, take witnesses over them) that they have received it, for this negates suspicion (نَفْي لِلتُّهْمَة), distances from dispute (الْخُصُومَة), and the necessity of guarantee (وُجُوب الضَّمَان). ** وَكَفى بِاللهِ حَسِيباً (Allah suffices as reckoner.) as a reckoner (مُحَاسِبًا).

(1) In the Hadith: "I do not diminish (أَرْزَأ) a single dirham from your spoils (فَيْئِكُمْ)," meaning: I do not decrease anything, not even a dirham (دِرْهَمًا) (Majma').

(٧) لِلرِّجالِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوالِدانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ وَلِلنِّساءِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوالِدانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ (7) For men there is a share in what the parents and the relatives leave, and for women there is a share in what the parents and the relatives leave, meaning by them those who inherit through kinship (القرابة). ** مِمَّا قَلَّ مِنْهُ أَوْ كَثُرَ be it little or much, from its little and its much, a prescribed (مَفْرُوضاً) and obligatory (واجباً) share. It was said that the Arabs during the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) used to grant inheritance to males without females. So Allah, glory be to Him, refuted them and said that for each of the two groups there is a portion (سهم) and a share (حظ).

(1) Everything that adheres to something has لاط به (lāṭa bihi), يلوط (yalūṭu) لوطاً (lawṭan), and يليط (yalīṭu) ليطاً (layṭan). The origin of اللوط (al-lawṭ) is adherence (اللصوق). Until he said: And I plastered (لطت) the basin (الحوض) with mud (بالطين) لوطاً (lawṭan), meaning I smeared it (ملطته) and plastered it (طينته). (مجمع)

(2) ها هناه (hā hanāhu) يهنأ (yahna'u) and يهنؤه (yahna'uhu) means He fed him (أطعمه) and gave him (أعطاه), like أهنأه (ahna'ahu). And the camels (الإِبل) He applies يهنأها (yahna'uhā) (with the نون (nūn) having three possible vowelizations) means He smeared them (طلاها) with الهناء (al-hināʾ), like the word كتاب (kitāb), which is tar (القطران). (مجمع)

(3) الجرب (al-jarab) (with the second letter having a vowel (بالتحريك)) is a well-known disease (داء معروف). It is said: The camel (البعير) suffered from mange (جرب) جرباً (jaraban) (following the pattern of تعب (taʿiba)), so it is أجرب (ajrab), and a female camel is جرباء (jarbāʾ). (مجمع)

(٨) وَإِذا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ meaning, the division of the inheritance ** أُولُوا الْقُرْبى those who do not inherit ** وَالْيَتامى وَالْمَساكِينُ فَارْزُقُوهُمْ مِنْهُ (8) (If, when the division is made, the relatives, the orphans and the needy are present, provide for them out of it,) so give them something from the divided property, to soothe their hearts and as charity upon them ** وَقُولُوا لَهُمْ قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً (and speak to them honourable words.) Be gentle with them in speech, apologize to them, consider what you give them small, and do not remind them of the favor (لا تمنوا بذلك عليهم).

And al-Qummi said: It is abrogated by His saying: ** يُوصِيكُمُ اللهُ** (Allah enjoins you).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them): The verse of the fixed shares (آية الفرائض) abrogated it.

And in a narration from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked whether it was abrogated, and He said no, "If they attend you, give them."

I say: The abrogation of obligation (الوجوب) does not negate the continuation of permissibility (الجواز) and recommendation (الاستحباب). A similar instance has passed in Surat al-Baqarah.

(٩) وَلْيَخْشَ الَّذِينَ لَوْ تَرَكُوا مِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّةً ضِعافاً خافُوا عَلَيْهِمْ (9) Let those fear who, if they leave behind a weak progeny, would be afraid for them.

It is a command that they should fear Allah and be mindful of Him (يَتَّقُوهُ) concerning the matter of the orphans, so they should do unto them what they would love to be done unto their own weak progeny after their death.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever oppresses an orphan, Allah will empower over him someone who will oppress him, or over his offspring (عَقِبِهِ), or over the offspring of his offspring (عَقِبِ عَقِبِهِ). Then He recited this Ayah: فَلْيَتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (So let them be mindful of Allah) concerning the matter of the orphans ** وَلْيَقُولُوا (and let them speak) to them ** قَوْلاً سَدِيداً (a straight word), like what they say to their own children, with compassion (الشَّفَقَة) and good manners (حُسْنِ الأَدَب).

** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوالَ الْيَتامى ظُلْماً إِنَّما يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ ناراً وَسَيَصْلَوْنَ سَعِيراً** (10) Indeed those who consume the property of orphans unjustly, they only consume fire in their bellies, and they will enter a blaze.

** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوالَ الْيَتامى ظُلْماً إِنَّما يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ (Indeed those who consume the property of orphans unjustly, they only consume in their bellies) means the filling of their bellies ** ناراً (fire), meaning that which leads to the Fire. ** وَسَيَصْلَوْنَ سَعِيراً (and they will enter a blaze) means they will enter a Fire, and what a Fire it is. And it was recited with a dammah on the yā’ (يُصْلَوْنَ). And صَلْيُ the Fire is enduring its heat. And صَلَيْتُهُ means I roasted him. And الإِصْلاءُ is casting into it. And سَعْرُ the Fire is igniting it.

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, the consumption of the orphan's property will bring its consequence (وَبَال) upon him in this world and the Hereafter. As for this world, Allah says: ** وَلْيَخْشَ الَّذِينَ (the Ayah). And as for the Hereafter, Allah says: ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ (the Ayah).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "When I was taken on the Night Journey (أُسْرِيَ بِي) to the heaven, I saw a people into whose bellies the Fire was being cast, and it was exiting from their rear ends (أَدْبَارِهِمْ). So I asked: 'Who are these, O Gabriel (يَا جِبْرَئِيل)?' He replied: 'These are ** الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوالَ الْيَتامى ظُلْماً** (those who consume the property of orphans unjustly).'"

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the consumer of the orphan's property will come on the Day of Resurrection while the Fire is blazing in his belly, until the flame of the Fire exits from his mouth. The people of the gathering (أَهْلُ الْجَمْعِ) will recognize him as the consumer of the orphan's property.

(١١) يُوصِيكُمُ اللهُ (Allah enjoins you) He commands you, entrusts you, and makes obligatory upon you ** فِي أَوْلادِكُمْ (concerning your children) regarding the matter of their inheritance. ** لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ (for the male shall be the share of two females) if the two categories (males and females) are present together. The reason (العلة) for this is what is narrated in al-Kafi (الكافي) from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): that they (the females) return as dependents (عيالاً) upon them (the males).

And in al-Faqih (الفقيه), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): because of what Allah has assigned for her from the dowry (الصداق).

And in what is narrated from him (peace be upon him): because there is no jihad (جهاد) upon her, nor maintenance (نفقة), nor blood-money (معقلة) (1). And others were enumerated in al-Kafi and al-Faqih from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). ** فَإِنْ كُنَّ نِساءً (But if they are women) and there is no male with them, ** فَوْقَ اثْنَتَيْنِ فَلَهُنَّ ثُلُثا ما تَرَكَ (more than two, they shall have two-thirds of what he leaves) which the deceased among you left. And if she is only one, she shall have the half. ** وَلِأَبَوَيْهِ (And for his parents)—meaning the parents of the deceased— ** لِكُلِّ واحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا السُّدُسُ مِمَّا تَرَكَ إِنْ كانَ لَهُ وَلَدٌ (for each of the two shall be a sixth of what he leaves, if he has children) whether male or female, one or more. ** فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ وَلَدٌ وَوَرِثَهُ أَبَواهُ فَلِأُمِّهِ الثُّلُثُ (But if he has no children, and his parents inherit from him, his mother shall have a third) of what he leaves. ** فَإِنْ كانَ لَهُ إِخْوَةٌ فَلِأُمِّهِ السُّدُسُ (But if he has brothers, his mother shall have a sixth). And it was recited fal-i-ummihi (فلإِمه) with a kasrah (كسر) on the hamzah (الهمزة), following what precedes it. The term al-ikhwah (الاخوة) applies to two or more, and two sisters (الأختان) are equivalent to one brother (أخ واحد).

For this reason, it is narrated in al-Kafi, al-Tahdhib (التهذيب), and others, in more than one narration from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that nothing screens the mother (الام) from the third except two brothers (اخوان), or one brother and two sisters (أخ واختان), or four sisters (أربع أخوات). And it is narrated: that brothers from the mother only (الاخوة من الام فقط) do not screen the mother from the third, and that brothers and sisters (الاخوة والأخوات) do not inherit alongside the parents (الأبوين). And the reason (الوجه) for this is: that the father spends upon them, so his share is made abundant. مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصِي بِها أَوْ دَيْنٍ (after [the fulfillment of] any bequest he may have made or any debt). Meaning that these shares (الأنصباء) are distributed after the two matters (bequest and debt), if they exist. And it was recited in the passive voice (على البناء للمفعول). The word "or" (أو) does not necessitate sequence (الترتيب).

And in al-Majma' (المجمع), narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "You read the bequest (الوصية) before the debt (الدين) in this verse, yet the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ruled that the debt precedes the bequest." It was said: The bequest was placed before the debt, while it is subsequent in ruling (في الحكم), because it resembles the inheritance (الميراث) and is burdensome upon the heirs (الورثة). آباؤُكُمْ وَأَبْناؤُكُمْ لا تَدْرُونَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ لَكُمْ نَفْعاً (Your fathers and your sons—you do not know which of them is nearer to you in benefit). Meaning, you do not know who is more beneficial to you among your ancestors (أصولكم) and descendants (فروعكم), in your immediate life (عاجلكم) and your afterlife (آجلكم), among those who inherit from you and those whom you inherit from: Is it the one among them who made a bequest (أوصى), thereby exposing you to reward (الثواب) by executing his bequest? Or is it the one who did not make a bequest, thereby preserving His wealth for you? Or is it the one to whom you made a bequest, thereby preserving wealth for him? Or is it the one to whom you did not make a bequest, thereby depriving him? Therefore, seek concerning them what Allah has enjoined upon you, and do not transgress by altering the bequest (تبديل الوصية), or preferring some and depriving others. This is an emphatic interjection (اعتراض مؤكد) regarding the matter of division (القسمة) and the execution of the bequest (تنفيذ الوصية). ** فَرِيضَةً مِنَ اللهِ (a divine ordinance from Allah), an emphatic verbal noun (مصدر مؤكد). ** إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلِيماً (Indeed Allah is all-knowing) of the interests (المصالح) and ranks (الرتب), ** حَكِيماً** (all-wise) in what He decreed and ordained.

footnote (١) *‘Aqala* (*عقل*) like *fariha* (*فرح*). And *ta‘āqalū* (*تعاقلوا*) the blood of so-and-so: they paid the blood-money (*عقلوه*) among themselves. And his blood is *ma‘qulah* (*معقلة*)—with a *ḍammah* (*ضم*) on the *qāf* (*القاف*)—upon his people, a fine (*غرم*) imposed upon them. And *al-ma‘qalah* (*المعقلة*) is the blood-money (*الدية*) itself (*Qāmus* - *ق*).

(١٢) ** وَلَكُمْ نِصْفُ ما تَرَكَ أَزْواجُكُمْ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُنَّ وَلَدٌ فَإِنْ كانَ لَهُنَّ وَلَدٌ فَلَكُمُ الرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْنَ (12) And for you is half of what your wives leave, if they have no child. But if they have a child, then for you is a quarter of what they leave, meaning, an inheriting child, whether from her womb, or from the loins of her sons, or the wombs of her daughters, however low they descend, male or female, whether from you or from others. ** مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصِينَ بِها أَوْ دَيْنٍ وَلَهُنَّ الرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُمْ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَكُمْ وَلَدٌ فَإِنْ كانَ لَكُمْ وَلَدٌ فَلَهُنَّ الثُّمُنُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ تُوصُونَ بِها أَوْ دَيْنٍ after [payment of] any bequest they may have made or any debt. And for them is a quarter of what you leave, if you have no child. But if you have a child, then for them is an eighth of what you leave, after [payment of] any bequest you may have made or any debt. The share decreed for the man by virtue of marriage is double that of the woman, just as in lineage (النسب), and the reason (العلة) here is the same reason (العلة) there. The single wife and the plural number of wives are equal in the quarter and the eighth. وَإِنْ كانَ رَجُلٌ يُورَثُ كَلالَةً (1) There are grammatical aspects (وجوه من الإِعراب) to this statement, by which the ruling does not change. The Kalālah (الكلالة) is the kinship (القرابة), and it is applied to the heir (الوارث) and the inherited (الموروث). It was interpreted in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), as: "He who is neither a child nor a parent," meaning the relative from the horizontal direction (جهة العرض), not the vertical (الطول). What is meant by it here are the brothers and sisters from the mother specifically, and in the other Ayah, those from the father and mother, or the father only, as narrated from the Infallibles (peace be upon them). ** أَوِ امْرَأَةٌ ** or a woman, likewise. وَلَهُ and he has meaning, and for each one of them (the man or the woman). And it is said: meaning, and for the man, His ruling suffices for the ruling concerning the woman, due to the conjunction (العطف) indicating their shared participation in it. ** أَخٌ أَوْ أُخْتٌ a brother or a sister, meaning, from the mother. ** فَلِكُلِّ واحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا السُّدُسُ فَإِنْ كانُوا أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذلِكَ فَهُمْ شُرَكاءُ فِي الثُّلُثِ then for each of the two is a sixth; but if they are more than that, they shall share in a third, He made the male and the female equal here because the relationship (الانتساب) is purely through the female line (بمحض الانوثة). ** مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصى بِها أَوْ دَيْنٍ after [payment of] any bequest that may have been made or any debt, And it was recited with the passive voice (على البناء للمفعول). without prejudice (غَيْرَ مُضَارٍّ) to his heirs, by exceeding the third (in bequest), or by intending to harm them rather than the kinship (القرابة), or by acknowledging a debt that is not binding upon him. وَصِيَّةً مِنَ اللهِ وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ a charge from Allah, and Allah is All-knowing, of the one who causes prejudice (بالمضار) and others. ** حَلِيمٌ All-forbearing. He does not hasten His punishment.

(1) The Almighty's saying: ** وَإِنْ كانَ رَجُلٌ يُورَثُ كَلالَةً (And if a man is inherited from, [being] kalālah) Regarding كان (kāna), there are two views (وجهان). The first is that it is a complete verb (تامّة), and رجل (rajul) is its subject (فاعلها), and يورث (yūrath) is an adjective (صفة) for it, and كلالة (kalālah) is an adverbial qualifier (حال) derived from the pronoun in يورث. According to this, الكلالة (al-Kalālah) is the name for the deceased who left neither a child nor a parent. If كلالة (kalālah) were recited in the nominative case (بالرفع) as an adjective (صفة) or an appositive (بدل) for the pronoun in يورث, it would be permissible, except that I do not know of anyone who recited it that way, so one should only recite what has been transmitted. The second view is that كان (kāna) is the incomplete verb (الناقصة), and رجل (rajul) is its subject (اسمها), and يورث (yūrath) is its predicate (خبرها), and كلالة (kalālah) is also an adverbial qualifier (حال). And it is said that الكلالة (al-Kalālah) is the name for the inherited property (المال الموروث). Based on this, كلالة (kalālah) is in the accusative case (ينتصب) as the second object (المفعول الثاني) of يورث, just as you say: "Zayd inherited property (ورث زيد مالاً)." And it is said that الكلالة (al-Kalālah) is the name for the heirs (الورثة) who have neither a child nor a parent among them. Based on this, there is no justification (لا وجه) for this statement according to the famous recitation (القراءة المشهورة), because it has no governing factor (ناصب) for the accusative case. Do you not see that if you said: "Zayd is inherited by his brother (زيد يورث أخوه)," it would not be correct? Rather, it is only correct according to the recitation of those who recited it with a kasrah on the rā’ (بكسر الراء), whether lightened (مخففة) or doubled (مثقلة), and both have been recited. And it is said that this doctrine (المذهب) is correct based on the estimation of a deleted annex (حذف مضاف), the estimation of which is: "And if a man is inherited from, [being] the possessor of kalālah (وإن كان رجل يورث ذا كلالة)," then ذا (dhā) is an adverbial qualifier (حال) or the predicate (خبر) of كان (kāna). And whoever recited with a kasrah on the rā’ made كلالة (kalālah) the object (مفعولاً به), whether it refers to the heirs (الورثة) or the property (المال). In both cases, one of the two objects (المفعولين) is deleted, and the estimation is: "He inherits his family property (يورث أهله مالاً)." (Suyūṭī)

(13) ** تِلْكَ (Those) is a reference to the rulings that preceded concerning the matter of the orphans, the bequests (الوصايا), and the inheritances (المواريث). ** حُدُودُ اللهِ (are Allah’s bounds), meaning His defined laws (شرائعه المحدودة) which it is not permissible to transgress. وَمَنْ يُطِعِ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها وَذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ. (Whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle, He will admit him into gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them forever, and that is the great success.)

(١٤) ** وَمَنْ يَعْصِ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَيَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَهُ يُدْخِلْهُ ناراً (But whoever disobeys Allah and His Apostle and transgresses His bounds, He will admit him into a Fire) And it was recited (وقرئ) as ندخله (We will admit him) with the nūn (بالنون), خَالِداً فِيهَا وَلَهُ عَذَابٌ مُهِينٌ** (to abide in it forever, and there is a humiliating punishment for him).

The singularization (توحيد) of the pronoun in ** يُدْخِلْهُ (1) and the pluralization (جمع) of خالِدِينَ is due to the word (اللفظ) and the meaning (المعنى).

If it is said that Allah, glory be to Him and exalted is He, did not clarify the ruling concerning the two daughters (البنتين) in the obligatory shares (الفرائض), nor the ruling concerning the obligatory shares if the estate (التركة) falls short of the shares (السهام) or exceeds them, we say: There is no harm (لا ضير), for the People of the House (أهل البيت) have clarified all of that in the best manner, and the rightful group (الطائفة المحقة) has agreed upon what they heard from them, without any disagreement among themselves, because it conforms to the requirement of sound intellects (العقول السليمة). This is just as in the case of the rest of the concise (المجملة) Quranic verses, for only those firmly grounded in knowledge (الراسخون في العلم) among them interpret them, and neither of the two weighty things (الثقلين) acts independently of the other. As for the ruling concerning the sons (البنين), these verses have drawn attention to it, and it has been established from them through narrations (الروايات) without disagreement.

He said in al-Kafi: People have discussed the matter of the two daughters (البنتين): from where were the two-thirds (الثلثان) assigned to them, since Allah, the Exalted, only assigned the two-thirds to what is more than two? So, some people said it was by consensus (إجماع), and some people said it was by analogy (قياساً), arguing that just as the single daughter receives half (النصف), this is evidence that what is more than one receives two-thirds (الثلثين). And some people said it was by imitation (التقليد) and narration (الرواية). None of them hit upon the correct aspect (الوجه) regarding this. So we said: Allah made the share (حظ) of the two females (الأنثيين) two-thirds (الثلثين) by His saying: ** لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ (for the male shall be the like of the share of two females). This is because if a man leaves a daughter and a son, the male receives the like of the share of the two females, which is two-thirds (الثلثان). Thus, the share of the two females is two-thirds (الثلثان). He sufficed with this clarification, that the two females (الأنثيين) are mentioned with the two-thirds (بالثلثين). This is a clarification that all of them were ignorant of, and abundant praise belongs to Allah. His speech ends here.

As for when the estate (التركة) falls short of the shares (السهام), the shortfall (النقص) according to us falls only upon the daughters (البنات) and the sisters (الأخوات). This is because each of the two parents (الأبوين) and the two spouses (الزوجين) has two shares (سهمان), a higher and a lower, whereas the daughter, the two daughters, and the two sisters, were it not for what we have stated, would only have one share (سهم واحد). Thus, when the shortfall affects them (the daughters/sisters), those entitled to shares (ذوو السهام) become equal in that matter. This has been clarified in their narrations (اخبارهم). The opponents (المخالفون) speak of al-ʿawl (العول) (1) regarding this, causing the shortfall to fall upon everyone in proportion to their shares, by analogy (قياساً) with an estate that does not cover debts (الديون), and based on a ruling (قضية) attributed to ʿUmar (عُمَرية) and another similar ruling attributed to ʿAlī (عَلَوية). Their analogy (قياس), despite its invalidity, involves a difference (مع الفارق). And their ʿUmar’s ruling (عُمَرُهم) stemmed from an innovation (بدعة) that is undeniable, despite Ibn ʿAbbās’s denial of it, even if the denial did not appear until after him, with the excuse that he (ʿUmar) was a man of awe (مهيباً). And the interpretation (تأويل) of the ambiguous (المتشابه) rests with the one who brought it, not with those in whose hearts is deviation (زيغ), especially since the narration (الرواية) is not established, and its opposite is narrated frequently (تواتر خلافها) from him (peace be upon him). This is besides the contradiction (التناقض) and impossibility (المحال) inherent in al-ʿawl (العول), as clarified by our Imams (peace be upon them) and detailed by our companions (أصحابنا). And Fadl ibn Shādhān (may Allah have mercy on him) has statements regarding this chapter, which he presented accurately in al-Tahdhīb.

As for when the estate (التركة) exceeds the shares (السهام), the surplus (الزائد) is only added to those upon whom the shortfall would fall if the estate were deficient, as they (peace be upon them) clarified and upon which our companions (أصحابنا) agreed. The opponents (المخالفون) speak of al-taʿṣīb (التعصيب) (2), giving the surplus (الفاضل) to the male ʿaṣabah (عصبة) (agnates) and not giving the female anything, even if she is closer to the deceased in lineage (النسب) than him. They rely on the story of Zachariah (زكريا), where he did not ask for a female heir, due to his knowledge that she would not inherit alongside the ʿaṣabah (العصبة). Thus were they deluded (يؤفكون). I wish I knew what informed them that he did not ask for a female heir, when what motivated him to ask was the guardianship (كفالة) of Maryam (مريم) and what he saw of her honor (كرامتها). Furthermore, what prevents Him from having intended the gender (الجنس) encompassing both male and female? He only desired the male because he is more beloved to human nature (طباع البشر). He requested him for both inheritance (الإرث) and undertaking the burdens of prophethood (أعباء النبوّة) simultaneously, and there is no doubt that this is not conceivable for women. Or perhaps his law (شرعه) regarding inheritance was contrary to our law (شرعنا). They also relied on a weak narration (رواية ضعيفة) which their higher narrators (رواتها الأعلى) transmitted after hearing it conveyed from the lower narrators (الأدنى). Some rejected it based on the clear verses of the Book (محكمات الكتاب), and another said: "By Allah, I did not narrate this; rather, Satan (الشيطان) cast it upon their tongues." Moreover, they narrated from Zayd ibn Thābit that he said: "It is from the rulings (قضاء) of the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) that men inherit to the exclusion of women."

(1) His saying: The singularization (توحيد) of the pronoun in يُدْخِلْهُ (end of quote). The singular form (الإفراد) is based on the word (اللفظ), and the plural form (الجمع) is based on consideration of the meaning (المعنى). The pluralization of the inhabitants of Paradise (أصحاب الجنة) and the singularization of the inhabitants of the Fire (أصحاب النار) suggests their harmony (ايتلافهم) and their familiarity (استيناس) with one another in the ranks of Paradise, due to the contentment of their hearts and their lack of preoccupation with themselves. This is contrary to the inhabitants of the Fire, for each one of them on that Day will have a concern (شأناً) of punishment that preoccupies him with himself.

(1) From their saying: ʿāla (عال) in judgment, meaning he inclined (مال) and acted unjustly (جار). And in the Ḥadīth: "He Who counted the sands of ʿĀlij knows that the shares (السهام) do not suffer al-ʿawl (تعول)." And in it: "The first person to apply al-ʿawl (أعال) to the obligatory shares (الفرائض) was ʿUmar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb." Al-ʿawl (العول) is an expression for the estate (التركة) falling short of the shares of those entitled to fixed shares (ذوي الفروض), and it only falls short with the inclusion of the husband or the wife. In religious law (الشرع), it is the opposite of al-taʿṣīb (التعصيب), which is the inheritance of the ʿaṣabah (العصبة) (agnates) of what remains after the fixed shares (ذوي السهام) have been distributed. (Majmaʿ)

(2) The ʿuṣbah (عصبة) of a man, with the movement (بالتحريك), is the plural of ʿāṣib (عاصب), like kafarah (كفرة) is the plural of kāfir (كافر). They are his sons and his relatives through his father. The plural is al-ʿiṣāb (العصاب). Al-Jawharī said: They were only named ʿaṣabah (عصبة) because they ʿaṣabū (عصبوا) him, meaning they surrounded him. So the father is one side, the son is one side, the brother is one flank, and the paternal uncle is one flank. From this comes al-taʿṣīb (التعصيب), which is invalid (باطل) according to us. (Majmaʿ)

(15) وَاللَّاتِي يَأْتِينَ الْفاحِشَةَ مِنْ نِسائِكُمْ (As for those of your women who commit the indecency) Meaning: who commit it. It is said that the indecency (الْفَاحِشَةَ) is fornication (الزنا). It was named thus due to the increase in its ugliness (قُبْحِهَا) and heinousness (شَنَاعَتِهَا). ** فَاسْتَشْهِدُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ أَرْبَعَةً مِنْكُمْ (bring against them four witnesses from among yourselves) Meaning: Request from those who accuse them four believing men (الرِّجَالِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ) to bear witness against them. ** فَإِنْ شَهِدُوا فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ فِي الْبُيُوتِ (If they testify, confine them to the houses) Meaning: Imprison them therein. ** حَتَّى يَتَوَفَّاهُنَّ الْمَوْتُ أَوْ يَجْعَلَ اللهُ لَهُنَّ سَبِيلاً (until death takes them away or Allah appoints a way for them.) This verse and the one following it are abrogated (مَنْسُوخَتَانِ) by the verse concerning the fornicating woman and the fornicating man (آيَةِ الزَّانِيَةِ وَالزَّانِي).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: And Surat al-Nur was revealed after Surat al-Nisa'. The confirmation of this is that Allah, the Exalted, revealed to Him in Surat al-Nisa' ** وَاللَّاتِي يَأْتِينَ الْفاحِشَةَ ** (As for those of your women who commit the indecency) [the rest of] the verse. And the way (السبيل) that Allah mentioned is ** سُورَةٌ أَنْزَلْناها وَفَرَضْناها ** (A surah which We have sent down and which We have enjoined) up to His saying ** طائِفَةٌ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ.** (a group of the faithful.)

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is abrogated (مَنْسُوخَةٌ), and the way (السبيل) is the prescribed punishments (الْحُدُود).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse ** وَاللَّاتِي يَأْتِينَ الْفاحِشَةَ (As for those of your women who commit the indecency). He said: "This is abrogated (مَنْسُوخَةٌ)." He was asked: "How was it [applied]?" He said: "When a woman committed fornication (فَجَرَتْ) and four witnesses stood against her, she was confined to a house, and she was not allowed to speak (تُحَدَّثْ), nor to be spoken to (تُكَلَّمْ), nor to sit with others (تُجَالَسْ). She was provided with her food and drink until she died, ** أَوْ يَجْعَلَ اللهُ لَهُنَّ سَبِيلاً (or Allah appoints a way for them)." He said: "The way (السبيل) that was appointed is lashing (الجلد) and stoning (الرجم)."

And in al-Ghawali, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Take [the ruling] from me. Allah has appointed a way (سَبِيلاً) for them: The virgin (الْبِكْر) with the virgin: one hundred lashes (جَلْدُ مِائَة) and banishment (تَغْرِيب) for a year. And the previously married person (الثَّيِّب) with the previously married person: one hundred lashes and stoning (الرجم)."

(١٦) وَالَّذانِ يَأْتِيانِها مِنْكُمْ فَآذُوهُما فَإِنْ تابا وَأَصْلَحا فَأَعْرِضُوا عَنْهُما إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ تَوَّاباً رَحِيماً. (16) And those of you who commit it, punish them. But if they repent and reform, let them alone. Indeed Allah is All-clement, All-merciful.

Al-Qummi said: In the Jahiliyyah (الجاهلية), if a man committed zina (زنا), he would be harmed, and the woman would be confined in a house until she died. Then that was abrogated (نسخ) by His Almighty saying: الزَّانِيَةُ وَالزَّانِي فَاجْلِدُوا (The fornicating woman and the fornicating man, flog them) the Ayah. (End of quote). And it was said that the first Ayah concerns lesbianism (السحاقات), and this one concerns sodomites (اللواطين), and الزَّانِيَةُ وَالزَّانِي (The fornicating woman and the fornicating man) concerns those who commit zina (الزناة). But this is not confirmed from the People of the House (أهل البيت) عليهم السلام.

(١٧) إِنَّمَا التَّوْبَةُ عَلَى اللهِ (17) Repentance is incumbent upon Allah meaning the acceptance of repentance which Allah has made incumbent upon Himself according to His promise to whomever repents to Him, if He accepts his repentance. However, this (على) is not the same as the (على) in their saying (تاب عليه) [He turned to him/forgave him]. The verification of the meaning of repentance has already passed during the exegesis of the Almighty's saying: فَتَلَقَّى آدَمُ مِنْ رَبِّهِ كَلِماتٍ فَتابَ عَلَيْهِ لِلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ السُّوءَ بِجَهالَةٍ Then Adam received certain words from his Lord, and He turned to him. (17) for those who commit evil out of ignorance, being characterized by it (سفهاً) (foolishness), for the commission of sin (الذنب) and disobedience (المعصية) is foolishness (سفه) and willful ignorance (تجاهل).

In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Every sin committed by the servant, even if he is knowledgeable (عالماً), he is ignorant (جاهل) when he risks his soul in the disobedience of his Lord. For Allah, the Glorified, related the saying of Joseph to his brothers: هَلْ عَلِمْتُمْ ما فَعَلْتُمْ بِيُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ إِذْ أَنْتُمْ جاهِلُونَ Did you know what you did to Joseph and his brother when you were ignorant? Thus, He attributed ignorance (الجهل) to them because they risked their souls in the disobedience of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ).

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "What if he returns [to sin] and repents repeatedly?" He said: "Allah will forgive him." It was asked: "Until when?" He said: "Until Satan is the one who is exhausted (المحسور) (1)." ثُمَّ يَتُوبُونَ مِنْ قَرِيبٍ Then they repent soon. It was asked: Meaning, before its love (حبّه) is drunk into their hearts, sealing them, making return difficult for them? Or before the presence of death, due to His Almighty saying: حَتَّى إِذا حَضَرَ أَحَدَهُمُ الْمَوْتُ until when death approaches one of them? He called it "soon" (قريباً) because the span of life is short, just as He, the Glorified, said: قُلْ مَتاعُ الدُّنْيا قَلِيلٌ Say: 'The enjoyment of the world is little.'

I say: The second interpretation is far from the apparent meaning of the wording, and furthermore, the Ayah does not indicate it, due to the permissibility of remaining silent regarding the third category, as often occurs in similar ambiguous (مجملات) parts of the Quran. As for the restriction (الحصر) indicated by the word (انما), it does not contradict the forthcoming narrations, because the obligation of acceptance is different from the bestowal of favor (التفضل به).

In al-Faqih, the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his Household) said in the last sermon He delivered: "Whoever repents a year before his death, Allah turns to him [in forgiveness]." Then He said: "And a year is indeed long." "And whoever repents a month before his death, Allah turns to him." Then He said: "And a month is indeed long." "And whoever repents a day before his death, Allah turns to him." Then He said: "And a day is indeed long." "And whoever repents an hour before his death, Allah turns to him." Then He said: "And an hour is indeed long. Whoever repents when his soul reaches this"—and He pointed with His hand to His throat—"Allah turns to him."

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi is what is close to this, and it also mentioned the Friday (الجمعة). And He said at the end of it: "Whoever repents before he witnesses [the unseen], Allah, the Almighty, accepts his repentance." And in a narration of the general public (العامّة): "Whoever repents before it (بها) rattles [in his throat], Allah turns to him."

And in a narration: When Iblis descended, he said: "By Your Might and Your Grandeur, I shall not leave the son of Adam until his soul leaves his body." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), the Glorified, said: "By My Might and My Grandeur, I shall not withhold repentance from My servant until it rattles [in his throat]."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "When the soul reaches here"—and He pointed with His hand to His throat—"there is no repentance for the knowledgeable person (للعالم)." Then He recited this Ayah. And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), is the like of it, and He added: "But there is repentance for the ignorant person (للجاهل)."

I say: Perhaps the reason for the knowledgeable person (العالم) not having repentance at that time is the onset of his despair of life due to the signs (أمارات) of death, unlike the ignorant person (الجاهل), who does not despair until he witnesses the unseen (الغيب). It is said: And part of Allah, the Almighty's, kindness (لطف) toward His servants is that He commanded the Seizer of Souls (قابض الأرواح) to begin extracting them from the toes of the feet, then ascending gradually until it reaches the chest, and then ending at the throat, so that during this respite, the person may be able to turn his heart toward Allah, the Almighty, make a will (الوصية), repent (as long as he has not witnessed [the unseen]), seek absolution (الاستحلال), and remember Allah. Then his soul is extracted while the remembrance of Allah is upon his tongue, thereby hoping for a good conclusion (حسن خاتمته). May Allah grant us that by His favor. فَأُولئِكَ يَتُوبُ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ it is such whose repentance Allah accepts. This is a promise to fulfill what He promised and what He made incumbent upon Himself regarding the acceptance of repentance. وَكانَ اللهُ عَلِيماً and Allah is All-knowing, knowing their sincerity (إخلاصهم) in repentance, حَكِيماً All-wise, not punishing the penitent.

footnote (١) (*حسر بصره يحسر حسوراً*) means his sight became weary (*كل*) and his gaze was cut off due to a long distance or similar things, so he is (*حسر*) and also (*محسور*) (*صحاح*). What is meant by (*المحسور*) in the narration is the one characterized by weariness (*الكلالة*), exhaustion (*الإعياء*), boredom (*الملالة*), and fatigue (*التعب*). That is, there is no limit to the acceptance of repentance unless Satan becomes weary, so he does not deceive him, and thus he does not disobey Allah, requiring a new repentance.

(١٨) وَلَيْسَتِ التَّوْبَةُ لِلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ السَّيِّئاتِ حَتَّى إِذا حَضَرَ أَحَدَهُمُ الْمَوْتُ قالَ إِنِّي تُبْتُ الْآنَ. (18) But repentance is not for those who commit misdeeds until, when death approaches one of them, he says, 'Indeed I repent now,'

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "That is when he witnesses the matter of the Hereafter." ** وَلَا الَّذِينَ يَمُوتُونَ وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ ** He (Allah) equated between those who delayed repentance until the presence of death, whether they were wicked (الفسقة) or disbelievers (الكفّار), and between those who died upon disbelief (الكفر), in denying repentance, in order to emphasize the lack of consideration for it in that state. And it is as if He said: the repentance of these and the non-repentance of those are the same. And it was said: What is meant by "those who commit evil (السوء)" are the sinful believers (عصاة المؤمنين), and by "those who commit misdeeds (السيئات)" are meant the hypocrites (المنافقون), due to the multiplication of their disbelief (كفرهم) and the evil of their deeds, and by "those who die" are meant the disbelievers (الكفّار). ** أُولئِكَ أَعْتَدْنا We prepared (هيّأنا) ** لَهُمْ عَذاباً أَلِيماً This is an affirmation of the non-acceptance of their repentance, due to the preparation of their punishment, and that He will punish them whenever He wills.

(١٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَنْ تَرِثُوا النِّساءَ كَرْهاً (19) O you who have faith! It is not lawful for you to inherit women against their will. And it was recited with a dammah (بالضم).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): In the Era of Ignorance (الجاهلية), at the beginning of their acceptance of Islam among the Arab tribes, if a man's close relative (حميم) died and he had a wife, the man would cast his garment over her, thereby inheriting her marriage (نكاحها) with the dowry (صداق) of his close relative who had given it to her. He would inherit her marriage just as he inherited his wealth. When Abu Qays ibn al-Aslat died, Muhsin ibn Abi Qays cast his garment over his father's wife, who was Kubayshah bint Ma'mar ibn Ma'bad. He thus inherited her marriage, then he left her, neither entering upon her nor spending upon her. So she came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, Abu Qays ibn al-Aslat died, and his son Muhsin inherited my marriage. He neither enters upon me nor spends upon me, nor does he free my way (يخلي سبيلي) so that I may join my family." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Return to your house, and if Allah brings about something concerning your affair, I will inform you of it." Then the following was revealed: وَلا تَنْكِحُوا ما نَكَحَ آباؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّساءِ إِلَّا ما قَدْ سَلَفَ إِنَّهُ كانَ فاحِشَةً وَمَقْتاً وَساءَ سَبِيلاً (And do not marry women whom your fathers have married, except what is already past. Indeed, that is an indecency and an odium, and a wicked way.) So she joined her family.
