There were women in Medina whose marriages had been inherited, just as the marriage of Kubayshah was inherited, except that those who inherited them were not the sons. So He revealed: ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَنْ تَرِثُوا النِّساءَ كَرْهاً** (O you who have faith! It is not lawful for you to inherit women against their will.)

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah. He said: "It concerns the man who has an orphan girl (اليتيمة) under his guardianship (في حجره). He prevents her from marrying, harming her, especially if she is a relative of his."

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the man who keeps the woman confined with him, having no need for her, and waits for her death so that he may inherit her. وَلا تَعْضُلُوهُنَ (and do not restrain them) And do not confine them in order to harm them, ** لِتَذْهَبُوا بِبَعْضِ ما آتَيْتُمُوهُنَ** (so that you may take away a part of what you have given them,)

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). He said: "It concerns the man who has a wife and harms her until she ransoms herself (تفتدي) from him. So Allah forbade that."

In al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): The intended meaning refers to the husband. Allah, the Glorified, commanded him to free her way (تخلية سبيلها) if he had no need for her, and not to restrain her in order to harm her until she ransoms herself with some of her wealth, إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِينَ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ (unless they commit a gross indecency) which is manifest (ظاهرة), such as disobedience (النشوز), bad companionship (سوء العشرة), and lack of chastity (عدم التعفف).

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [It means] every act of disobedience (كل معصية).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If she says to him, "I will not perform ghusl (اغتسل) for you from ritual impurity (جنابة), nor will I fulfill (1) an oath for you, nor will I tread your bed with one whom you dislike," then it is lawful for him to divorce her (يخلعها) and lawful for him what he took from her. وَعاشِرُوهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (and consort with them honourably.) [This means] with fairness (بالإِنصاف) in action and kindness (الإِجمال) in speech. فَإِنْ كَرِهْتُمُوهُنَّ فَعَسى أَنْ تَكْرَهُوا شَيْئاً وَيَجْعَلَ اللهُ فِيهِ خَيْراً كَثِيراً (Should you dislike them, it is possible that you dislike something wherein Allah has placed much good.) Meaning: So be patient with them and do not separate from them merely due to the soul's dislike, for perhaps the soul dislikes what is more beneficial (أصلح) in religion and more praiseworthy (أحمد), and loves what is contrary to it.

** وَإِنْ أَرَدْتُمُ اسْتِبْدالَ زَوْجٍ مَكانَ زَوْجٍ (If you desire to take a wife in place of another wife) meaning, divorcing one woman and marrying another, وَآتَيْتُمْ إِحْداهُنَّ قِنْطاراً (and you have given one of them a qintar)—a great amount of wealth—فَلا تَأْخُذُوا مِنْهُ (do not take anything from it)—meaning, from the qintar— شَيْئاً** (anything).

In al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): The qintar (القنطار) is the measure of a bull's hide filled with gold.

أَتَأْخُذُونَهُ بُهْتاناً وَإِثْماً مُبِيناً ** (Would you take it by way of calumny and manifest sin?) This is a denial and a reprimand (إنكار وتوبيخ). It was said: When a man desired a new wife, he would calumniate (بَهَتَ) the wife under him with an indecency (فاحشة) until he compelled her to ransom herself from him with what He had given her, so that he could spend it on marrying the new wife. Thus, they were forbidden from doing that.

(21) ** وَكَيْفَ تَأْخُذُونَهُ وَقَدْ أَفْضى بَعْضُكُمْ إِلى بَعْضٍ And how can you take it, when you have gone into each other? Al-Qummi said: al-Ifdā’ (الإِفضاء) is sexual intercourse (المباشرة). ** وَأَخَذْنَ مِنْكُمْ مِيثاقاً غَلِيظاً And they have taken a solemn covenant from you. (A firm pledge (عهداً وثيقاً).) In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is the covenant taken upon the husband at the time of the contract, concerning holding [the wife] with kindness (بِمَعْرُوفٍ) or releasing [her] with goodness (تَسْرِيحٌ بِإِحْسانٍ). In al-Kafi and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The covenant (الميثاق) is the word (الكلمة) by which the marriage was contracted, and the solemn (الغليظ) is the man's water (ماء الرجل) which he empties (2) into her. And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: You have taken them by the trust (أمانة) of Allah and made their private parts lawful (استحللتم فروجهن) by the word (كلمة) of Allah.

(٢٢) وَلا تَنْكِحُوا ما نَكَحَ آباؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّساءِ إِلَّا ما قَدْ سَلَفَ (22) Do not marry women whom your fathers have married, except what is already past. [This is] an exception from the implication of the prohibition, so it is as if it was said: You deserve punishment for that, except what has already passed in the Era of Ignorance (الجاهلية), for you are excused regarding it. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Allah the Almighty says: ** وَلا تَنْكِحُوا ما نَكَحَ آباؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّساءِ (Do not marry women whom your fathers have married), so it is not permissible for a man to marry the wife of his grandfather. إِنَّهُ كانَ فاحِشَةً وَمَقْتاً (١) وَساءَ سَبِيلاً (It is indeed an indecency and an odium, and) (22) an evil way. It was said that they used to marry their stepmothers (روابهم) (٢), and those among them who possessed chivalry (مرواتهم) hated it and called it the marriage of hatred (نكاح المقت) (٣). They called the one born from it al-Maqti (المقتي). The reason for its revelation has already passed shortly before.

(٢٣) ** حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهاتُكُمْ وَبَناتُكُمْ وَأَخَواتُكُمْ وَعَمَّاتُكُمْ وَخالاتُكُمْ وَبَناتُ الْأَخِ وَبَناتُ الْأُخْتِ** (23) Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your paternal aunts, your maternal aunts, your brother’s daughters, and your sister’s daughters.

meaning, marrying them. And mothers include those who are higher [in lineage], and likewise paternal aunts (العمات) and maternal aunts (الخالات). And daughters include those who are lower [in lineage], and likewise brother’s daughters (بنات الأخ) and sister’s daughters (بنات الاخت). And sisters include the three categories (الوجوه الثلاثة).

** وَأُمَّهاتُكُمُ اللَّاتِي أَرْضَعْنَكُمْ وَأَخَواتُكُمْ مِنَ الرَّضاعَةِ** (and your mothers who have suckled you, and your sisters through suckling).

He named her a mother (أماً) and a sister (أختاً). And the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Forbidden through suckling is what is forbidden through lineage (النسب)." And He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Suckling is a kinship (لحمة) like the kinship of lineage (النسب)." Thus, the prohibition is general.

** وَأُمَّهاتُ نِسائِكُمْ** (and your wives’ mothers)

even if they are higher [in lineage].

** وَرَبائِبُكُمُ اللَّاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ** (and your stepdaughters—those of them who are under your guardianship)

even if they are lower [in lineage],

** مِنْ نِسائِكُمُ اللَّاتِي دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَ** (from among your wives with whom you have consummated marriage)

meaning: you have entered with them in privacy (في السر), which is a euphemism for intercourse (الجماع).

** فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُونُوا دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ**. (But if you have not consummated marriage with them, there is no sin upon you).

In الفقيه and التهذيب, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: If a man marries a woman, her daughter becomes forbidden to him if he has consummated marriage with the mother. But if he has not consummated marriage with the mother, there is no harm in him marrying the daughter. And if he marries the daughter, whether he has consummated marriage with her or not, the mother becomes forbidden to him. And He said: The stepdaughters (الربائب) are forbidden, whether they were under guardianship (في الحجر) or not. In another narration, He said: The stepdaughters (الربائب) are forbidden to you along with the mothers with whom marriage has been consummated, whether they were under guardianship (في الحجور) or not under guardianship (وغير الحجور). And the mothers are absolute (مبهمات), whether he has consummated marriage with the daughters or not consummated marriage with them. And in another [narration], He said: This one is excepted (مستثناة) and this one is unrestricted (مرسلة).

** وَأُمَّهاتُ نِسائِكُمْ** (and your wives’ mothers)

Therefore, whatever has been narrated from them contrary to that is attributed to dissimulation (التقية) due to conformity with the general populace (العامّة) and contradiction of the Quran.

In الكافي, narrated from Abu al-Hasan عليه السلام: He was asked about a man who marries a woman in temporary marriage (متعة). Is it lawful for him to marry her daughter? He said: No.

And from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Regarding a man who owns a slave-girl (الجارية) and has intercourse with her (يصيب منها). Is it lawful for him to marry her daughter? He said: No. She is like the saying of Allah the Almighty: ** وَرَبائِبُكُمُ اللَّاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ** (and your stepdaughters—those of them who are under your guardianship).

And from Him عليه السلام: He was asked about a man who divorced his wife, and she became irrevocably separated (بانت منه) from him, and she had a slave-girl daughter. If he buys her, is it lawful for him to have intercourse with her (يطأها)? He said: No.

And concerning a man who has a slave-girl (المملوكة) and her daughter, and he has intercourse with one of them, and she dies, and the other remains. Is it proper for him to have intercourse with the remaining one? He said: No.

القمّيّ: The Khawarij claimed that if a man’s wife had a daughter, and he did not raise her and she was not under his guardianship (في حجره), she would be lawful for him due to the saying of Allah the Almighty: ** اللَّاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ (those of them who are under your guardianship). Then al-Sadiq عليه السلام said: She is not lawful for him. It was said: And the benefit of His saying ** فِي حُجُورِكُمْ (under your guardianship) is to strengthen the reason (العلة) and complete it. The meaning is that if you consummate marriage with the mothers of the stepdaughters (الربائب) while they are under your care (في احتضانكم) or are about to be, the resemblance between her and your own children is strengthened, and she becomes deserving that you treat her as you treat them, not that it restricts the prohibition (لا تقييد الحرمة).

** وَحَلائِلُ أَبْنائِكُمُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ أَصْلابِكُمْ** (and the wives of your sons who are from your own loins)

This is to exclude the adopted son (المتبنى), not the sons of the children, for they are included even if they are lower [in lineage].

In الكافي, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام in a tradition: Was it lawful for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to marry the wives (حليلتي) of al-Hasan and al-Husayn عليهما السلام? If they say yes, they have lied and committed wickedness (فجروا). And if they say no, then they are sons from his loins (أبناء لصلبه).

In الفقيه and التهذيب, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Regarding a man who has a slave-girl (الجارية) with him, and he strips her and looks at her body with a lustful gaze (نظر شهوة). Is she lawful for his father? And if his father does that, is she lawful for his son? He said: If he looks at her with a lustful gaze and looks at what is forbidden for others to see of her, she is not lawful for his son. And if his father does that, she is not lawful for the father.

** وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ إِلَّا ما قَدْ سَلَفَ** (and that you should bring together two sisters [in marriage]—except what is already past).

for that is forgiven. إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً (Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful).

In الكافي, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Regarding a man who divorced his wife, and she sought divorce by compensation (اختلعت) or mutual release (بارأت). Is it lawful for him to marry her sister? He said: If her bond (عصمتها) has been released and he has no right of return (رجعة) over her, then he may propose marriage to her sister.

And concerning a man who had two slave-girl sisters (اختان مملوكتان) with him, and he had intercourse with one of them, and then had intercourse with the other. He said: If he has intercourse with the second, the first becomes forbidden to him until the second dies. I said: What if he sells her? Does the first become lawful for him? He said: If he sells her out of necessity (لحاجة) and nothing concerning the other [sister] crosses his mind, then I see no harm in that. But if he sells her only to return to the first, then no, and no dignity (ولا كرامة).

And in التهذيب, narrated from Him, from his father عليهما السلام: Concerning two slave-girl sisters (أختين مملوكتين) who are both with a man. He said: Ali عليه الصلاة والسلام said: "One verse made them lawful, and another verse made them forbidden, and I forbid myself and my children from it."

I say: The verse that makes them lawful is His saying, the Glorified: ** وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ لِفُرُوجِهِمْ حافِظُونَ إِلَّا عَلى أَزْواجِهِمْ أَوْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُهُمْ (and those who guard their private parts, except from their spouses or those whom their right hands possess). And the verse that makes them forbidden is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ (and that you should bring together two sisters [in marriage]). The scope of lawfulness (الحل) and prohibition (الحرمة) is only intercourse (الوطي) specifically, not merely possessing them in ownership (الجمع في الملك), as the author of التهذيب supposed, who thought that the verse of lawfulness (آية الحل) was the verse of ownership (آية الملك) and the verse of prohibition (آية التحريم) was the verse of intercourse (آية الوطي). What explicitly indicates this is the narration contained therein from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He was asked about what people narrate concerning the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام regarding matters of private parts (الفروج) which He would neither command nor forbid except for Himself and His children. It was asked: How can that be? He said: "One verse made it lawful, and another made it forbidden." It was asked: Did one of the two verses abrogate the other, or are both decisive (محكمتان) and ought to be acted upon? He replied: "He made it clear to them when He forbade Himself and His children." It was asked: What prevented Him from making that clear to the people? He said: "He feared that He would not be obeyed. If the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام had established His footing, He would have upheld the entire Book of Allah and the entire Truth."

And العيّاشيّ narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked about two slave-girl sisters (الأختين المملوكتين); if he marries one of them, is the other lawful for him? He said: "It is not for him to marry the other except in what is short of the private part (دون الفرج), and if he does not do it, it is better for him. This is similar to the woman who is menstruating (تحيض), and it is forbidden for her husband to approach her in her private part, due to the saying of Allah the Almighty: وَلا تَقْرَبُوهُنَّ حَتَّى يَطْهُرْنَ (and do not approach them until they are clean). He said: وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ إِلَّا ما قَدْ سَلَفَ (and that you should bring together two sisters [in marriage]—except what is already past), meaning in marriage (في النكاح). Thus, it is permissible for a man to approach his wife while she is menstruating in what is short of the private part (فيما دون الفرج)."

(٢٤) وَالْمُحْصَناتُ مِنَ النِّساءِ إِلَّا ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ كِتابَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأُحِلَّ لَكُمْ ما وَراءَ ذلِكُمْ أَنْ تَبْتَغُوا بِأَمْوالِكُمْ مُحْصِنِينَ غَيْرَ مُسافِحِينَ فَمَا اسْتَمْتَعْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْهُنَّ فَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَ فَرِيضَةً وَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيما تَراضَيْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ الْفَرِيضَةِ إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلِيماً حَكِيماً (24) And married women, all except those whom your right hands own. This is Allah’s ordinance upon you. Lawful for you are [women] other than these, provided that you seek [them] with your wealth, in wedlock, not in license. As for those of whom you seek enjoyment, give them their dowries as a mandatory offering. There is no sin upon you in what you agree upon after the mandatory offering. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-wise.

(١) مِنَ النِّساءِ (from among women) are those whom marriage or husbands have rendered chaste (أحصنهن). It was also recited with a kasrah on the ṣād (الْمُحْصِنَاتُ) because they have guarded (أحصن) their private parts (فروجهن).

In al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are those who have husbands (ذوات الأزواج).

إِلَّا ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ (except those whom your right hands own) from among those women who were taken captive (سبين) and who have disbelieving husbands (أزواج كفّار). Indeed, they are lawful (حلال) for the captors (للسابين).

As narrated in al-Majma' from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): And those women who are purchased (اشترين) and who have husbands, their sale is their divorce (طلاقهن).

As narrated in al-Kafi from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in several narrations: And those women who are under the control of slaves (تحت العبيد), their masters (مواليهم) order them (the slaves) to separate (بالاعتزال), so they (the masters) seek clearance of their wombs (فيستبرؤونهن), and then they may touch them (يمسونهن) without marriage (بغير نكاح).

As narrated in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi from him (peace be upon him): ** كِتابَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ (Allah’s ordinance upon you) is an emphatic infinitive (مصدر مؤكد), meaning Allah has ordained (كتب) the prohibition of these women upon you as a decree (كتاباً). وَأُحِلَّ لَكُمْ ما وَراءَ ذلِكُمْ (and lawful for you are [women] other than these) meaning those besides the mentioned forbidden women (المحرمات المذكورة). Excluded from this by the Sunnah are those who fall under the meaning of the mentioned women, such as the rest of the prohibitions due to suckling (محرمات الرضاع) and combining a woman with her paternal aunt (عمتها) or her maternal aunt (خالتها) without her permission (بغير اذنها). As narrated in al-Kafi from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in several narrations. And the word وأحل (and made lawful) was recited in the passive voice (على البناء للمفعول). ** أَنْ تَبْتَغُوا بِأَمْوالِكُمْ مُحْصِنِينَ غَيْرَ مُسافِحِينَ (that you seek [them] with your wealth, in wedlock, not in license). This means that you spend your wealth on their dowries (مهورهن) or their prices (أثمانهن). الإحصان (wedlock/chastity) is chastity (العفة), and السفاح (license) is fornication (الزنا).

** فَمَا اسْتَمْتَعْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْهُنَّ فَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَ (As for those of whom you seek enjoyment, give them their dowries). Their dowries (مهورهن). It was named a wage (أجراً) because it is in exchange for the enjoyment (الاستمتاع). ** فَرِيضَةً (as a mandatory offering) is an emphatic infinitive (مصدر مؤكد).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed thus: ** فَمَا اسْتَمْتَعْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْهُنَ (As for those of whom you seek enjoyment) until a specified term (إلى اجل مسمى) ** فَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً (give them their dowries as a mandatory offering).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He used to recite it thus. The general public (العامّة) also narrated it from a group of the Companions (الصحابة).

** وَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيما تَراضَيْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ الْفَرِيضَةِ (There is no sin upon you in what you agree upon after the mandatory offering) such as an increase in the dowry (المهر) or the term (الأجل), or a decrease in both, or anything else that does not contradict the Sacred Law (الشرع).

In al-Kafi, narrated maqṭūʿan (مقطوعاً).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): There is no harm in you increasing her (payment) and her increasing you (the term) if the term (الأجل) between you has ended. You say: "I have made you lawful for myself (استحللتك) for another term (بأجل آخر) with her consent (برضى منها)." And she is not lawful for anyone else until her waiting period (عدتها) ends, and her waiting period is two menstrual cycles (حيضتان).

إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلِيماً (Indeed Allah is all-knowing) of the interests (بالمصالح) ** حَكِيماً (all-wise) in the rulings (الأحكام) He has legislated (شرع).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Al-Mut'ah (المتعة) was revealed in the Quran, and the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was established concerning it.

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Ali (peace be upon him) used to say: "Were it not for what the son of al-Khattab (بني الخطاب) preceded me in, none would commit fornication (زنى) except for a few (إلا شفي)."

I say: إلّا شفي (except for a few) with the letter fā' (الفاء), meaning "except for a small number (الا قليل)." He meant that were it not for what Umar preceded him in—his prohibition of al-Mut'ah (المتعة) and the establishment of his prohibition in the hearts of the people—he would have called people to it (لندبت الناس عليها) and encouraged them in it, so they would have been independent of fornication (الزنا) through it, and only a few of them would have committed fornication. His prohibition of it was sometimes by his saying: "Two Mut'ahs (متعتان) existed during the time of the Messenger of Allah, and I prohibit them and will punish for them: Mut'at al-Hajj (متعة الحجّ) and Mut'at al-Nisa' (متعة النساء)." And at other times by his saying: "Three things existed during the time of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), and I prohibit them and will punish for them: Mut'at al-Hajj, Mut'at al-Nisa', and Ḥayyi ‘alā khayr al-‘amal (حيّ على خير العمل) in the Adhan (الأذان)."

And in it (the narration): Abdullah ibn Umayr al-Laythi (عبد الله بن عمير الليثي) came to Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) and said to him: "What do you say about Mut'at al-Nisa' (متعة النساء)?" He replied: "Allah made it lawful in His Book and upon the tongue of His Prophet, so it is lawful until the Day of Resurrection." He said: "O Abu Ja'far, someone like you says this, when Umar has forbidden it and prohibited it?" He replied: "Even if he did so?" He said: "Then I seek refuge for you in Allah from making lawful something that Umar forbade." He (Abu Ja'far) said to him: "Then you follow the saying of your companion, and I follow the saying of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). Come, let us invoke the curse of Allah (أُلاعنك), that the true saying is what the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said, and the falsehood is what your companion said." The narrator said: Then Abdullah ibn Umayr turned and said: "Would it please you if your wives, your daughters, your sisters, and your paternal cousins (بنات عمك) did that?" The narrator said: Then Abu Ja'far turned away from him when he mentioned his wives and his paternal cousins.

And in it (the narration): Abu Hanifah asked Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn al-Nu'man, Ṣāḥib al-Ṭāq (صاحب الطاق), and said to him: "O Abu Ja'far, what do you say about al-Mut'ah? Do you claim that it is lawful (حلال)?" He replied: "Yes." He said: "Then what prevents you from ordering your women to practice Mut'ah and earn money for you?" Abu Ja'far said to him: "Not all professions (الصناعات) are desirable, even if they are lawful. People have statuses (أقدار) and ranks (مراتب) by which they elevate their statuses. But what do you say, O Abu Hanifah, about al-Nabīdh (النبيذ)? Do you claim that it is lawful?" He replied: "Yes." He said: "Then what prevents you from seating your women in the shops (الحوانيت) as sellers of Nabīdh (نباذات) so they can earn money for you?" Abu Hanifah said: "One for one, and your arrow is more penetrating (سهمك أنفد)." Then he said to him: "O Abu Ja'far, the Ayah in Sa'ala Sā'il (سَأَل سائل) speaks of the prohibition of al-Mut'ah, and the narration from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) has come confirming its abrogation (نسخها)." Abu Ja'far said to him: "O Abu Hanifah, Sūrat Sa'ala Sā'il is Meccan (مكية), and the Ayah of al-Mut'ah is Medinan (مدنية), and your narration is anomalous (شاذة) and rejected (ردية)." Abu Hanifah said: "And the Ayah of inheritance (الميراث) also speaks of the abrogation of al-Mut'ah." Abu Ja'far said: "Marriage (النكاح) without inheritance has already been established." Abu Hanifah said: "From where do you say that?" Abu Ja'far said: "If a man from the Muslims marries a woman from the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) and then dies while married to her, what do you say about her?" He replied: "She does not inherit from him." He said: "Then marriage without inheritance has already been established." Then they parted.

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Abu Hanifah asked him about al-Mut'ah. He said: "Which of the two Mut'ahs are you asking about?" He replied: "I asked you about Mut'at al-Hajj, so inform me about Mut'at al-Nisa'. Is it true?" He said: "Glory be to Allah! Do you not read the Book of Allah: ** فَمَا اسْتَمْتَعْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْهُنَّ فَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً (As for those of whom you seek enjoyment, give them their dowries as a mandatory offering)?" Abu Hanifah said: "By Allah, it is as if I have never read that Ayah before."

And in al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "He is not one of us who does not believe in our return (بكرتنا) and does not deem our Mut'ah (متعتنا) lawful."

I say: Al-Karrat (الكرة) is al-Raj'ah (الرجعة) (the Return), and it is a reference to what is established concerning them (peace be upon them) regarding their return to the world along with groups of their followers (شيعتهم) during the time of al-Qā’im (peace be upon him) to support Him. Reference to this has passed previously, and other narrations concerning it will come, Allah willing.

(٢٥) وَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ مِنْكُمْ طَوْلاً (And whoever of you cannot afford to marry free believing women, then [let him marry] from what your right hands own of your believing maidservants.) Meaning wealth (غنىً). This is according to al-Majma' narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). أَنْ يَنْكِحَ الْمُحْصَناتِ الْمُؤْمِناتِ (to marry free believing women) meaning the free women (الحرائر). ** فَمِنْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ مِنْ فَتَياتِكُمُ الْمُؤْمِناتِ (then [let him marry] from what your right hands own of your believing maidservants) meaning the female slaves (الإماء).

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man marrying a female slave (الأمة). He said: "No, unless he is compelled (يضطر) to do so."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It is not appropriate for a free man (الحرّ) to marry a female slave (المملوكة) today. That was only [permissible] when Allah the Almighty said: ** وَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ مِنْكُمْ طَوْلاً (And whoever of you cannot afford ṭawlan). And al-ṭawl (الطول) is the dowry (المهر). And the dowry of a free woman (الحرة) today is the dowry of a female slave (الأمة) or less."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "He may marry a free woman (الحرة) while already married to a female slave (الأمة), but he may not marry a female slave while already married to a free woman. And the marriage of a female slave while [already married to] a free woman is void (باطل). If a free woman and a female slave are both with you, the free woman has two days and the female slave has one day. And the marriage of a female slave is not valid (يصلح) except with the permission of her masters (مواليها)."

وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِإِيمانِكُمْ (Allah knows best your faith). So, suffice yourselves with the apparent faith (ظاهر الإِيْمان), for He is the Knower of the secrets (العالم بالسرائر). And there is variation (يتفاضل) in the faith among you, for perhaps a female slave (أمة) surpasses a free woman (الحرة) in it, and superiority of lineage (فضل النسب) alone is not considered. ** بَعْضُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ (You are parts of one another). You and your slaves (مماليككم) are related (متناسبون); your lineage is from Adam (آدم) and your religion is Islam (الإِسلام). فَانْكِحُوهُنَّ بِإِذْنِ أَهْلِهِنَ** (So marry them with the permission of their guardians).

In al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked whether a man may marry a female slave without the knowledge of her guardians (أهلها). He said: "That is fornication (زنا). Indeed, Allah the Almighty says: فَانْكِحُوهُنَّ بِإِذْنِ أَهْلِهِنَ (So marry them with the permission of their guardians)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "There is no harm in a man contracting temporary marriage (يتمتع) with the female slave of a woman. As for the female slave of a man, he may not contract temporary marriage with her except by his command (بأمره)." And in al-Tahdhib, [there is a narration] close to this meaning.

وَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (and give them their dowries honourably) without delay (مطل), harm (ضرار), or reduction (نقصان), [as] muḥṣanāt (مُحْصَناتٍ) meaning chaste women (عفائف), ** غَيْرَ مُسافِحاتٍ (neither fornicating) meaning not openly committing fornication (الزنا), ** وَلا مُتَّخِذاتِ أَخْدانٍ (nor taking secret lovers) meaning secret companions (اخلاء في السر). ** فَإِذا أُحْصِنَ (Then, when they are married) meaning by marriage (بالتزويج). It was also recited with the opening of the hamzah and the ṣād (بفتح الهمزة والصاد). ** فَإِنْ أَتَيْنَ بِفاحِشَةٍ (should they commit an indecent act) meaning fornication (زناء), ** فَعَلَيْهِنَّ نِصْفُ ما عَلَى الْمُحْصَناتِ (there shall be half the punishment for them that is [prescribed] for free chaste women) meaning the free women (الحرائر), مِنَ الْعَذابِ (of the punishment) meaning the prescribed penalty (الحدّ), just as the Almighty said: وَلْيَشْهَدْ عَذابَهُما طائِفَةٌ (and let a group of the faithful witness their punishment).

Al-Qummi said: It means that male slaves (العبيد) and female slaves (الإِماء) who commit fornication (زنيا) are given half the prescribed penalty (الحدّ). If they repeat it, then the like of that [punishment is given], until they do that eight times. In the eighth [instance], they are executed (يقتلون). Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "He is only executed in the eighth [instance] because Allah had mercy on him by not combining the bond of slavery (ربق الرق) and the prescribed penalty of the free person (حدّ الحر)." And in al-Kafi, [there is a narration] with a similar meaning.

Narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) and from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding a female slave who commits fornication (تزني): He said: "She is lashed half the prescribed penalty of the free woman, whether she has a husband or does not have a husband." And in one narration: "She is neither stoned (ترجم) nor exiled (تنفى)."

ذلِكَ ** (That) meaning the marriage of female slaves (نكاح الإماء), ** لِمَنْ خَشِيَ الْعَنَتَ مِنْكُمْ ** (is for those of you who fear falling into distress) meaning for those who fear the sin (الإِثم) to which the dominance of lust (غلبة الشهوة) leads. The origin of al-ʿanat (العنت) is the breaking of a bone after it has been set, so it is metaphorically used for every hardship (مشقة) and necessity (ضرورة). ** وَأَنْ تَصْبِرُوا خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ (But should you be patient, it is better for you). And your patience from marrying female slaves, while remaining chaste (متعففين), is better for you. وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ (and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful).

(٢٦) يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ (26) Allah desires to clarify for you what was hidden from you concerning your interests (مصالحكم) and the merits of your deeds (محاسن أعمالكم) ** وَيَهْدِيَكُمْ سُنَنَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ and guide you to the ways of those who were before you from the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the people of truth (أهل الحق), so that you may follow their example ** وَيَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ and turn to you clemently and guide you to that which prevents you from acts of disobedience (المعاصي) ** وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ and Allah is all-knowing, of them (the acts of disobedience) ** حَكِيمٌ all-wise. in establishing them (the rulings).

(٢٧) ** وَاللهُ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ (27) And Allah desires to turn to you clemently He repeated it for emphasis and juxtaposition. ** وَيُرِيدُ الَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ الشَّهَواتِ And those who follow desires desire the people of falsehood (أهل الباطل). ** أَنْ تَمِيلُوا that you should incline from the truth by agreeing with them in following desires and deeming forbidden things lawful (استحلال المحرمات). ** مَيْلاً عَظِيماً a great inclination. compared to the deviation of one who commits a sin infrequently (على ندور) without deeming it lawful (غير مستحل له).

(28) يُرِيدُ اللهُ أَنْ يُخَفِّفَ عَنْكُمْ (28) Allah desires to lighten your burden. For that reason, He legislated for you the easy, tolerant, upright Law (الشريعة الحنفية) and granted you dispensations in difficult situations, such as making lawful the marriage of the slave-woman when necessity arises. ** وَخُلِقَ الْإِنْسانُ ضَعِيفاً ...and man was created weak. He does not endure being away from desires (الشهوات) nor does he bear the hardships of acts of obedience (الطاعات).

(29) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوالَكُمْ بَيْنَكُمْ بِالْباطِلِ (O you who have faith! Do not consume your property among yourselves wrongfully) by that which the Divine Law (الشرع) has not permitted.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He meant by it gambling (القمار). And Quraysh used to gamble with a man over his family and his wealth, so Allah forbade them from that.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Usury (الربا), gambling (القمار), reduction (in value/cheating) (البخس), and injustice (الظلم). ** إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ تِجارَةً عَنْ تَراضٍ مِنْكُمْ** (unless it is a trade based on mutual consent among you).

Al-Qummi said: He means by it lawful buying and selling (الشراء والبيع الحلال).

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man among us who possesses something by which he sustains himself, but he owes a debt. Should he feed his dependents with it until Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, brings him ease (ميسرة) so that he can pay his debt, or should he borrow upon his own responsibility (على ظهره) during the wickedness of the time and the difficulty of earnings, and accept charity (الصدقة)? He said: He should pay his debt with what he possesses, and he should not consume the wealth of people unless he has the means to render their rights to them. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: وَلا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوالَكُمْ بَيْنَكُمْ بِالْباطِلِ إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ تِجارَةً عَنْ تَراضٍ مِنْكُمْ (and do not consume your property among yourselves wrongfully, unless it is a trade based on mutual consent among you). And he should not borrow upon his own responsibility (على ظهره) unless he has the means to fulfill it, even if he has to go around to the doors of people and they turn him away with a morsel or two, or a date or two. This is unless he has a guardian (ولي) who will pay his debt after him. He is not one of us who dies unless Allah appoints for him a guardian (ولياً) who stands by his promise (عدته) and his debt, and fulfills his promise and his debt. وَلا تَقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ (Do not kill yourselves).

Al-Qummi said: When a man went out with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in a campaign (الغزو), he would charge the enemy alone without the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanding him. So Allah forbade him from killing himself without His command.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Its meaning is: Do not risk your souls in fighting, so that you fight those whom you cannot overcome.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The Muslims used to enter upon their enemy in caves (المغارات), and their enemy would gain control over them and kill them however they wished. So Allah forbade them from entering upon them in the caves. ** إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِكُمْ رَحِيماً** (Indeed Allah is All-merciful to you). Allah only forbade you from killing yourselves due to the abundance of His mercy toward you.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: I asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم about the splints (الجبائر) that are placed on a broken limb: How does the person perform ablution (يتوضأ) and how does he perform the ritual bath (يغتسل) if he is in a state of major ritual impurity (اجنب)? He said: Wiping water over them suffices him for both janabah and ablution (الوضوء). I said: What if it is cold, and he fears for himself if he pours water over his body? So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم recited: وَلا تَقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِكُمْ رَحِيماً (Do not kill yourselves. Indeed Allah is All-merciful to you).

I say: This narration suggests the generality of the ruling concerning all types of killing, casting the soul into destruction (التهلكة), and committing that which leads to it, or rather, committing that which destroys it, for that is the true killing of the soul. And it was said that what is meant by "yourselves" (الأنفس) are those who belong to their religion, for the believers are like a single soul. He combined in the recommendation the preservation of the soul and the wealth, which is its twin (شقيقها), since by it the soul is sustained, keeping them alive until the souls (1) are perfected and fulfill their virtues, out of compassion for them.

(٣٠) ** وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذلِكَ (30) And whoever does that is a reference to what preceded of the prohibitions, ** عُدْواناً وَظُلْماً (aggressively and unjustly), meaning excessiveness in transgressing the truth and committing what he is not entitled to, فَسَوْفَ نُصْلِيهِ ناراً (then We shall cast him into a Fire), meaning We shall make him enter it. ** وَكانَ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيراً** and that is easy for Allah there is no difficulty in it, nor is there anything that can avert it from Him.

** إِنْ تَجْتَنِبُوا كَبائِرَ ما تُنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ نُكَفِّرْ عَنْكُمْ سَيِّئاتِكُمْ (31) If you avoid the major sins that you are forbidden, We will absolve you of your misdeeds He will forgive your minor sins (صغائركم), erase them from you, and you will not be questioned about them. ** وَنُدْخِلْكُمْ مُدْخَلاً كَرِيماً and admit you into a noble entrance. [Meaning] Paradise and the reward you were promised, or an entrance (إدخالاً) accompanied by honor. It was also recited with a fathah on the mīm (مُدْخَلاً), and this also allows for the meaning of place (المكان) or verbal noun (المصدر).

In al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the major sins (الكبائر), and He said: "Everything for which Allah has promised the Fire." In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: "The major sins (الكبائر) are those for which Allah has made the Fire obligatory." In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: "Whoever avoids that for which Allah has promised the Fire, if he is a believer (مؤمناً), Allah will absolve him of his misdeeds and admit him into a noble entrance. The seven major sins (الكبائر) that necessitate [the Fire] (الموجبات) are: unlawful killing of a soul, disobedience to parents (عقوق الوالدين), consuming usury (الربى), becoming a nomad after the emigration (التعرب بعد الهجرة), slandering a chaste woman (قذف المحصنة), consuming the property of an orphan, and fleeing from the battlefield (الفرار من الزحف)." And [al-Kulayni] narrated it in al-Kafi from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), along with four narrations from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in which seven [sins] were counted in each. The general populace (العامّة) also narrated it similarly, except that some of them replaced some [sins] with others. The common [sins] in the seven narrations are: killing, disobedience to parents (العقوق), consuming the property of an orphan, and fleeing from the battlefield (الفرار عن الزحف).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in the context of the four [narrations]: Zurarah asked Him about the major sins (الكبائر). He said: "They are seven in the book of Ali (prayers and peace of Allah be upon him): disbelief in Allah (الكفر بالله), killing a soul, disobedience to parents (عقوق الوالدين), consuming usury (الربى) after clarification (بعد البينة), consuming the property of an orphan unjustly, fleeing from the battlefield (الفرار من الزحف), and becoming a nomad after the emigration (التعرب بعد الهجرة)." Zurarah said: "I said: 'Are these the greatest acts of disobedience (المعاصي)?'" He said: "Yes." I said: "Is consuming a dirham of an orphan's property unjustly greater, or abandoning the prayer (ترك الصلاة)?" He said: "Abandoning the prayer." I said: "But you did not count abandoning the prayer among the major sins (الكبائر)." He said: "What was the first thing I mentioned to you?" I said: "Disbelief (الكفر)." He said: "Indeed, the one who abandons the prayer is a disbeliever (كافر), meaning without an excuse."

I say: The word الموجبات (necessitating/obligating) allows for both the kasrah and the fathah [on the jīm], meaning those [sins] that necessitate the Fire (توجب النار) and those upon which Allah the Almighty has made the Fire obligatory (أوجب الله تعالى عليها النار). Becoming a nomad after the emigration (التعرب بعد الهجرة) means returning to the desert (البادية) and residing with the Bedouins (الأعراب) after having been an emigrant (مهاجراً). Whoever returned to his place after the emigration without an excuse was considered by them to be like an apostate (كالمرتد). It is not far-fetched to generalize this to everyone who learned the manners (آداب) of the Sacred Law (الشرع) and the Sunnah, then abandoned them, turned away from them, and did not act upon them.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The one who becomes a nomad after the emigration (المتعرب بعد الهجرة) is the one who abandons this matter (هذا الأمر) after knowing it. The meaning of "after clarification" (بعد البينة) is after its prohibition (تحريمه) becomes clear to him. Al-Muhsanah (المحصنة) with a fathah on the sad (الصاد) is the one known for chastity (العفة), whether she is married or not. Al-Zahf (الزحف) is marching toward the enemy for combat (للمحاربة). In some narrations, other things besides those mentioned were counted among the major sins (الكبائر): such as associating partners with Allah (الإِشراك بالله), despairing of the relief of Allah (اليأس من روح الله), feeling secure from the plan of Allah (الامن من مكر الله), sorcery (السحر), fornication/adultery (الزنا), the false oath that plunges [one into sin] (اليمين الغموس الفاجرة), misappropriation of war booty (الغلول), false testimony (شهادة الزور), concealing testimony (كتمان الشهادة), drinking wine (شرب الخمر), abandoning the obligatory prayer and Zakat (ترك الصلاة والزكاة المفروضتين), breaking a covenant (نقض العهد), severing kinship ties (قطيعة الرحم), sodomy (اللواط), and theft (السرقة), and so on. The meaning of the false oath that plunges [one into sin] (اليمين الغموس الفاجرة) is the lying oath (الكاذبة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The oath that plunges [one into sin] (اليمين الغموس) which necessitates the Fire is when a man swears an oath concerning the right of a Muslim man in order to withhold his property. It was said: It was only named Ghamūs (غموس) because it plunges (تغمس) its taker into sin (الإِثم). And narrated from Ibn Abbas: The major sins (الكبائر) are closer to seven hundred than to seven.

In al-Majma', it is attributed to our companions (أصحابنا) that all acts of disobedience (المعاصي) are major sins (كبيرة), but some are greater than others, and there is no minor sin (صغيرة) among the misdeeds; rather, it is only minor in relation to what is greater and what incurs a greater deserving of punishment. It was said: The reconciliation of this with the verse is to say that if two matters presented themselves to a person, and his soul called him to both such that he could not restrain himself, then his restraining it from the greater of the two absolves him of what he committed [of the lesser], due to the reward he deserves for avoiding the greater [sin]. This is like when he was able to look with lust and to kiss, but he contented himself with looking instead of kissing. Perhaps this also varies depending on individuals and circumstances, for the good deeds (حسنات) of the righteous (الأبرار) are the misdeeds (سيئات) of those brought near [to Allah] (المقربين), and the one who chooses freely (المختار) is held accountable for what is pardoned for those who are compelled (المضطرين).

I say: The apparent meaning of the verse and the narrations reported in its exegesis and the exegesis of the major sins (الكبائر) indicate a distinction between minor sins (الصغائر) and major sins (الكبائر) from one another, as is clear to anyone who reflects upon them. What was attributed in al-Majma' to our companions (أصحابنا) has no basis (لا مستند له). The saying of the reconciler (الموفق) implies that if someone was able to kill another person but only cut off his limbs, the cutting off of his limbs would be an expiation (مكفراً), and this is as you see [problematic]. Therefore, there must be an interpretation (توجيه) for his statement and the statement of the companions so that they conform to the apparent meanings [of the texts].

(٣٢) ** وَلا تَتَمَنَّوْا ما فَضَّلَ اللهُ بِهِ بَعْضَكُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ (32) Do not covet what Allah has preferred some of you over others with. [This refers to] worldly matters (الأمور الدنيوية) such as status (الجاه) and wealth (المال), for perhaps the lack of it is better.

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Meaning, let none of you say, "I wish what so-and-so was given of wealth (المال), blessing (النعمة), and a beautiful wife (المرأة الحسناء) were mine," for that would be envy (حسداً). However, it is permissible for him to say, "O Allah, grant me the like of it."

In al-Khisal, narrated from him (Imam al-Sadiq) through his forefathers (peace be upon them), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Whoever wishes for something that is pleasing (رضىً) to Allah the Almighty will not depart from the world until He grants it. لِلرِّجالِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا اكْتَسَبُوا وَلِلنِّساءِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا اكْتَسَبْنَ For the men there is a share of what they earn, and for the women there is a share of what they earn. This is an explanation of that, meaning: for each of the men and the women there is a merit (فضل) and a share (نصيب) due to what they earned (اكتسب) and because of it. So, seek merit (الفضل) through action (العمل), not through envy (الحسد) and wishing (التمني). ** وَسْئَلُوا اللهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ And ask Allah for His grace. Meaning, do not covet what belongs to people, but ask Allah for the like of it from His treasures (خزائنه) which are inexhaustible (لا تنفد).

In al-Faqih, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Indeed, Allah the Almighty loved something for Himself and disliked it for His creation. He, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), disliked begging (المسألة) for His creation, but He loved for Himself to be asked. There is nothing more beloved to Him than to be asked. So, let none of you be ashamed to ask Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), for His grace (فضله), even if it be the thong of a sandal (شِسْعَ نَعْلٍ).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever does not ask Allah for His grace (فضله) will become poor (افتقر).

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): There is no soul except that Allah has decreed for it a lawful provision (رزقاً حلالاً) which comes to it in well-being (عافية). And He presents to it the unlawful (الحرام) from another direction. If it takes something of the unlawful (الحرام), He deducts it from the lawful (الحلال) which He decreed for it. And with Allah, besides these two, there is abundant grace (فضل كثير), and that is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ): وَسْئَلُوا اللهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ (And ask Allah for His grace).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: [A narration] close to it.

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, provisions (الأرزاق) are guaranteed (مضمونة) and apportioned (مقسومة). And Allah has a grace (فضل) which He distributes from the rising of the dawn (طلوع الفجر) until the rising of the sun (طلوع الشمس). And that is the Almighty's saying: ** وَسْئَلُوا اللهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ (And ask Allah for His grace). Then he said: And the remembrance of Allah (ذكر الله) after the rising of the dawn is more effective in seeking provision (طلب الرزق) than traveling (الضرب في الأرض). إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيماً Indeed Allah is all-knowing of all things. So He knows what everyone deserves (يستحقه).

(٣٣) وَلِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنا مَوالِيَ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوالِدانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ (33) We have appointed heirs for everyone to inherit from what the parents and relatives leave. That is, for every single one of the men and women, We have appointed heirs (وَرَثَة) who are more entitled to his inheritance, inheriting from what the parents (الْوالِدانِ) and the relatives (الْأَقْرَبُونَ) leave.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): What is meant by this are the possessors of kinship (أُولِي الْأَرْحَام) in matters of inheritance, and not the masters of favor (أَوْلِيَاء النِّعْمَة). Thus, the most entitled to the deceased are those closest to him from the kinship (الرَّحِم) that draws him to it.

** وَالَّذِينَ عَقَدَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ فَآتُوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ and those with whom you have made a compact, give them their share. It was said that a man would make a contract (يُعَاقِد) with another man, saying: "My blood is your blood, my destruction (هَدْمِي) is your destruction, my war is your war, and my peace is your peace; you inherit from me and I inherit from you, and you pay the blood-money (تَعْقِل) on my behalf and I pay the blood-money on your behalf." Thus, the ally (الْحَلِيف) would receive one-sixth (السُّدُس) of the ally's inheritance. This was abrogated (فَنُسِخَ) by His saying: ** وَأُولُوا الْأَرْحامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلى بِبَعْضٍ. (But the blood relatives are closer to one another.)

Al-Qummi said: ** وَأُولُوا الْأَرْحامِ abrogated His saying ** وَالَّذِينَ عَقَدَتْ . And it was said that its meaning is: Give them their share of support (النَّصْر), blood-money (الْعَقْل), and aid (الرِّفْد), but no inheritance (مِيرَاث); thus, there is no abrogation (نَسْخ).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If a man allies himself (وَالَى) with another man, he has inheritance (مِيرَاث) and upon him is his blood-money obligation (مَعْقَلَتُهُ), meaning the blood-money (دِيَة) for his unintentional crime (جِنَايَة خَطَأَهُ).

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): What is meant by this are the Imams (الْأَئِمَّة), through Whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has bound your oaths (أَيْمَانُكُمْ). This is supported by what preceded in the verse of the bequest (آيَة الْوَصِيَّة) in Surat al-Baqarah, that the Master of this Affair (صَاحِب هَذَا الْأَمْر) has a right (حَقًّا) in the wealth of the people.

And it was read (قُرِئَ) as ʿāqadat (عَاقَدَتْ), meaning: your hands have contracted with them (عَاقَدَتْهُمْ أَيْدِيكُمْ) and you have shaken hands with them (مَاسَحْتُمُوهُمْ).

** إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيداً Indeed Allah is witness to all things. This is a threat against withholding their share (نَصِيبَهُمْ).

(34) ** الرِّجالُ قَوَّامُونَ عَلَى النِّساءِ (Men are the managers of women) They manage them just as governors manage the subjects (الرعية). ** بِما فَضَّلَ اللهُ بَعْضَهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ (because of the advantage Allah has given some of them over others) Due to His favoring men over women by means of complete intellect (كمال العقل), sound management (حسن التدبير), and increased strength (مزيد القوّة) in deeds and acts of obedience (الطاعات). ** وَبِما أَنْفَقُوا مِنْ أَمْوالِهِمْ (and for the maintenance they provide out of their wealth.) [This refers to what they spend] in their marriage, such as the dowry (المهر) and the maintenance (النفقة). In al-Ilal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked what the advantage of men over women was. He said: "It is like the advantage of water over the earth. By water, the earth is brought to life, and by men, women are brought to life. Were it not for men, women would not have been created." Then He recited this Ayah. Then He said: "Do you not see how women menstruate, and they are unable to perform worship (العبادة) due to impurity (القذارة), while men are not afflicted by anything of the menses (الطمث)?" فَالصَّالِحاتُ قانِتاتٌ. (Thus righteous women are obedient,) Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: He says [it means] obedient (مطيعات). حافِظاتٌ لِلْغَيْبِ (careful in the absence [of their husbands]) [They are careful] regarding themselves and the wealth of their husbands. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, from his forefathers عليهم السلام, from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "No Muslim man has gained a benefit after Islam better than a Muslim wife who pleases him when he looks at her, obeys him when he commands her, and guards him when he is absent from her, regarding herself and his wealth." بِما حَفِظَ اللهُ (as Allah has guarded.) By Allah guarding them. ** وَاللَّاتِي تَخافُونَ نُشُوزَهُنَ (As for those [wives] whose insubordination you fear,) [Meaning] their rising up from your obedience (طاعتكم) and their disobedience (عصيانهن) to you. فَعِظُوهُنَ (advise them) with speech. وَاهْجُرُوهُنَّ فِي الْمَضاجِعِ (and banish them to beds apart) If the advice (العظة) is not effective. In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: [It means] that he turns his back toward her. وَاضْرِبُوهُنَ (and strike them) if the abandonment (الهجرة) is not effective, [with] a non-severe striking (ضرباً غير شديد) that neither cuts flesh (لحماً) nor breaks bone (عظماً). In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: [It means] striking with a tooth-stick (السواك). فَإِنْ أَطَعْنَكُمْ فَلا تَبْغُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ سَبِيلاً (Then if they obey you, do not seek any way to harass them.) [Meaning] through reproach (التوبيخ) and harm (الإيذاء). ** إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلِيًّا كَبِيراً** (Indeed Allah is all-exalted, all-great.) So beware of Him, for He is more powerful over you than you are over those under your authority.

(35) ** وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ شِقاقَ بَيْنِهِما (35) (And if you fear a split between the two of them) meaning the disagreement and lack of consensus on an opinion, as if each one is on a shaqq (side), meaning a side (جانب). ** فَابْعَثُوا حَكَماً مِنْ أَهْلِهِ وَحَكَماً مِنْ أَهْلِها إِنْ يُرِيدا إِصْلاحاً يُوَفِّقِ اللهُ بَيْنَهُما (send an arbitrator from his family and an arbitrator from her family. If they desire reconciliation, Allah will reconcile them.)

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The two arbitrators stipulate that if they wish, they may separate them, and if they wish, they may reconcile them. If they reconcile them, it is permissible (جايز), and if they separate them, it is permissible (جايز). And He said: It is not for them to separate them until they seek their authorization (يستأمراهما). إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلِيماً خَبِيراً (Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-aware), so He knows how the split (الشقاق) is removed and the harmony (الوفاق) occurs.

(٣٦) وَاعْبُدُوا اللهَ وَلا تُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئاً وَبِالْوالِدَيْنِ إِحْساناً (36) Worship Allah and do not ascribe any partners to Him, and be good to the parents. And be good to them with goodness (إحساناً). In al-Ayyashi, narrated from them (peace be upon them both) regarding this Ayah: Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is one of the two parents, and Ali عليه السلام is the other. وَبِذِي الْقُرْبى And to the possessor of kinship (القرابة). ** وَالْيَتامى وَالْمَساكِينِ وَالْجارِ ذِي الْقُرْبى The one whose neighborhood is near, وَالْجارِ الْجُنُبِ (and the distant neighbor). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: The limit of neighborhood (الجوار) is forty houses from every side: from in front of him, from behind him, from his right, and from his left. And narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: Every forty houses are neighbors, from in front of him, from behind him, from his right, and from his left. And narrated from him عليه السلام: Good neighborliness (حسن الجوار) increases provision (الرزق). And he said: Good neighborliness makes homes prosperous (يعمر الديار) and increases lifespans (الأعمار). And narrated from al-Kazim عليه السلام: Good neighborliness is not merely refraining from harm (كف الأذى), but good neighborliness is your patience upon harm (صبرك على الأذى). And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Neighbors are of three types: A neighbor who has three rights: the right of neighborhood (حق الجوار), the right of kinship (حقّ القرابة), and the right of Islam (حقّ الإسلام). And a neighbor who has two rights: the right of neighborhood and the right of Islam. And a neighbor who has one right: the right of neighborhood, and that is the polytheist (المشرك) from the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب). وَالصَّاحِبِ بِالْجَنْبِ It was said: He who accompanies you and is beside you for companionship (رفاقة) in a good matter, such as marriage (تزوج), learning (تعلّم), business (تصرّف), craftsmanship (صناعة), or travel (سفر). وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ The traveler (المسافر) and the guest (الضيف). ** وَما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ The male slaves (العبيد) and the female slaves (الإماء). And al-Qummi (said): وَالصَّاحِبِ بِالْجَنْبِ is your companion in travel (صاحبك في السفر). And Ibn al-Sabil (ابن السبيل) means the sons of the road (أبناء الطريق) who seek your help on their journey. And ** وَما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ means the family (الأهل) and the servant (الخادم). ** إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ مَنْ كانَ مُخْتالاً Arrogant (متكبراً), who disdains his relatives, neighbors, and companions, and does not pay attention to them. ** فَخُوراً Who boasts over them (يتفاخر عليهم).

(٣٧) الَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ (Those who are stingy) with what they have been granted ** وَيَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبُخْلِ**. (and bid the people to be stingy)

In al-Faqih, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "The stingy person (البخيل) is not he who pays the obligatory Zakat from his wealth and gives the al-Bā’inah (1) among his people. Rather, the truly stingy person (البخيل حقّ البخيل) is he who does not pay the obligatory Zakat from his wealth and does not give the al-Bā’inah among his people, while he squanders in everything besides that."

I say: Al-Bā’inah (2) is the gift (العطية). It was named thus because it is separated (أُبينت) from the wealth.

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The stingy person (البخيل) is stingy with what is in his hand, while the miser (الشحيح) is miserly regarding what is in the hands of the people and regarding what is in his own hands, such that he does not see anything in the hands of the people except that he wishes it were his, whether through lawful (الحل) or unlawful (الحرام) means, and he is not content with what Allah has provided Him."

In al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "What is found among our followers (شيعتنا)—three things will not be found among them: there will not be among them one who begs with his palm (يسأل بكفه), nor will there be among them one who is stingy with knowledge (بخيل الحديث)."

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Two characteristics do not combine in a Muslim: stinginess (البخل) and bad character (سوء الخلق)." وَيَكْتُمُونَ ما آتاهُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ (and conceal what Allah has given them out of His grace) such as wealth (الغنى) and knowledge (العلم) where disclosure is appropriate (حيث ينبغي الاظهار).

وَأَعْتَدْنا لِلْكافِرِينَ عَذاباً مُهِيناً (and We have prepared for the faithless a humiliating punishment) The explicit noun (الظاهر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (المضمر) to indicate that whoever is of this nature is ungrateful (كافر) for the favor (نعمة) of Allah, and thus he shall have a punishment that humiliates him, just as he humiliated the favor through stinginess (البخل) and concealment (الإخفاء).

footnote (١) Al-Bawn (*البون*) with *fathah* followed by *sukūn* (*بالفتح فالسكون*) means superiority (*الفضل*) and distinction (*المزية*). It is the verbal noun (*المصدر*) of *bāna yawūnu bawnan* (*بانه بوناً*) when He favored him. And *baynahumā bawnun ba‘īd* (*وبينهما بون بعيد*) means between their ranks (*درجتيهما*) or between their standing in honor (*اعتبارهما في الشرف*). As for physical distance (*التباعد الجسماني*), one says *baynahumā bayn* (*بينهما بين*) with the *yā’* (*بالياء*). (*Majma‘*)

footnote (٢) Thus, *al-Sadaqah al-Bā’inah* (*الصدقة البائنة*) is that charity which its owner gives voluntarily (*يتفضل بها*) without Allah the Almighty having made it obligatory upon him.

(٣٨) وَالَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ أَمْوالَهُمْ رِئاءَ النَّاسِ (38) and those who spend their wealth to show off to people, He included them with the misers (البخلاء) in the condemnation and the threat (of punishment), because they both share in the failure to spend as is proper. ** وَلا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَلا بِالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ and do not believe in Allah nor the Last Day. (This is stated) so that they might seek through spending His good pleasure and His reward. وَمَنْ يَكُنِ الشَّيْطانُ لَهُ قَرِيناً فَساءَ قَرِيناً And whoever has Satan for a companion—what an evil companion! This is a warning that Satan is their companion (قرينهم); He incites them to that and makes it appealing to them, like His saying: ** إِنَّ الْمُبَذِّرِينَ كانُوا إِخْوانَ الشَّياطِينِ Indeed the wasteful are brothers of the devils,

(٣٩) وَما ذا عَلَيْهِمْ لَوْ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَأَنْفَقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَهُمُ اللهُ (39) What harm would befall them if they believed in Allah and the Last Day, and spent out of what Allah has provided them?

(Spending) in obedience to Allah. This is a reprimand for them due to their ignorance of the place of benefit (المنفعة) and their belief concerning the matter contrary to what it truly is. It is also an incitement to reflection in order to seek the answer, perhaps this will lead them to the knowledge of the benefits (الفوائد) and returns (العوائد) contained therein. It is also an admonition that the one who is called to a matter in which there is no harm ought to respond to it as a precaution (احتياطاً); so how (should he respond) if it includes benefits (المنافع)? Verily, He presented faith (الإِيمان) first here and delayed it in the previous Ayah because the intention here is specification (التخصيص) and there (in the previous Ayah) it was causation/reason (التعليل). ** وَكانَ اللهُ بِهِمْ عَلِيماً and Allah is all-aware of them. (This is) a threat (وعيد) to them.

(٤٠) إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ (Indeed Allah does not wrong [anyone] even to the extent of a speck of dust.) He does not diminish from the reward nor does He increase in the punishment [even] the smallest thing, like the dharr (الذرة), which is the small ant. And it is said that it refers to every part of its parts, the motes (الهباء) (1). And al-mithqāl (المثقال) is derived from weight (الثقل). وَإِنْ تَكُ حَسَنَةً And it was read with the rafʿ (الرفع) [vowel] based on it being a complete verb (التامة). يُضاعِفْها He multiplies its reward. وَيُؤْتِ مِنْ لَدُنْهُ And He gives its owner from His presence by way of grace (التفضل), exceeding what He promised in return for the deed. ** أَجْراً عَظِيماً A great gift, which He named a reward (أجراً) as a consequence of it.

(٤١) فَكَيْفَ (Then how) will their state be, due to the terror and dread, ** إِذا جِئْنا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنا بِكَ ** (when We bring a witness from every nation, and bring you) O Muhammad, ** عَلى هؤُلاءِ شَهِيداً** (as a witness against these)? (Then how [will it be] when We bring a witness from every nation, and bring you as a witness against these?)

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed specifically concerning the Ummah of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace). In every generation (قرن) of them, there is an Imam (إمام) who is a witness over them, and Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) is a witness over us.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him), in a hadith where he mentions the states of the people gathered at the Station (أهل الموقف):

The Messengers will be made to stand and will be questioned about the delivery of the messages they carried to their nations. They will inform that they have indeed delivered that to their nations. Then the nations will be questioned, and they will deny, just as Allah said: ** فَلَنَسْئَلَنَّ الَّذِينَ أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلَنَسْئَلَنَّ الْمُرْسَلِينَ (We will surely question those to whom [the apostles] were sent, and We will surely question the apostles) They will say: "No bearer of good news or warner came to us." So the Messengers will call upon the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) to bear witness. He will testify to the truthfulness of the Messengers and refute those among the nations who denied them. He will say to every nation among them: "Yes, a bearer of good news and a warner did come to you. And Allah is powerful over everything (عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)," meaning, He is capable (مقتدر) of making your limbs (جوارحكم) testify against you regarding the Messengers' conveyance of their messages to you. For this reason, Allah the Almighty said to His Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): فَكَيْفَ إِذا جِئْنا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنا بِكَ عَلى هؤُلاءِ شَهِيداً (Then how [will it be] when We bring a witness from every nation, and bring you as a witness against these?). They will not be able to reject His testimony, fearing that Allah might seal their mouths and that their limbs might testify against them concerning what they used to do. He will testify against the hypocrites (منافقي) of his people and his Ummah and their disbelievers (كفّارهم) regarding their deviation (إلحادهم), their obstinacy (عنادهم), their breaking of His covenant (عهده), their changing of His Sunnah (سنته), their aggression against His Household (أهل بيته), their turning back on their heels (انقلابهم على أعقابهم), their apostasy (ارتدادهم) on their backs, and their following in this the way (سنة) of the preceding oppressive nations (الأمم الظالمة) who betrayed their prophets. Then they will all say: ** رَبَّنا غَلَبَتْ عَلَيْنا شِقْوَتُنا وَكُنَّا قَوْماً ضالِّينَ (Our Lord, our wretchedness overcame us, and we were a stray lot).

I say: The revelation of the Ayah concerning this Ummah does not negate the generality of its ruling (حكمها), so there is no contradiction (تنافى) between the two narrations. The full discussion regarding this has already passed in Surat al-Baqarah concerning His saying, glory be to Him: ** وَكَذلِكَ جَعَلْناكُمْ أُمَّةً وَسَطاً لِتَكُونُوا شُهَداءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ** (Thus We have made you a middle nation that you may be witnesses to the people).

footnote (١) *Al-Haba'* (*الهباء*) is what emerges from the niche (*الكوة*) with the sunlight, resembling dust (*الغبار*) (*Majma'*).

(٤٢) يَوْمَئِذٍ يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَعَصَوُا الرَّسُولَ لَوْ تُسَوَّى بِهِمُ الْأَرْضُ وَلا يَكْتُمُونَ اللهَ حَدِيثاً. (42) On that day those who disbelieved and disobeyed the Apostle will wish that the earth were levelled with them, and they will not conceal any matter from Allah.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq, from his grandfather, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in a sermon wherein He describes the terror of the Day of Resurrection: Seals will be placed upon the mouths, so they will not speak. And the hands will speak, and the feet will bear witness, and the skins will be made to speak concerning what they did. So ** وَلا يَكْتُمُونَ اللهَ حَدِيثاً** (and they will not conceal any matter from Allah).

And al-Qummi said: Those who usurped the right of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) will wish that the earth would swallow them on the day they gathered to usurp His right, even if they did not conceal what the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said concerning Him.

(٤٣) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَقْرَبُوا الصَّلاةَ (43) O you who have faith! Do not approach the prayer (meaning: do not stand up for it) ** وَأَنْتُمْ سُكارى while you are intoxicated (from something like sleep or wine) ** حَتَّى تَعْلَمُوا ما تَقُولُونَ until you know what you are saying (meaning: until you become alert and sober).

In al-Kafi, al-Ilal, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Do not stand up for prayer lazily, drowsily, or heavily, for that is among the characteristics (خِلال) (1) of hypocrisy. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has indeed forbidden you from standing up for prayer while you are intoxicated. He (peace be upon him) said: [This refers to] the intoxication of sleep (سكر النوم).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Part of it is the intoxication of sleep (سكر النوم), and this implies generalization [of the ruling].

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): That what is meant by it is the intoxication of drink (سكر الشراب), then the prohibition of wine (تحريم الخمر) abrogated it. Similar to this is what the ‘Ammah (Sunni scholars) narrated: That it was revealed concerning one who, while intoxicated, recited in his prayer: "I worship what you worship."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (al-Kazim, peace be upon him): This was before wine (الخمر) was forbidden. And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah. He said: It means the intoxication of sleep (سكر النوم), meaning that drowsiness comes upon you lazily, preventing you from knowing what you are saying in your bowing (ركوع), prostration (سجود), and takbir (saying Allahu Akbar). It is not as many people describe, claiming that the believers become intoxicated from drink, for the believer does not drink intoxicants (مسكراً) nor does he become intoxicated.

I say: Since wisdom necessitated the gradual prohibition of wine (الخمر) and the delay in explicitly stating it, as its explanation preceded in Surat al-Baqarah, and since a group of Muslims used to pray while intoxicated by it before its prohibition was established, this Ayah was revealed, and they were addressed with such a discourse. Then, when its prohibition was confirmed and established, and they became those who ought not to be addressed with such a discourse (because believers do not become intoxicated from drink after it has been forbidden to them), it became permissible to say that the Ayah is abrogated (منسوخة) by the prohibition of wine (تحريم الخمر). This means that it was no longer appropriate to address them with such a discourse thereafter, not that it meant the permissibility of prayer while intoxicated. Then, when the ruling generalized to everything else that prevents presence of heart (حضور القلب), it became permissible to interpret it sometimes as the intoxication of sleep (سكر النوم) and the like, and sometimes to generalize the ruling. Thus, there is no contradiction among these narrations whatsoever. And all praise belongs to Allah for the understanding He has granted us of the speech of His successors.

** وَلا جُنُباً إِلَّا عابِرِي سَبِيلٍ حَتَّى تَغْتَسِلُوا. nor those who are junub, except while traversing a road, until you wash yourselves.**

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), and in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The menstruating woman (الحائض) and the one who is junub (in a state of major ritual impurity) shall not enter the mosque (المسجد) except as passersby (مجتازين), for Allah the Almighty says: nor those who are junub, except while traversing a road, until you wash yourselves.

One of our companions, skilled in the science of rhetoric (علم البلاغة), said in a book he authored concerning the wondrous literary arts (الصناعات البديعة), when mentioning al-Istikhdam (الاستخدام)—after defining it as when the speaker uses a word common to two meanings, coupled with two contextual indicators (قرينتين), where each indicator uses one of the two meanings of that word—he said: In the noble Ayah, the Glorious One has employed the word al-Salat (الصلاة) for two meanings: the first is the performance of the prayer (إقامة الصلاة), indicated by His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: until you know what you are saying; and the second is the place of prayer (موضع الصلاة), indicated by His saying, Glorious is His praise: nor those who are junub, except while traversing a road.

I say: This is the correct view (الصواب), and it conforms to the narrations (الأخبار) we have related concerning this chapter, as you have learned. It is not what the ‘Ammah (Sunni scholars) laboriously asserted: sometimes claiming that the meaning of al-Salat (الصلاة) at the beginning of this Ayah is its places, which are the mosques (المساجد), based on the indicator traversing a road (عابِرِي سَبِيلٍ); and other times claiming that the meaning of traversing a road (عابِرِي سَبِيلٍ) is the state of travel, specifically when one does not find water and performs tayammum (dry ablution), based on the indicator until you know what you are saying. And if you are sick. It is said that this means a sick person who fears for himself by using water or reaching it.

I say: There is no need for this restriction, because His saying, the Almighty: and you do not find any water is related to the four clauses, and it includes the inability to use it, because that which is prevented is like that which is lost. Likewise, the restriction of travel to the condition of not finding water—both are also derived from the infallible texts (النصوص المعصومية). or on a journey (meaning: engaged in it), since the loss of water is common in most deserts. or one of you comes from the privy (a euphemism for ritual impurity (الحدث), since al-gha’it (الغائط) is the low ground; they used to seek a low place for relieving themselves where their persons would be concealed from the observer) or you have touched women (a euphemism for sexual intercourse (الجماع)), as stated in al-Majma' narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him)...

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: It is sexual intercourse (الجماع), but Allah is the Concealer (ستير), Who loves concealment, and He did not name it as they name it.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): What is meant by or you have touched women is nothing but penetration (المواقعة في الفرج). And in another narration in al-Kafi: Allah is Modest (حي) and Generous (كريم); He expresses sexual intimacy with women by mentioning their touching (ملامستهن). and you do not find any water (meaning: either you lose it or you are unable to use it, as preceded) then have recourse to pure earth (meaning: intend pure dust (تراباً طاهراً)).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Al-Sa'id (الصعيد) is the elevated place, and al-Tayyib (الطيب) is the place from which water flows down. It was said that al-Sa'id (الصعيد) is the surface of the earth, whether it is dust or otherwise, so tayammum (dry ablution) is permissible on solid rock. This is refuted by His saying, the Glorious, in Surat al-Ma'idah: then wipe your faces and your hands with it (meaning: with some of it). To make min (مِنْ) [in مِنْهُ] denote the starting point (لابتداء الغاية) is a forced interpretation (تعسف), since nothing is understood from such a usage except partiality (التبعيض). Indeed, its interpretation as such has been mentioned in some narrations (الأخبار), as will come in its proper place. And from the Hadith is His saying صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, in the context of facilitating and alleviating matters and explaining the favor of Allah, the Glorious, upon him and upon this blessed Ummah, in one of the two narrations: "The earth has been made a place of prostration (مسجداً) for me, and its dust (ترابها) a purifier (طهوراً)." If the earth absolutely were a purifier (طهوراً), then the mention of dust (التراب) would be detrimental to the application of the speech to the intended purpose for which it was delivered. The requirement of the context would have been for Him to say: "The earth has been made a place of prostration (مسجداً) and a purifier (طهوراً) for me," as in the other narration: then wipe your faces and your hands.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) concerning the Ayah of tayammum (التيمم) in al-Ma'idah: When He established wudu' (الوضوء) [as a substitute] if they did not find water, He confirmed some of the washing (الغسل) as wiping (مسحاً), because He said: your faces then connected to it and your hands.

I say: By this, He drew attention to the fact that it is not obligatory to wipe the entire face and hands, as the ‘Ammah (Sunni scholars) do, and that the ba' (الباء) [in بِوُجُوهِكُمْ] denotes partiality (التبعيض). The rest of the Hadith will come, if Allah wills.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) concerning the description of tayammum (التيمم): That He placed his palms (كفيه) on the ground, then wiped his face and his palms, and did not wipe the forearms (الذراعين) with anything.

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That he described tayammum (التيمم), so he struck the ground with his hands, then raised them and shook them off (1), then wiped his forehead (جبينه) and his palms once. And in a narration: Then he wiped his palms, one upon the back of the other.

And narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): Tayammum (التيمم) is one strike for the face and one strike for the palms.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is one type of striking (ضرب واحد) for wudu' (الوضوء) and for ghusl (الغسل) from janabah (major ritual impurity). You strike with your hands twice, then shake them off: one shaking for the face and one time for the hands. And whenever you find water, you must perform ghusl (الغسل) if you are junub, and wudu' (الوضوء) if you are not junub.

I say: Darb wahid (ضرب واحد) (one type of striking) means one type [of action] for the two purifications (الطهارتين), with no difference between them, as is understood from the apparent meaning of the Ayah and the apparent meanings of the narrations (الأخبار) reported in this chapter. It does not mean one strike for wudu' (الوضوء) and two for ghusl (الغسل), as a group of our later companions claimed. How can that be, when everything reported concerning the substitute for ghusl (بدل الغسل) suffices with a single strike, besides the fact that it contradicts the apparent meaning of the wording?

In al-Faqih and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That he was asked whether tayammum (التيمم) for wudu' (الوضوء), for janabah (الجنابة), and for menstruation (الحيض) for women are the same, and he said: "Yes."
