I say: The two strikes (المرتان) were only recommended for both [purifications] due to the condition that the dust adheres to the palm (الكف), as we indicated. For the strike in tayammum (التيمم) is equivalent to scooping up water in wudu' (الوضوء) and ghusl (الغسل). It is possible that the dust might sometimes leave the palms when wiping the face, leaving none for the hands. Thus, precaution (الاحتياط) necessitates two strikes for both purifications (الطهارتين). As for shaking off [the hands] (النفض), it is perhaps to reduce the dust so that the face is not disfigured by it. ** إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَفُوًّا غَفُوراً Indeed Allah is All-forgiving, All-merciful,** and for this reason, He made the matter easy for you and granted you a concession.

footnote (1) *Khilal al-Diyar* (*خِلال الديار*) refers to what surrounds its boundaries and what is between its houses. *Al-Khullah* (*الخُلَّة*) is a characteristic, and its plural is *Khilal* (*خِلال*) (Q).

footnote (1) I shook off (*Nafadhtu*) the garment and the tree (*unfiduhu nafdhan*) when I moved it so that it would shake off (Sihah).

(٤٤) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا نَصِيباً (Have you not regarded those who were given a share) a small portion (حظاً يسيراً) ** مِنَ الْكِتابِ (of the Book) from the knowledge of the Torah (علم التوراة), as it was said that it was revealed concerning the Jewish scholars (أحبار اليهود). ** يَشْتَرُونَ الضَّلالَةَ (who buy error) they exchange it for guidance (الهدى), after having attained it through the miracles indicating the truthfulness of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and that he is the one foretold in the Torah. ** وَيُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَضِلُّوا (and desire that you go astray) O believers, the path (السَّبِيلَ), meaning the path of truth (سبيل الحق).

** أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا نَصِيباً مِنَ الْكِتابِ يَشْتَرُونَ الضَّلالَةَ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَضِلُّوا السَّبِيلَ ** (Have you not regarded those who were given a share of the Book, who buy error and desire that you go astray from the way?)

(٤٥) ** وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ مِنْكُمْ بِأَعْدائِكُمْ (45) But Allah knows best your enemies. And He has informed you of the enmity of these people and what they desire for you, so beware of them. ** وَكَفى بِاللهِ وَلِيًّا Allah is sufficient as a guardian, Who manages your affairs. ** وَكَفى بِاللهِ نَصِيراً** and Allah is sufficient as a helper. Who assists you, so trust in Him and suffice yourselves with Him instead of others.

(٤٦) مِنَ الَّذِينَ هادُوا يُحَرِّفُونَ الْكَلِمَ عَنْ مَواضِعِهِ (46) of those who are Jews, who distort the words out of their places, They deviate from them by substituting one word for another, just as they distorted the description of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم as being dark-complexioned (أسمر) (1), of medium height (ربعة), from its place in the Torah, and put in its place: dark-skinned (أدم) and tall (طوال) (1). ** وَيَقُولُونَ سَمِعْنا [Your word] ** وَعَصَيْنا [Your command] ** وَاسْمَعْ غَيْرَ مُسْمَعٍ They mean: Hear from us, [as] we invoke a curse upon you, [meaning] 'may you not hear,' or 'Hear, [but] may you not be answered regarding what you call to.' ** وَراعِنا [They mean] 'Look at us (انظرنا) so we may speak to you or understand your speech,' but they intend by it insult (السب), for راعنا is an insult (سب) in their language. ** لَيًّا بِأَلْسِنَتِهِمْ meaning: twisting them (فتلاً بها) and diverting the speech to what resembles an insult, since they placed راعنا, which resembles what they use to insult one another, in the place of 'Look at us (انظرنا)' and 'Watch over us (راقبنا),' and [they placed] غير مسمع in the place of 'May you not hear anything disliked (لا أسمعت مكروهاً),' or [it means] twisting them (فتلاً بها) and joining what they manifest of supplication and reverence to what they conceal of abuse (الشتم) and contempt (التحقير), out of hypocrisy (نفاقاً). ** وَطَعْناً فِي الدِّينِ and reviling the religion. [Meaning] mocking it (استهزاء به) and ridiculing it (سخرية). ** وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ قالُوا سَمِعْنا وَأَطَعْنا وَاسْمَعْ وَانْظُرْنا لَكانَ خَيْراً لَهُمْ وَأَقْوَمَ Had they said, 'We hear and obey,' and 'Hear us, and regard us,' it would have been better for them and more upright. and more just (أعدل) and more correct (أسد). ** وَلكِنْ لَعَنَهُمُ اللهُ But Allah has cursed them [Meaning] He abandoned them (خذلهم) and distanced them from guidance (الهدى) ** بِكُفْرِهِمْ for their faithlessness. due to their disbelief (كفرهم). ** فَلا يُؤْمِنُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً So they will not believe, except a few. except for a slight belief (إيماناً قليلاً) that is insignificant, which is belief in some of the signs (الآيات) and the Messengers (الرسل), and a weak belief (إيماناً ضعيفاً) in which there is no sincerity (إخلاص), or except for a few of them.

(1) الأسمر (dark-complexioned) is he whose color resembles the color of wheat, and الأدم (dark-skinned) is he whose dark complexion is intense, and الربعة (medium height) is he who is neither tall nor short. (From him, may Allah have mercy on him).

(٤٧) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ آمِنُوا بِما نَزَّلْنا مُصَدِّقاً لِما مَعَكُمْ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ نَطْمِسَ وُجُوهاً فَنَرُدَّها عَلى أَدْبارِها. (47) O you who were given the Book! Believe in what We have sent down, confirming what is with you, before We obliterate faces and turn them on their backs, In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The meaning is that We obliterate them from guidance (الهدى), so We turn them on their backs in their misguidance (ضلالتها) such that they will never succeed. And al-tams (الطمس) (2) is the removal of the form (الصورة) and the erasure of the outline (التخطيط). ** أَوْ نَلْعَنَهُمْ كَما لَعَنَّا أَصْحابَ السَّبْتِ (or curse them as We cursed the People of the Sabbath.) or We disgrace them through transformation (المسخ) just as We disgraced them with it. ** وَكانَ أَمْرُ اللهِ مَفْعُولاً (And Allah’s command is bound to be fulfilled) Thus, what you have been promised will inevitably occur if you do not believe.

(٤٨) إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ (Indeed Allah does not forgive that a partner should be ascribed to Him) because He has decreed the eternity of his punishment, due to the fact that the effect of his sin is not erased from him, so he is not prepared for forgiveness unless he repents and returns to al-Tawhid (التوحيد), for the door of repentance is always open. وَيَغْفِرُ ما دُونَ ذلِكَ (but He forgives anything besides that)—what is less than al-shirk (الشرك), whether it is minor or major—لِمَنْ يَشاءُ (to whomever He wishes), as a favor (تفضلاً) and kindness (إحساناً) upon him.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: "The major sins (الكبائر) and what is besides them."

And in it (al-Kafi) and in al-Faqih: He was asked whether the major sins (الكبائر) fall under the Will (مشيئة) of Allah. He said: "Yes, that belongs to Him, the Mighty and Majestic. If He wills, He punishes for them, and if He wills, He pardons them."

And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him) what is close to this meaning.

And in al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (prayers of Allah be upon him) in a hadith: "And I certainly heard my beloved Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saying: 'If the believer departs from the world having sins like the sins of the people of the earth, death would be an expiation for those sins.' Then He said: 'Whoever says لا إله إلَّا الله (There is no god except Allah) with sincerity, he is innocent of al-shirk (الشرك). And whoever departs from the world without associating anything with Allah, he shall enter Paradise.' Then He recited this verse: إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ ما دُونَ ذلِكَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ (Indeed Allah does not forgive that a partner should be ascribed to Him, but He forgives anything besides that to whomever He wishes) from your followers (شيعتك) and those who love you, O Ali." The Commander of the Faithful (peace and prayers be upon him) said: "So I said, 'O Messenger of Allah, is this for my followers (شيعتي)?' He said: 'Yes, by my Lord, it is indeed for your followers (شيعتك).'"

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ (Indeed Allah does not forgive that a partner should be ascribed to Him), meaning that He does not forgive the one who disbelieves in the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (prayers of Allah be upon him). وَيَغْفِرُ ما دُونَ ذلِكَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ (but He forgives anything besides that to whomever He wishes), meaning the one who follows Ali (peace be upon him).

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the minimum degree by which a person becomes an associator (مشرك). He said: "The one who innovates an opinion (رأياً) and loves or hates based upon it."

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "There is no verse in the Quran more beloved to me than His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ ما دُونَ ذلِكَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ (Indeed Allah does not forgive that a partner should be ascribed to Him, but He forgives anything besides that to whomever He wishes)."

وَمَنْ يُشْرِكْ بِاللهِ فَقَدِ افْتَرى إِثْماً عَظِيماً (and whoever ascribes a partner to Allah has indeed fabricated a tremendous sin.) He has committed that beside which other sins (الآثام) are considered insignificant. And al-iftira' (الافتراء) (fabrication/slander), just as it is applied to speech (القول), is also applied to action (الفعل).

footnote (١) الطوال بالضم الطويل (منه ره)

footnote (٢) في الحديث : لا صورة ولا تخطيط ولا تحديد وفيه : أن قوماً يصنعون الله بالصورة والتخطيط أي انه ذو أضلاع (م)

(٤٩) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ يُزَكُّونَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ (49) Have you not regarded those who claim sanctity for themselves? It was revealed concerning the Jews and the Christians when they said, "We are the sons of Allah and His beloved ones," ** وَقالُوا لَنْ يَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّةَ إِلَّا مَنْ كانَ هُوداً أَوْ نَصارى ** and they said, 'None shall enter paradise except one who is a Jew or a Christian.' This is according to al-Majma' narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). And al-Qummi said: They are those who named themselves with (الصديق) (the Truthful), (الفاروق) (the Distinguisher), and (ذي النورين) (Possessor of the Two Lights). بَلِ اللهُ يُزَكِّي مَنْ يَشاءُ Rather, Allah purifies whomever He wishes, because He is the Knower of what man conceals within himself, whether good or evil, unlike others. وَلا يُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلاً ** and they will not be wronged so much as a thread. [Meaning] the slightest and smallest injustice, which is the thread (الخيط) in the groove of the date stone (النواة), used as a proverb for insignificance.

(٥٠) انْظُرْ كَيْفَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ (50) See how they fabricate a lie against Allah! in their claim that they are the sons of Allah and pure (أزكياء) in His sight ** وَكَفى بِهِ إِثْماً مُبِيناً by the fabrication. (And that is a sufficient manifest sin.) (٥١) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا نَصِيباً مِنَ الْكِتابِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْجِبْتِ وَالطَّاغُوتِ. (51) Have you not regarded those who were given a share of the Book, believing in al-jibt and al-ṭāghūt? Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Jews when the polytheists of the Arabs asked them: "Is our religion better, or the religion of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم?" They replied: "Rather, your religion is better." He said, and it is also narrated: that it was revealed concerning those who usurped the right of the Family of Muhammad صلوات الله عليهم أجمعين and envied their status. And Al-Ayyashi narrated from Al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding: ** بِالْجِبْتِ وَالطَّاغُوتِ (al-jibt and al-ṭāghūt), [it refers to] so-and-so and so-and-so. I say: Al-Jibt (الجبت) is originally the name of an idol, and thus it is used for everything worshipped besides Allah, the Exalted. And al-Ṭāghūt ( وَالطَّاغُوتِ) is applied to Satan and to every falsehood, whether it is an object of worship or otherwise. وَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (And they say concerning those who disbelieve) for their sake and among them هؤُلاءِ (These) is a reference to them ** أَهْدى مِنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا سَبِيلاً (are better guided on the way than the faithful), meaning: more upright in religion and more rightly guided in path. In Al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Baqir عليه السلام: They say concerning the Imams of misguidance and the callers to the Fire: "These are better guided than the Family of Muhammad صلوات الله عليهم أجمعين."

(٥٢) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَعَنَهُمُ اللهُ وَمَنْ يَلْعَنِ اللهُ فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُ نَصِيراً. (They are those whom Allah has cursed, and whomever Allah curses, you will never find any helper for him.) (٥٣) أَمْ لَهُمْ نَصِيبٌ مِنَ الْمُلْكِ (Do they have a share in the kingdom?) This is a denial (إنكار), meaning they do not possess that. ** فَإِذاً لا يُؤْتُونَ النَّاسَ نَقِيراً ** (If so, they would not give the people [even] a speck.) Meaning, if they had a share in the kingdom (الملك), then they would not give the people [even] a speck (نقيراً).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ** أَمْ لَهُمْ نَصِيبٌ مِنَ الْمُلْكِ (Do they have a share in the kingdom?) means the Imamate (الإمامة) and the Caliphate (الخلافة). He said: "And we are the people (الناس) whom Allah meant, and the naqir (النقير) is the speck (النقطة) which is in the middle of the date-stone (النواة)."

I say: Perhaps the specification (التخصيص) is because the world was created for them, and the Caliphate (الخلافة) is their right. Thus, if the wealth were in their hands, the rest of the people (سائر الناس) would not benefit from it; and if they were prevented from their rights, the rest of the people would also be prevented. Therefore, it is as if they are all the people (كل الناس). And it has been narrated: "We are the people (الناس), and our followers (شيعتنا) are like the people (أشباه الناس), and the rest of the people (سائر الناس) are nasnās (نسناس)."

(٥٤) أَمْ يَحْسُدُونَ النَّاسَ عَلى ما آتاهُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ. (54) Do they envy the people for what Allah has given them of His grace?

In al-Kafi, al-Ayyashi, and others, narrated from them (peace be upon them) in several narrations: "We are the envied ones (المحسودون) concerning whom Allah said [this verse], due to what Allah has given us of the Imamate (الإِمامة)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The intent behind "the people" (الناس) is the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: فَقَدْ آتَيْنا آلَ إِبْراهِيمَ الْكِتابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَآتَيْناهُمْ مُلْكاً عَظِيماً (We have given the progeny of Abraham the Book and wisdom, and We have given them a great sovereignty). Thus, it is not unlikely that Allah should give them [the progeny of Muhammad] the like of what He gave them [the progeny of Abraham], for they (١) were their paternal cousins (بني عمهم).

And in al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** الْكِتابَ (the Book) means Prophethood (النبوّة); ** وَالْحِكْمَةَ (and wisdom) means understanding (الفهم) and judgment (القضاء); and ** وَالْمُلْكَ الْعَظِيمَ (and the great sovereignty) means the obligatory obedience (الطاعة المفروضة).

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Meaning, He made among them the Messengers (الرسل), the Prophets (الأنبياء), and the Imams (الأئمة). So how can they affirm it concerning the progeny of Abraham (آل إبراهيم) yet deny it concerning the progeny of Muhammad (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them all)? And He said: The great sovereignty (الملك العظيم) is that He made Imams among them; whoever obeys them obeys Allah, and whoever disobeys them disobeys Allah. That is the great sovereignty (الملك العظيم).

(٥٥) فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ آمَنَ بِهِ وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ صَدَّ عَنْهُ (So there are some of them who believe in him, and some of them who bar [others] from him.) He turned away and did not believe. ** وَكَفى بِجَهَنَّمَ سَعِيراً (Hell is enough as a blaze.) A blazing fire (ناراً مسعورة) with which they will be tormented. Meaning, if they are not hastened with the punishment, then what has been prepared for them of the blaze (سعير) of Hell (جهنم) is sufficient for them.

(٥٦) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِنا سَوْفَ نُصْلِيهِمْ ناراً. (56) Indeed, those who disbelieve in Our signs, We shall soon make them enter a Fire. Al-Qummi said: The signs (الآيات) are the Commander of the Faithful and the Imams, may the blessings of Allah be upon them all. كُلَّما نَضِجَتْ جُلُودُهُمْ بَدَّلْناهُمْ جُلُوداً غَيْرَها لِيَذُوقُوا الْعَذابَ. Whenever their skins are cooked, We shall replace them with other skins so that they may taste the punishment. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Ibn Abi al-Awja' asked him about this Ayah, saying: "What is the sin of the other (skin)?" He (peace be upon him) said: "Woe unto you! It is it, and it is other than it." He (Ibn Abi al-Awja') said: "Then give me an example of that from the affairs of this world (الدنيا)." He (peace be upon him) said: "Yes. Do you not see that if a man took a brick (لَبِنَة), broke it, and then returned it to its mold (مَلْبَن), it is it, and it is other than it?" And Al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him) that which is similar in meaning.

(٥٧) إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَزِيزاً (57) Indeed Allah is all-mighty, He is not prevented from what He wills. ** حَكِيماً all-wise. He punishes according to His wisdom. وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ سَنُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً لَهُمْ فِيها أَزْواجٌ مُطَهَّرَةٌ وَنُدْخِلُهُمْ ظِلًّا ظَلِيلاً But those who have faith and do righteous deeds—We shall admit them into gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them forever. Therein they will have immaculate spouses, and We shall admit them into a perpetual shade. perpetual (دائماً), which the sun does not abolish. It is derived from (الظل) (shade) for emphasis, just as it is said (ليل أليل) (a dark night) and (شمس شامس) (a scorching sun). And the mention of the promise (of reward) was only delayed after the threat (of punishment) because it is (1) secondary (بالعرض).

(٥٨) ** إِنَّ اللهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تُؤَدُّوا الْأَماناتِ إِلى أَهْلِها** (58) Indeed Allah commands you to deliver the trusts to their owners.

In al-Kafi and other sources, in several narrations: The address is directed to the Imams (peace be upon them), commanding each of them to deliver [the trust] to the Imam who comes after him and to entrust it to him. Then, this applies generally to all other trusts (الأمانات).

And in it (al-Kafi) and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He meant us, that the first Imam should deliver to the one after him the knowledge (العلم), the books (الكتب), and the weapon (السلاح).

And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): It concerns everyone who is entrusted with a trust (أمانة) from among the trusts (الأمانات): the trusts of Allah, His commands and prohibitions, and the trusts of His servants concerning what some of them entrust to others, whether it be wealth (المال) or otherwise.

And narrated from them (peace be upon them) in several narrations: Do not look at the length of a man's bowing (ركوع) and prostration (سجوده), for that is something he has become accustomed to, and if he were to abandon it, he would feel uneasy about it. Rather, look at the truthfulness of his speech (صدق حديثه) and the delivery of his trust (أداء أمانته).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If the one who struck Ali (peace be upon him) with the sword and killed him were to entrust me, seek my advice, and consult me, and I accepted that from him, I would certainly deliver the trust (الأمانة) to him. There are many reports (أخبار) with this meaning. وَإِذا حَكَمْتُمْ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ أَنْ تَحْكُمُوا بِالْعَدْلِ. (and when you judge between people, that you judge with fairness.)

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the justice (العدل) which is in your hands (1).

And in another narration by al-Ayyashi: ** أَنْ تَحْكُمُوا بِالْعَدْلِ (that you judge with fairness) when it appears, and then ** أَنْ تَحْكُمُوا بِالْعَدْلِ (that you judge with fairness) when it becomes manifest in your hands. ** إِنَّ اللهَ نِعِمَّا يَعِظُكُمْ**. (Indeed, excellent is what Allah advises you.)

Al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning us. وَاللهُ الْمُسْتَعانُ إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ سَمِيعاً بَصِيراً (and Allah is the One Whose help is sought. Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing) regarding your sayings (أقوالكم), your deeds (أفعالكم), and what you do concerning your trusts (أماناتكم).

(1) His saying, "due to its being secondary (بالعرض)," meaning that the promise (الوعد) in this context is mentioned secondarily (بالعرض) because the primary purpose (الغرض الأصلي) therein is the threat (الوعيد), or because the promise (الوعد), when considering its causes (أسبابه), is secondary (عرضي) to man, since the secondary habit (العادة الثانوية) in him is evil (الشر), or for other reasons, so reflect upon this.

(٥٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْكُمْ. (O you who have faith! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle and those vested with authority among you.) In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "He meant us specifically, commanding all believers until the Day of Resurrection to obey us."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked whether obedience to the Successors (الأوصياء) is obligatory. He said: "Yes, they are those concerning whom Allah said: ** أَطِيعُوا اللهَ (Obey Allah)..." (the Ayah), and Allah said: ** إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ (Your guardian is only Allah)... (the Ayah).

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: He said it was revealed concerning Ali ibn Abi Talib, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace and blessings be upon them). It was said: "People say, 'Why did He not name Ali and his Household (Ahl al-Bayt) in His Book?'" He replied: "Tell them: The Prayer (الصلاة) was revealed, but Allah did not name for them three or four (units of prayer) until the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) explained that to them. And the Zakat was revealed to him, but He did not name for them one dirham out of every forty dirhams until the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was the one who explained that to them. And the Hajj was revealed, but He did not say 'circumambulate seven times' until the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was the one who explained that to them. And ** أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْكُمْ was revealed, and it was revealed concerning Ali, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them). Then the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said concerning Ali: 'For whomever I am his Mawla (مولاه), Ali is his Mawla (مولاه).' And He said: 'I commend to you the Book of Allah and my Household (Ahl al-Bayt), for I asked Allah not to separate them until they meet me at the Pond (al-Hawd), and He granted me that.' And He said: 'Do not teach them, for they are more knowledgeable than you.' And He said: 'They will never lead you out of a door of guidance, nor will they lead you into a door of misguidance.' If the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) had remained silent and had not clarified who his Household (Ahl al-Bayt) were, the family of so-and-so (آل فلان) and the family of so-and-so (آل فلان) would have claimed it. But Allah revealed in His Book, confirming His Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): ** إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً (Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification.) Thus, Ali, al-Hasan, al-Husayn, and Fatimah (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them all) were included. The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) brought them under the cloak (al-Kisa') in the house of Umm Salamah, and then the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'O Allah, every Prophet has a family (Ahl) and a weighty trust (Thiqal), and these are my Household (Ahl al-Bayt) and my weighty trust (Thiqal).' Umm Salamah asked: 'Am I not one of your family?' He replied: 'You are upon goodness, but these are my Household (Ahl al-Bayt) and my weighty trust (Thiqal).' (The Hadith)."

And al-Ayyashi added: Āl Abbas (آل عبّاس) and Āl Aqil (آل عقيل) before His saying: "the family of so-and-so (آل فلان) and the family of so-and-so (آل فلان)."

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the pillars (دعائم) upon which Islam is built, such that if one adheres to them, the deed is purified, and ignorance of what is unknown thereafter does not harm. He said: "The testimony that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), and the affirmation of what He brought from Allah, and the right in wealth which is Zakat, and the Wilayah (الولاية) which Allah commanded, which is the Wilayah (ولاية) of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them all). For the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Whoever dies without knowing his Imam dies the death of Jahiliyyah (جاهلية).' Allah the Almighty said: ** أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْكُمْ. So it was Ali (peace be upon him), then after him al-Hasan, then after him al-Husayn, then after him Ali ibn al-Husayn, then after him Muhammad ibn Ali, and thus the matter continues, for the earth is not set right except by an Imam (peace be upon them)." (The Hadith).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from Sulaym ibn Qays al-Hilali, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked what is the minimum by which a man becomes misguided (ضالاً). He said: "That he does not know the one whose obedience Allah commanded, whose Wilayah (ولايته) He made obligatory, whom He made His Proof (Hujjah) on His earth, and His witness over His creation." He (Sulaym) said: "Who are they, O Commander of the Faithful?" He replied: "They are those whom Allah linked to Himself and His Prophet, saying: ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْكُمْ**." He (Sulaym) said: "So I kissed his head and said: 'You have clarified for me, relieved me, and removed every doubt that was in my heart.'"

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari (رضي الله عنه), who said: When this Ayah was revealed, I said: "O Messenger of Allah, we know Allah and His Messenger, but who are أُولِي الْأَمْرِ (those vested with authority) whose obedience Allah linked to your obedience?" He said: "They are my successors (خلفائي), O Jabir, and the Imams of the Muslims after me. The first of them is Ali ibn Abi Talib, then al-Hasan, then al-Husayn, then Ali ibn al-Husayn, then Muhammad ibn Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), who is known in the Torah as al-Baqir (الباقر). You will meet him, O Jabir, so when you meet him, convey my greetings to him. Then al-Sadiq Ja'far ibn Muhammad, then Musa ibn Ja'far, then Ali ibn Musa, then Muhammad ibn Ali, then Ali ibn Muhammad, then al-Hasan ibn Ali, then my namesake Muhammad and my kunya (كنيي), the Proof (Hujjah) of Allah on His earth and His remnant among His servants, the son of al-Hasan ibn Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). He is the one through whose hands Allah will conquer the Easts and Wests of the earth. He is the one who will disappear from his followers (Shi'ah) and his friends (Awliya') in an occultation (Ghaybah) in which none will remain firm in affirming his Imamate except those whose hearts Allah has tested for faith." Jabir said: "I asked him: 'O Messenger of Allah, will his followers benefit from him during his occultation (Ghaybah)?'" He said: "Yes, by the One Who sent me with Prophethood! They will be illuminated by his light and benefit from his Wilayah (ولايته) during his occultation, just as people benefit from the sun even if clouds conceal it. O Jabir, this is from the hidden secret of Allah and the stored knowledge of Allah, so conceal it except from its people." And the narrations concerning this meaning in the commonly circulated and reliable books are too numerous to count.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Know Allah by Allah the Almighty, and the Messenger by the Message (al-Risalah), and Ūlī al-Amr (وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ) by good conduct (al-Ma'ruf), justice (al-Adl), and benevolence (al-Ihsan)."

In al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "There is no obedience to one who disobeys Allah. Obedience is only for Allah, His Messenger, and the Guardians of the Authority (Wulat al-Amr). Allah only commanded obedience to the Messenger because He is infallible (Ma'sum) and purified (Mutahhar), and He does not command disobedience to Him. And He only commanded obedience to Ūlī al-Amr because they are infallible (Ma'sumun) and purified (Mutahharun), and they do not command disobedience to Him. فَإِنْ تَنازَعْتُمْ (Then if you dispute) O commanded ones, ** فِي شَيْءٍ (concerning anything) regarding matters of the religion, ** فَرُدُّوهُ (refer it) — so refer concerning it — ** إِلَى اللهِ (to Allah), meaning to the decisive verses (Muhkam) of His Book, ** وَالرَّسُولِ (and the Apostle) by asking Him about it during His time, and by adhering to His Sunnah and referring to the one whom He commanded to be referred to after Him, for that is a referral to Him."

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) who said: "It was revealed: 'Then if you dispute concerning anything, refer it to Allah, and to the Apostle, and to Ūlī al-Amr (أولي الأمر) among you.'"

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He recited this Ayah thus: "Then if you fear a dispute concerning a matter, refer it to Allah, and to the Apostle, and to Ūlī al-Amr (أولي الأمر) among you." He said: "It was revealed thus. How could Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, command them to obey the Guardians of the Authority (Wulat al-Amr) and then permit disputing with them? That was only said to the commanded ones (al-Ma'murun) to whom it was said: أَطِيعُوا اللهَ (Obey Allah)."

In Nahj al-Balaghah, concerning the meaning of the Khawarij when they rejected the arbitration by men: "We did not appoint men as arbitrators; rather, we appointed the Quran as arbitrator. This Quran is merely a written line between two covers; it does not speak with a tongue, and it must have an interpreter (Tarjuman). It is only men who speak on its behalf. When the people called us to arbitrate between us by the Quran, we were not the party turning away from the Book of Allah the Almighty. And He, the Glorified, said: ** فَإِنْ تَنازَعْتُمْ فِي شَيْءٍ فَرُدُّوهُ إِلَى اللهِ وَالرَّسُولِ (Then if you dispute concerning anything, refer it to Allah and the Apostle). Referring it to Allah means that we judge by His Book, and referring it to the Messenger means that we adhere to His Sunnah. If the Book of Allah judges with truth, then we are the most entitled people to it, and if the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) judges, then we are His descendants (awlad) who are most entitled to it." And He (peace be upon him) said in His covenant to al-Ashtar: "Refer to Allah and His Messenger whatever burdens you of calamities and whatever matters become ambiguous to you. For Allah, the Glorified, said to a people whose guidance He loved: ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنْ تَنازَعْتُمْ فِي شَيْءٍ فَرُدُّوهُ إِلَى اللهِ وَالرَّسُولِ (O you who have faith! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle and those vested with authority among you. Then if you dispute concerning anything, refer it to Allah and the Apostle). Thus, referring it to Allah is adhering to the decisive verses (Muhkam) of His Book, and referring it to the Messenger is adhering to His comprehensive Sunnah, which does not cause division."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon him) in his sermon: "And obey us, for our obedience is obligatory, as it is linked to the obedience of Allah and the obedience of His Messenger. Allah the Almighty said: ** أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنْ تَنازَعْتُمْ فِي شَيْءٍ فَرُدُّوهُ إِلَى اللهِ وَالرَّسُولِ (Obey Allah and obey the Apostle and those vested with authority among you. Then if you dispute concerning anything, refer it to Allah and the Apostle). And He said: ** وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلى أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَنْبِطُونَهُ مِنْهُمْ وَلَوْ لا فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ لَاتَّبَعْتُمُ الشَّيْطانَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً (Had they referred it to the Apostle and to those vested with authority among them, those of them who are competent to investigate would have surely ascertained it. And were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you, you would have surely followed Satan, [all] except a few.) ** إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ (if you have faith in Allah and the Last Day), for faith necessitates that. ** ذلِكَ ** (That) — meaning the referral — ** خَيْرٌ وَأَحْسَنُ تَأْوِيلاً (is better and fairer in the outcome) than your interpretation without referral.

(1) Perhaps He meant by the justice (al-Adl) that is in our hands the clear Muhammadan Law (al-Shari'ah al-Muhammadiyyah al-Bayda') in addition to the rest of the abrogated laws (al-Shara'i' al-Mansukhah). For even though each of them was justice and truth, the command in this Ayah relates specifically to it, setting aside the abrogation of the rest. And judging according to them after the completion of the religion by this Law is a judgment of falsehood if it contradicts it. Or, the address is to the Shi'ah, so what is meant by what is in their hands is the Alawi doctrine (al-Madhhab al-Alawi) in contrast to the false doctrines (al-Madhahib al-Batilah). Or, what is meant are the rulings taken from the apparent meaning of the Quran and the Sunnah based on Taqiyyah (التقية) from the Infallibles (peace be upon them), or the observance and overlooking of the distortions that affect it until the Master of this Affair (Sahib Hadha al-Amr) appears, whereby it will be rectified and guided. The second narration points to this, for His saying (peace be upon him): ** أَنْ تَحْكُمُوا بِالْعَدْلِ (that you judge with justice) when He appears—the apparent meaning is that the time of appearance refers to universal justice (al-Adl al-Kulli). And His saying: "Then that you judge with justice when it appears in your hands" means whatever you are capable of after universal justice has not yet appeared, meaning the time before the era of al-Qa'im (may Allah hasten his reappearance). The first was mentioned first, even though it was not present, and the second was delayed despite its presence, due to the importance and nobility of the first and its natural precedence. Or, what is meant is the justice that you are capable of or that you know.

(٦٠) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ يَزْعُمُونَ أَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا بِما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَما أُنْزِلَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَتَحاكَمُوا إِلَى الطَّاغُوتِ وَقَدْ أُمِرُوا أَنْ يَكْفُرُوا بِهِ وَيُرِيدُ الشَّيْطانُ أَنْ يُضِلَّهُمْ ضَلالاً بَعِيداً (Have you not regarded those who claim that they believe in what has been sent down to you, and what was sent down before you? They desire to take their disputes to the Taghut for judgment, though they were commanded to disbelieve in it, and Satan desires to lead them astray into a deep error.)

Al-Qummi (said): It was revealed concerning al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, who disputed with a man from the Jews over a garden. Al-Zubayr said: "We agree to the judgment of Ibn Shaybah the Jew." And the Jew said: "We agree to the judgment of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." So Allah revealed [this Ayah].

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه الصلاة والسلام: Any man between whom and his brother there is a dispute (مماراة) concerning a right, and [the brother] calls him to one of his brethren (إخوانه) to judge between them, but he refuses and insists on taking the case to these [others], he is in the position of those concerning whom Allah said: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ يَزْعُمُونَ (Have you not regarded those who claim) the Ayah.

And narrated from him عليه السلام: He was asked about two men from our companions (أصحابنا) between whom there is a dispute (منازعة) concerning a debt (دين) or an inheritance (ميراث), and they take their case to the ruler (السلطان) or to the judges (القضاة). Is that permissible (يحل)? He said: "Whoever takes his case to the الطاغوت (tyrant/false authority) and judgment is given in his favor, he only takes forbidden gain (سحتاً) (1), even if his right is established, because he took it by the judgment of the الطاغوت, and Allah has commanded that it be disbelieved in (أن يكفر به)." It was asked: "What should they do?" He said: "Look among yourselves for one who has narrated our حديث (traditions), looked into our lawful (حلالنا) and unlawful (حرامنا), and knows our rulings (أحكامنا). Be content with him as a judge (حكماً), for I have appointed him as a ruler (حاكماً) over you. If he judges according to our ruling (بحكمنا) and [the other party] does not accept it from him, then he has belittled the ruling of Allah and rejected us. And the one who rejects us has rejected Allah, and this is on the boundary (حدّ) of associating partners with Allah (الشرك بالله)."

(٦١) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعالَوْا إِلى ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ وَإِلَى الرَّسُولِ رَأَيْتَ الْمُنافِقِينَ يَصُدُّونَ (61) When they are told, 'Come to what Allah has sent down and to the Apostle,' you see the hypocrites turning away from you يعرضون ** عَنْكَ صُدُوداً. they turn away with aversion.**

Al-Qummi said: They are the enemies of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them all); this verse applies to them.

(٦٢) فَكَيْفَ (62) So how will be their condition إِذا أَصابَتْهُمْ مُصِيبَةٌ when a disaster befalls them meaning, a punishment reaches them from Allah بِما قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ because of what their hands have sent ahead due to seeking judgment from other than you and showing displeasure towards your ruling. ثُمَّ جاؤُكَ then they come to you so they apologize to you ** يَحْلِفُونَ بِاللهِ إِنْ أَرَدْنا swearing by Allah, 'We did not intend' by seeking judgment from other than you إِلَّا إِحْساناً except good treatment which is easing the burden on you وَتَوْفِيقاً and reconciliation'** between the two disputants through mediation, and we did not intend to oppose you.

footnote (١) السحت هو بضمتين واسكان الثاني تخفيفاً كل ما لا يحل كسبه واشتقاقه من السحت وهو الاستيصال يقال سحته وأسحته أي استأصله ويسمى الحرام به لأنّه يعقب عذاب الاستيصال وقيل لأنّه لا بركة فيه وقيل انه يسحت مروة الإنسان (مجمع) (1) السحت (al-suḥt) is with two ḍammahs (بضمتين) and the second letter is made quiescent (إسكان الثاني) for lightness (تخفيفاً). It is everything whose earning is unlawful (لا يحل كسبه). Its derivation is from السحت (al-saḥt), which means eradication (الاستيصال). It is said: سحته and أسحته, meaning he eradicated it (استأصله). The unlawful (الحرام) is named by this because it is followed by the punishment of eradication (عذاب الاستيصال). And it is said: because there is no blessing (بركة) in it. And it is said: because it eradicates the human's chivalry (مروة الإنسان) (مجمع).

(٦٣) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ يَعْلَمُ اللهُ ما فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ (Those are the ones whose hearts Allah knows what is in them) of polytheism (الشرك) and hypocrisy (النفاق). ** فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ (so disregard them) meaning: do not punish them due to an interest (مصلحة) in sparing them.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): The word of wretchedness (الشقاء) has already preceded against them, and the punishment (العذاب) has already preceded for them. ** وَعِظْهُمْ (and advise them) with your tongue, ** وَقُلْ لَهُمْ فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ (and speak to them concerning themselves) concerning the matter of their souls, or privately with them, for advice (النصيحة) given in secret is more effective (أنجع). ** قَوْلاً بَلِيغاً (an eloquent word) that affects them, such as frightening them with execution (القتل) and eradication (الاستيصال) if hypocrisy (النفاق) becomes manifest from them.

(٦٤) وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلَّا لِيُطاعَ بِإِذْنِ اللهِ (We did not send any apostle but that he should be obeyed by Allah’s leave.) By this, He alerted (people) to the fact that whoever is not pleased with His judgment is a disbeliever (كافر), even if he manifests Islam (الإِسلام). وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ إِذْ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ (Had they, when they wronged themselves) by hypocrisy (بالنفاق) ** جاؤُكَ (come to you) repenting (تائبين) ** فَاسْتَغْفَرُوا اللهَ (and sought forgiveness of Allah) sincerely (مخلصين) ** وَاسْتَغْفَرَ لَهُمُ الرَّسُولُ (and the Apostle had sought forgiveness for them) by apologizing to him until he stood up as an intercessor (شفيعاً) for them, لَوَجَدُوا اللهَ تَوَّاباً رَحِيماً (they would have surely found Allah clement and merciful.) They would have known Him as accepting their repentance and bestowing grace upon them with mercy (بالرحمة).

(٦٥) فَلا وَرَبِّكَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ حَتَّى يُحَكِّمُوكَ فِيما شَجَرَ بَيْنَهُمْ (65) But no, by your Lord! They will not believe until they make you a judge in their disputes, regarding what they disputed over and what became mixed up between them. From this (الشجر) is the tree (الشجر) due to the intertwining of its branches (أغصانه). ** ثُمَّ لا يَجِدُوا فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ حَرَجاً مِمَّا قَضَيْتَ then do not find in their hearts any discomfort on account of your verdict, a constriction (ضيقاً) regarding what you judged (حكمت به). ** وَيُسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيماً and submit in full submission. and submit to you (وينقادوا لك) with full submission (انقياداً) both outwardly (بظاهرهم) and inwardly (وباطنهم).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Allah addressed the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in His Book regarding His saying: ** وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ إِذْ ظَلَمُوا ** (And if, when they wronged themselves) — and he recited up to His saying: ** فِيما شَجَرَ بَيْنَهُمْ (in their disputes). He said: Regarding what they mutually agreed upon (تعاقدوا عليه): that if Allah causes Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to die, they would not return this matter (هذا الأمر) to Banu Hashim. ثُمَّ لا يَجِدُوا فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ حَرَجاً مِمَّا قَضَيْتَ (then do not find in their hearts any discomfort on account of your verdict) against them, whether it be execution (القتل) or pardon (العفو). ** وَيُسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيماً (and submit in full submission).

Al-Qummi said: جاؤُكَ يا علي (They came to you, O Ali). He said: This is how it was revealed (هكذا نزلت).

(٦٦) ** وَلَوْ أَنَّا كَتَبْنا عَلَيْهِمْ أَنِ اقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ (66) Had We prescribed for them: 'Slay yourselves,' either by exposing themselves to jihad (الجهاد) or as the Children of Israel (بنو إسرائيل) did. أَوِ اخْرُجُوا مِنْ دِيارِكُمْ ما فَعَلُوهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ مِنْهُمْ or 'Leave your homes,' they would not have done it, except a few of them. This is a profound rebuke to them. And the word قليلاً (few) was also recited (as an accusative). وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ فَعَلُوا ما يُوعَظُونَ بِهِ لَكانَ خَيْراً لَهُمْ وَأَشَدَّ تَثْبِيتاً Had they done what they were advised to do, it would have been better for them and more confirming (of their faith), for their faith. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** وَلَوْ (Had) the people of opposition (أهل الخلاف) ** فَعَلُوا (done it). And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ** ما يُوعَظُونَ بِهِ** (what they were advised to do) concerns Ali (peace be upon him). He said: "It was revealed thus."

(٦٧) وَإِذاً لَآتَيْناهُمْ مِنْ لَدُنَّا أَجْراً عَظِيماً. (67) and then We would have surely given them a great reward from Our presence, (٦٨) وَلَهَدَيْناهُمْ صِراطاً مُسْتَقِيماً (68) and We would have surely guided them to a straight path. By traversing this path, they would reach the Gardens of Holiness (جنات القدس), and the doors of the Unseen (الغيب) would be opened for them, for whoever acts upon what he knows, Allah grants him the inheritance of the knowledge of what he did not know. (٦٩) وَمَنْ يُطِعِ اللهَ وَالرَّسُولَ فَأُولئِكَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ (69) Whoever obeys Allah and the Apostle—they are with those whom Allah has blessed, including the prophets, —those who are in the highest ‘Illiyyin (أعلى عليين)— وَالصِّدِّيقِينَ (and the truthful ones), who were truthful in their sayings and their actions, وَالشُّهَداءِ (and the martyrs), whose souls and bodies were slain by the Greater and Lesser Jihad (الجهاد الأكبر والأصغر), وَالصَّالِحِينَ (and the righteous), whose state became sound (صلحت حالهم) and whose way became straight. ** وَحَسُنَ أُولئِكَ رَفِيقاً and how excellent companions they are! This contains the meaning of astonishment (التعجب), as if it were said: "How excellent are those as companions!" The companion (الرفيق), like the friend (الصديق), is the same whether singular or plural. Allah has encouraged the believers to obey Allah and obey His Messenger through this promise. And what an excellent promise it is! May Allah grant us its attainment through His grace (مَنّه) and generosity (جوده).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Help us through piety (الورع), for whoever among you meets Allah, the Almighty, with piety, there will be relief (فرجاً) for him with Allah. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: ** وَمَنْ يُطِعِ اللهَ وَالرَّسُولَ," and He recited the Ayah, then He said: "The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is from us, and the Truthful (الصديق), the Martyrs (الشهداء), and the Righteous (الصالحون) are from us."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The believer (المؤمن) is of two types: a believer in Allah with the conditions He stipulated upon him—such a one is with the prophets, the truthful ones, the martyrs, and the righteous, and how excellent companions they are! That one is among those who intercede (يشفع) but for whom intercession is not sought (لا يشفع له). That one is among those whom the terrors of this world (أهوال الدنيا) and the terrors of the Hereafter (أهوال الآخرة) do not afflict. And a believer whose foot has slipped (زلت به قدم)—that one is like the tender shoot (خامة) of the crop; however the wind turns it (كفأته), it turns over (انكفى). That one is among those whom the terrors of this world and the terrors of the Hereafter afflict, and intercession is sought for him (يشفع له), yet he is upon goodness (على خير)."

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "Allah has indeed mentioned you in His Book, saying: فَأُولئِكَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللهُ... the Ayah. Thus, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in the Ayah is the prophets (النبيون), and we, in this position, are the truthful ones (الصديقون) and the martyrs (الشهداء), and you are the righteous (الصالحون). So, call yourselves by righteousness (الصلاح) just as Allah has called you."

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): "It is incumbent upon Allah to make our follower (ولينا) a companion (رفيقاً) to the prophets, the truthful ones, the martyrs, and the righteous. And how excellent companions they are!"

And in al-Uyun, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "For every nation there is a truthful one (صديق) and a discriminator (فاروق). And the truthful one and the discriminator of this nation is ‘Ali ibn Abi Talib, may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him."

(٧٠) ذلِكَ الْفَضْلُ مِنَ اللهِ (70) (That is the grace of Allah) is a favor bestowed upon them by Allah, following their reward (ثوابهم). ** وَكَفى بِاللهِ عَلِيماً (and Allah suffices as Knower) of the measures of grace (الفضل) and the deservingness of those who are worthy of it (أهله). (٧١) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا خُذُوا حِذْرَكُمْ (71) (O you who have faith! Take your precautions) Be vigilant (تيقظوا) and prepare yourselves for the enemies. الحَذَر and الحِذر are synonymous (بمعنى). It is said: "He took his precaution (أخذ حذره)" when he became vigilant (تيقظ) and protected himself (تحفظ) from what is feared (المخوف), as if he made precaution (الحذر) the tool (آلته) by which he protects his soul (نفسه). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ** خُذُوا (Take) means your weapons (أسلحتكم). He named the weapons precaution (حذراً) because through them, one guards against what is feared (المحذور). ** فَانْفِرُوا ** (Then march forth) means: Go out for Jihad (الجهاد). This is its exegesis (تفسيره), and its interpretation (تأويله) extends to all good deeds (الخيرات كلها). ثُباتٍ (in detachments) means scattered groups (جماعات متفرقة), the plural of ثبة (ثبة). ** أَوِ انْفِرُوا جَمِيعاً (or march forth all together) means gathered together (مجتمعين), as one company (٢) (كوكبة واحدة), and do not fail one another (ولا تتخاذلوا). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): الثبات (al-thubāt) means the detachments (السرايا), and الجميع (al-jamī') means the army (العسكر).

(٧٢) وَإِنَّ مِنْكُمْ لَمَنْ لَيُبَطِّئَنَ (72) And indeed among you is he who will surely lag behind. It admits of both the intransitive and the transitive meanings, and they are the hypocrites (المنافقون). If a disaster befalls you, such as killing or defeat, he—meaning the one who lags behind (المبطئ)—says: ** قَدْ أَنْعَمَ اللهُ عَلَيَّ إِذْ لَمْ أَكُنْ مَعَهُمْ شَهِيداً (Allah has certainly favoured me, since I was not present with them), meaning: present (حاضراً), so that what befell them would befall me. Al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If the people of the East and the West had uttered this statement, they would have thereby exited faith (الإِيمان). However, Allah has named them believers (مؤمنين) due to their affirmation (إقرارهم). And in a narration: He named them believers (مؤمنين), yet they are not believers (مؤمنين), nor do they possess any honor (كرامة).

footnote (١) وسمه يسمه وسماً فأقسم والوسام والسمة بكسرهما ما وسم به الحيوان (ق) (١) Wasamahu yasimuhu wasman (وَسَمَهُ يَسِمُهُ وَسْماً) means he swore (فَأَقْسَمَ). Al-Wisām (الوسام) and al-Simah (السمة), both with a kasrah (بكسرهما), are what the animal is marked with (ما وسم به الحيوان) (ق).

footnote (٢) الكوكبة الجماعة (ق) (٢) Al-Kawkabah (الكوكبة) is the group (الجماعة) (ق).

(٧٣) ** وَلَئِنْ أَصابَكُمْ فَضْلٌ مِنَ اللهِ (73) But if a grace from Allah befalls you (meaning) like a conquest (فتح) and spoils (غنيمة), ** لَيَقُولَنَ he will surely say out of regret (تحسراً): ** كَأَنْ لَمْ تَكُنْ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُ مَوَدَّةٌ as if there were no affection between you and him (This is) an interjection (اعتراض) between the saying (القول) and the thing said (المقول). ** يا لَيْتَنِي ‘I wish I (meaning: O people (يا قوم), I wish I had been with them, so that I might achieve a great triumph. The interjection (الاعتراض) alerts (us) to the weakness of their belief (عقيدتهم), and that this saying of theirs is the saying of one who has no connection (مواصلة) between you and him, and he only desires to be with you merely for the sake of wealth (المال).

(٧٤) فَلْيُقاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ الَّذِينَ يَشْرُونَ يبيعون الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا بِالْآخِرَةِ (74) So let those fight in the way of Allah who sell the life of this world for the Hereafter. meaning the sincere ones (المخلصين) who sacrifice their souls in seeking the Hereafter. ** وَمَنْ يُقاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ فَيُقْتَلْ أَوْ يَغْلِبْ فَسَوْفَ نُؤْتِيهِ أَجْراً عَظِيماً Whoever fights in the way of Allah, and is killed or is victorious, We shall grant him a great reward. It is said that He promised him the great reward, whether he was victorious (غَلب) or defeated (غُلب), as an encouragement for fighting and a refutation of their saying: ** قَدْ أَنْعَمَ اللهُ عَلَيَّ إِذْ لَمْ أَكُنْ مَعَهُمْ شَهِيداً Allah has been gracious to me, since I was not present with them. And He only said: ** فَيُقْتَلْ أَوْ يَغْلِبْ (and is killed or is victorious) as an alert that the Mujahid (المجاهد) ought to stand firm in the battle until he honors his soul with martyrdom (الشهادة) or the religion with triumph (الظفر) and victory (الغلبة), and that his primary intention should not be the killing itself, but rather the exaltation of the Truth (إعلاء الحق) and the strengthening of the Religion (إعزاز الدين).

In al-Kafi and other sources, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): "Above every righteousness (برّ) is righteousness, until one is killed in the way of Allah. If he is killed in the way of Allah, there is no righteousness above it." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever is killed in the way of Allah, Allah will not make Him aware of any of his evil deeds (سيئاته)." And narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): "The martyr (الشهيد) has seven qualities (خصال) from Allah: The first is that with the first drop of his blood, every sin is forgiven for him. The second is that his head falls into the lap of his two wives from the Hur al-'In (الحور العين), and they wipe the dust from his face, saying: 'Welcome to you,' and he says the like of that to them. The third is that he is clothed from the garments of Paradise. The fourth is that the keepers (خزنة) of Paradise rush (1) with every sweet fragrance, [competing] as to which of them will take him. The fifth is that he sees his station (منزله). The sixth is that it is said to his soul (روحه): 'Hasten in Paradise wherever you wish.' And the seventh is that he looks upon the Face of Allah, and that is the comfort (الراحة) for every prophet (نبي) and martyr (شهيد)."

(٧٥) وَما لَكُمْ And what excuse do you have ** لا تُقاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ that you do not fight in the way of Allah, in His obedience, and the strengthening of His religion, and the exaltation of His word, ** وَالْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ and in the way of the oppressed (الْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ), by freeing them from captivity and protecting them from the enemy, or [it means] in their deliverance, or it is in the accusative case (نصب) due to specification (على الاختصاص), for the way of Allah encompasses every good, and this is the greatest of them, ** مِنَ الرِّجالِ وَالنِّساءِ وَالْوِلْدانِ الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنا أَخْرِجْنا مِنْ هذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ الظَّالِمِ أَهْلُها وَاجْعَلْ لَنا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ وَلِيًّا وَاجْعَلْ لَنا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ نَصِيراً**

(75) And what is wrong with you that you do not fight in the way of Allah, and for the oppressed men, women, and children, who say, 'Our Lord, bring us out of this town whose people are oppressors, and appoint for us a guardian from Your presence, and appoint for us a helper from Your presence'?

It was said that they are those who embraced Islam in Mecca, but the polytheists prevented them from migrating (الهجرة). So they remained among them (1), suffering harm from them. They used to supplicate to Allah for deliverance and seek His help. So He facilitated the departure of some of them to Medina, while others remained until the Conquest (الفتح), until Allah appointed for them the best guardian (ولي) and the best helper (ناصر), who is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. So He governed them with the best governance (التولي) and granted them the most powerful victory (النصر). They had included their children in their supplications, seeking to draw down the mercy of Allah through the prayer of their little ones who had committed no sin.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from them (the two Imams) عليهما السلام regarding this Ayah, that they both said: "We are those ones."

footnote (١) تبتدره خزنة الجنة أي يسرعون إليه (م)

(٧٦) الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يُقاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ (76) Those who have faith fight in the way of Allah, in that by which they reach Allah ** وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يُقاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ الطَّاغُوتِ and those who are faithless fight in the way of the Taghut. in that which leads them to Satan (الشيطان) ** فَقاتِلُوا أَوْلِياءَ الشَّيْطانِ إِنَّ كَيْدَ الشَّيْطانِ كانَ ضَعِيفاً So fight the friends of Satan; indeed, Satan’s guile is weak. This is an encouragement (ترغيب) for the believers toward fighting, a strengthening (تشجيع) for them, and an alert (تنبيه) to them that they are the friends (أولياء) of Allah and that He is their Helper (ناصرهم).

(٧٧) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ قِيلَ لَهُمْ كُفُّوا أَيْدِيَكُمْ ** from fighting ** وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكاةَ and busy yourselves with what you were commanded to do. That was when they were in Mecca, and they used to wish that permission for it would be granted to them. (77) Have you not regarded those to whom it was said, 'Restrain your hands, and maintain the prayer and give the zakat'?

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): كُفُّوا أَيْدِيَكُمْ (Restrain your hands) means: restrain your tongues (ألسنتكم). And He said: Are you not content that you maintain the prayer, give the zakat, restrain yourselves (تكفوا), and enter Paradise?

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): By Allah, you are the people of this Ayah: فَلَمَّا كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقِتالُ إِذا فَرِيقٌ مِنْهُمْ يَخْشَوْنَ النَّاسَ كَخَشْيَةِ اللهِ They fear the disbelievers (الكفّار) lest they kill them, just as they fear Allah lest He sends down His might (بأسه) upon them. (But when fighting was prescribed for them, behold, a group of them feared the people as they fear Allah,)

** أَوْ أَشَدَّ خَشْيَةً وَقالُوا رَبَّنا لِمَ كَتَبْتَ عَلَيْنَا الْقِتالَ لَوْ لا أَخَّرْتَنا إِلى أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ**. or with a greater fear, and they said, 'Our Lord! Why did You prescribe fighting for us? Why did You not defer us until a near term?'

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): كُفُّوا أَيْدِيَكُمْ ** (Restrain your hands) [refers to restraining] with al-Hasan (peace be upon him), كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقِتالُ (fighting was prescribed for them) [refers to fighting] with al-Husayn (peace be upon him). ** إِلى أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ (until a near term) [refers] to the emergence (خروج) of the Qa'im (القائم), for victory (الظفر) is with him. قُلْ مَتاعُ الدُّنْيا قَلِيلٌ (Say, 'The enjoyment of the world is little,) [it is] swift in passing away وَالْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِمَنِ اتَّقى وَلا تُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلاً (and the Hereafter is better for him who is Godwary, and you will not be wronged so much as the speck on a date-stone.') And they will not be diminished of the slightest thing (أدنى شيء) from your reward (ثوابكم), so do not turn away from it. And it was recited in the third person (بالغيبة).

footnote (١) And the *أظهر الناس* (most apparent of people) are their midst (*أوساطهم*). From this is the *hadith* of the Imams (*الأئمة*): "We move about on the earth *بين أظهركم* (among your midst)," meaning: in your midst (*في أوساطكم*). Similar to this is *أقاموا بين ظهرانيّهم* and *بين أظهرهم* (they resided among their midst), meaning: among them (*بينهم*) by way of seeking support (*الاستظهار*) and relying upon them (*الاستناد إليهم*). The *alif* and the open *nun* (*ألف ونون مفتوحة*) were added to it for emphasis (*تأكيداً*). Its meaning is a back (*ظهراً*) from them in front of them, and a back (*ظهراً*) behind them, so they are surrounded (*مكنوفون*) from their sides by harm (*اذىً*). Then it became widespread until it was used generally for residing among a people (*الإقامة بين القوم مطلقاً*) (*مجمع*).

(٧٨) أَيْنَما تَكُونُوا يُدْرِكْكُمُ الْمَوْتُ وَلَوْ كُنْتُمْ فِي بُرُوجٍ مُشَيَّدَةٍ (78) Wherever you may be, death will overtake you, even if you be in fortified towers. [meaning] in plastered palaces or high [towers]. ** وَإِنْ تُصِبْهُمْ حَسَنَةٌ meaning a blessing, such as fertility (خصب) (1) يَقُولُوا هذِهِ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ وَإِنْ تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ meaning an affliction (بلية), such as drought (قحط) ** يَقُولُوا هذِهِ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ they attribute the ill omen to you (يطيروا بك). Say: ** قُلْ كُلٌّ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ He expands and restricts according to His Will (ارادته). ** فَما لِهؤُلاءِ الْقَوْمِ لا يَكادُونَ يَفْقَهُونَ حَدِيثاً so that they may know that Allah is the Expander (الباسط) and the Restrictor (القابض), and all His actions are issued from wisdom (حكمة) and correctness (صواب).

(٧٩) ** ما أَصابَكَ O human ** مِنْ حَسَنَةٍ of blessing ** فَمِنَ اللهِ as a favor from Him, and a grace, and a test. For everything that the servant performs of worship does not suffice to repay even the smallest blessing from His favors. ** وَما أَصابَكَ مِنْ سَيِّئَةٍ of affliction ** فَمِنْ نَفْسِكَ because it is the cause of it, having brought it upon itself through acts of disobedience (المعاصي). And this does not contradict His saying: ** قُلْ كُلٌّ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ for all of it is from Him in terms of creation (إيجاداً) and delivery (إيصالاً). Except that the good (الحسنة) is benevolence (احسان) and a test (امتحان), and the evil (السيئة) is requital (مجازاة) and retribution (انتقام). Allah the Almighty has said: ** ما أَصابَكُمْ مِنْ مُصِيبَةٍ فَبِما كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَيَعْفُوا عَنْ كَثِيرٍ**.

** ما أَصابَكَ مِنْ حَسَنَةٍ فَمِنَ اللهِ وَما أَصابَكَ مِنْ سَيِّئَةٍ فَمِنْ نَفْسِكَ وَأَرْسَلْناكَ لِلنَّاسِ رَسُولاً وَكَفى بِاللهِ شَهِيداً ** (79) Whatever good befalls you, it is from Allah, and whatever evil befalls you, it is from yourself. We sent you as an apostle to mankind, and Allah is sufficient as a witness.

In al-Qummi, narrated from them (peace be upon them): Indeed, the good deeds (الحسنات) in the Book of Allah are of two types (وجهين): one is health (الصحة), safety (السلامة), and abundance in provision (السعة في الرزق); and the other is actions (الأفعال), as the Almighty has said: ** مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثالِها (Whoever brings a good deed will receive ten times its like). Likewise are the evil deeds (السيئات): some of them are fear (الخوف), sickness (المرض), and hardship (الشدة), and some of them are the actions (الأفعال) for which they are punished.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace and blessings be upon him): Just as the origin of blessings (بادي النعم) is from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, Who bestowed them upon you, so too is the evil (الشر) from your own selves, even if His decree (قدره) has run its course concerning it.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: (Allah said): O son of Adam, by My Will (بمشيتي) you were the one who willed for yourself whatever you willed, and by My Power (بقوتي) you performed My obligatory duties (فرائضي), and by My Blessing (بنعمتي) you gained strength for your disobedience (معصيتي). I made you hearing, seeing, and strong. ** ما أَصابَكَ مِنْ حَسَنَةٍ فَمِنَ اللهِ وَما أَصابَكَ مِنْ سَيِّئَةٍ فَمِنْ نَفْسِكَ (Whatever good befalls you, it is from Allah, and whatever evil befalls you, it is from yourself). That is because I am more deserving of your good deeds (حسناتك) than you are, and you are more deserving of your evil deeds (سيئاتك) than I am. That is because I am not questioned concerning what I do, but they are questioned.

And al-Ayyashi narrated what is close to it (the previous meaning): **وَأَرْسَلْناكَ لِلنَّاسِ رَسُولاً وَكَفى بِاللهِ شَهِيداً ** (We sent you as an apostle to mankind, and Allah is sufficient as a witness) to that, so it is not proper for anyone to depart from your obedience.

(٨٠) وَمَنْ يُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطاعَ اللهَ (80) Whoever obeys the Apostle certainly obeys Allah. because, in reality, he is the conveyor (مُبَلِّغ), and the Commander and Prohibitor is Allah. And it is narrated that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever loves me has loved Allah, and whoever obeys me has obeyed Allah." So the hypocrites said: "He has committed polytheism (الشِّرْك), (1) while he prohibits it. He only desires that we take him as a lord, just as the Christians took Jesus." So, [the verse] was revealed. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The peak (ذِرْوَة), (2) the hump (سَنَام), the key, the gate of all things, and the pleasure of the All-beneficent is obedience to the Imam (الإِمَام) after knowing him." Then He said: "Indeed, Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, says: مَنْ يُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطاعَ اللهَ. (Whoever obeys the Apostle certainly obeys Allah.)"

I say: The Imam (الإِمَام) in this narration includes the Messenger (الرَّسُول), and the ruling concerning the rest of the Imams (الأَئِمَّة) is the same as His ruling, because they are all His successors (خُلَفَاؤُهُمْ). That is because the Imam (peace be upon him) is a conveyor (مُبَلِّغ), just as the Messenger (الرَّسُول) is a conveyor (مُبَلِّغ). وَمَنْ تَوَلَّى meaning: turns away (أَعْرَضَ) from His obedience ** فَما أَرْسَلْناكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَفِيظاً meaning: you do not guard their deeds (أَعْمَالَهُمْ) and hold them accountable for them. For indeed, upon you is only the conveyance (الْبَلَاغ), and upon Us is the reckoning (الْحِسَاب).

(٨١) ** وَيَقُولُونَ طَاعَةٌ فَإِذَا بَرَزُوا مِنْ عِنْدِكَ بَيَّتَ طَائِفَةٌ مِنْهُمْ غَيْرَ الَّذِي تَقُولُ وَاللَّهُ يَكْتُبُ مَا يُبَيِّتُونَ فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ وَكَفَى بِاللَّهِ وَكِيلًا ** (81) And they say, "Obedience!" But when they leave your presence, a group of them plot by night other than what you say. And Allah writes down what they plot by night. So disregard them, and put your trust in Allah, and Allah is sufficient as a trustee.

And they say: when you command them with an order, "Obedience!" (meaning) our command and our concern is obedience. But when they leave your presence (خرجوا - they went out), a group of them plot by night (دبروا ليلاً - they planned by night) other than what you say, (meaning) contrary to what you said and commanded, or contrary to the acceptance and guarantee of obedience that they showed you. And Allah writes down what they plot by night (3): He confirms it in their scrolls (صحايفهم) for recompense (للمجازات). So disregard them, and put your trust in Allah, and Allah is sufficient as a trustee. Allah suffices you against their evil.

(٨٢) ** أَفَلا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ (82) Do they not contemplate the Quran? They reflect upon its meanings and gain insight into what is in it. وَلَوْ كانَ مِنْ عِنْدِ غَيْرِ اللهِ (And if it were from other than Allah)—from the speech of humans, as they claimed it to be— لَوَجَدُوا فِيهِ اخْتِلافاً كَثِيراً (they would surely find in it much discrepancy), such as the contradiction of meanings (تناقض المعاني), the disparity of composition (تفاوت النظم), and some of it departing from eloquence (الفصاحة) and from conformity with reality (مطابقة الواقع), and so forth.

(٨٣) ** وَإِذا جاءَهُمْ أَمْرٌ مِنَ الْأَمْنِ أَوِ الْخَوْفِ (83) When a matter of security or fear comes to them, [a matter] which necessitates security or fear, أَذاعُوا بِهِ they broadcast it. They divulged it. It was said that there were a group among the weak Muslims who, when news reached them concerning the detachments (سرايا) (1) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, or when the Messenger informed them of what was revealed to Him regarding a promise of victory or a warning concerning the disbelievers (الكفرة), they would broadcast it, and their broadcasting was a source of corruption (مفسدة). ** وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ ** (Had they referred it) ردوا ذلك الأمر ** إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلى أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَنْبِطُونَهُ مِنْهُمْ ** to the Apostle and to those vested with authority among them, those of them who investigate would have ascertained it. It was said: meaning, they extract its management (تدبيره) through their experiences and insights (أنظارهم).

In al-Jawami', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: They are the infallible Imams (الأئمة المعصومون) عليهم السلام.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida عليه السلام: He means the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد) صلوات الله عليهم, and they are those who investigate (يستنبطون) from the Quran and know the lawful (الحلال) and the unlawful (الحرام), and they are Allah's proof (حجة الله) over His creation.

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: Whoever places the guardianship (ولاية) of Allah and the people of extracting the knowledge of Allah (أهل استنباط علم الله) in other than the chosen people (أهل الصفوة) from the households of the Prophets (بيوتات الأنبياء) has indeed opposed the command of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and has made the ignorant (الجهال) the guardians of Allah's command (ولاة أمر الله) and those who undertake burdens without guidance (هدىً). They claimed that they are the people of extracting the knowledge of Allah (أهل استنباط علم الله), so they lied against Allah and deviated from the testament (وصية) of Allah and obedience to Him. Thus, they did not place the favor (فضل) of Allah where Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, placed it. So they went astray and led their followers astray, and they will have no proof (حجة) on the Day of Resurrection. ** وَلَوْ لا فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ ** Were it not for Allah’s grace upon you and His mercy, by sending the Messengers and sending down the Book.

In al-Jawami', narrated from them عليهم السلام: The grace (فضل الله) of Allah and His mercy (ورحمته) are the Prophet and Ali صلوات الله عليهما.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: ** فَضْلُ اللهِ ** (Allah’s grace) is His Messenger, and ** وَرَحْمَتُهُ ** (His mercy) is the Imams (الأئمة).

And narrated from al-Kazim عليه السلام: The mercy (الرحمة) is the Messenger of Allah, and the grace (الفضل) is Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام. ** لَاتَّبَعْتُمُ الشَّيْطانَ ** you would surely have followed Satan, through disbelief (الكفر) and misguidance (الضلال), ** إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** except a few. And they are the people of penetrating insights (البصائر النافذة).

(1) And in the Hadith: "He sent a sarīyah (سرية)." It is with the fatḥ (فتح) on the sīn (السين), a fa‘īlah (فعلية) form meaning a portion of the army, ranging from five individuals up to three hundred or four hundred, which is the vanguard of the army directed toward the enemy. The plural forms are sarāyā (سرايا) and sarāyāt (سرايات), like ‘aṭīyah (عطية), ‘aṭāyā (عطايا), and ‘aṭāyāt (عطايات). It was said they were named thus because they are the essence (خلاصة) and the best (خيارهم) of the army, or derived from the precious (النفيس) and noble (السري) thing. It was also said they were named thus because they execute [their mission] secretly (سراً) or hiddenly (خفية). [The author] said in al-Nihayah: This is not the correct derivation (الوجه) because the lām (لام) of al-sirr (السر) [secret] is a rā’ (راء), while this [word, sarīyah] has a yā’ (ياء). (Majma‘)

(٨٤) فَقاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ (84) So fight in the way of Allah; if they leave you alone ** لا تُكَلَّفُ إِلَّا نَفْسَكَ you are responsible only for yourself. So advance toward Jihad (الجهاد) even if no one assists you, for Allah will grant you victory, not the armies (الجنود).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah obligated the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with what He did not obligate anyone else among His creation. He obligated him to go out against all people alone, by himself, if he did not find a group (فئة) to fight alongside him. He did not obligate this upon anyone among His creation before him or after him. Then he recited this Ayah.

And al-Ayyashi narrated that which is similar in meaning: It is narrated that when Abu Sufyan returned on the Day of Uhud, he promised the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم the season of Lesser Badr (بدر الصغرى). The people disliked this and were reluctant when the appointed time arrived, so [this Ayah] was revealed. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم went out, and there were only seventy men with him. Had no one followed him, he would have gone out alone. وَحَرِّضِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ and urge on the faithful. Since your only duty regarding their affair is urging them on (التحريض). ** عَسَى اللهُ أَنْ يَكُفَّ بَأْسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا Maybe Allah will restrain the might of the faithless, and they are Quraysh. He did restrain their might when it occurred to Abu Sufyan, and he said, "This is a year of drought (مجدب)," and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned safely with those who were with him. وَاللهُ أَشَدُّ بَأْساً (and Allah is stronger in might) than Quraysh ** وَأَشَدُّ تَنْكِيلاً and more terrible in retribution. (meaning: punishment (تعذيباً)). This is a threat and a reproach (تقريع) (1) for those who did not follow him.

** مَنْ يَشْفَعْ شَفاعَةً حَسَنَةً (85) Whoever intercedes with a good intercession whereby he observes the right of a Muslim, either by repelling evil from him or bringing good to him, seeking the Face of Allah. And among this is supplicating for the believer. ** يَكُنْ لَهُ نَصِيبٌ مِنْها there shall be a share for him out of it as a reward for it. ** وَمَنْ يَشْفَعْ شَفاعَةً سَيِّئَةً and whoever intercedes with an evil intercession which is what is contrary to that, and among this is supplicating against the believer. ** يَكُنْ لَهُ كِفْلٌ مِنْها there shall be a portion for him out of it a share of its burden (وزرها) equal to it in measure, for the kifl (الكفل) is the share (النصيب) and the like (المثل). ** وَكانَ اللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُقِيتاً (2) And Allah is the Maintainer of all things. powerful (مقتدراً) and Preserver (حفيظاً), giving according to the need, for al-Muqīt (المقيت) has come with both meanings.

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his forefathers (upon them be peace and blessings), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever commands a good deed (بمعروف) or forbids an evil deed (عن منكر), or guides to good or points it out, he is a partner. And whoever commands evil (بسوء) or guides to it or points it out, he is a partner."

And in al-Jawami', narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever supplicates for his Muslim brother in his absence (بظهر الغيب), it is answered for him, and the angel says to him: 'And for you are two likes of that share (النصيب) in that.'"

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): When the angels hear the believer supplicating for his brother in his absence (بظهر الغيب) and mentioning him with good, they say: "What an excellent brother you are to your brother! You supplicate for him with good while he is absent from you and you mention him with good. Allah the Almighty has given you twice what you asked for him, and praised you twice what you praised him, and you have the superiority (الفضل) over him." And when they hear him mentioning his brother with evil and supplicating against him, they say: "What a wretched brother you are to your brother! Stop, O you whose sins and faults are covered (المستّر على ذنوبه وعورته). Be gentle (أربع) (1) with yourself, and praise Allah Who has covered you, and know that Allah knows His servant better than you do."

I say: أربع على نفسك (Be gentle with yourself) means: stop (قف) and restrain yourself (وامسك), and do not trouble yourself (ولا تتعب نفسك), derived from raba'a (ربع) like mana'a (منع).

(1) al-Taqrī' (التقريع): al-Ta'nīf (التعنيف). He treated him harshly (عنفه تعنيفاً), meaning he blamed him (لامه) and reproached him (عتب عليه). And al-Ta'nīf (التعنيف) is expression (التعبير) and blame (اللوم) (م).

(2) It is said that al-Muqīt (المقيت) is the Powerful (المقتدر), the Giver of the sustenance (أقوات) of creation, derived from aqātahu (أقاته), meaning He gave him his sustenance (قوته), and it is a dialectical variant of qātahu (قاته). And al-Muqīt (المقيت) is one of His exalted Names, and He is the Powerful (المقتدر), the Preserver (الحافظ), and the Witness (الشاهد) (مجمع).

** وَإِذا حُيِّيتُمْ بِتَحِيَّةٍ فَحَيُّوا بِأَحْسَنَ مِنْها أَوْ رُدُّوها** (86) When you are greeted with a salute, return it with a better one, or return it in kind.

Al-Qummi said: It is the greeting (السلام) and other forms of righteousness (البر).

In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): When one of you sneezes, say: "May Allah have mercy upon you (يرحمكم الله)," and he should say: "May Allah forgive you and have mercy upon you (يغفر الله لكم ويرحمكم)." The Almighty Allah said: ** وَإِذا حُيِّيتُمْ بِتَحِيَّةٍ** (the Ayah).

In al-Manaqib: A slave-girl came to Al-Hasan (peace be upon him) with a sprig of basil (ريحان). He (peace be upon him) said: "You are free for the sake of Allah." He was asked about that, and he replied: "Allah the Almighty has disciplined us, for He said: ** وَإِذا حُيِّيتُمْ بِتَحِيَّةٍ (the Ayah), and the better return for it was her emancipation (إعتاقها)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace and blessings be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The greeting (السلام) is voluntary (تطوع), and the response (الرد) is obligatory (فريضة)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): If one person from a group offers the greeting (سلم), it suffices for them, and if one person responds (ردّ), it suffices for them.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The few begin the greeting (السلام) to the many, and the rider begins the greeting to the walker, and the owners of mules begin the greeting to the owners of donkeys, and the owners of horses begin the greeting to the owners of mules. In one narration: The young greets the old, and the passerby greets the seated person. In another: If a group meets a group, the smaller number greets the larger number, and if one person meets a group, the one person greets the group.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): And among humility (التواضع) is that you greet (تسلم) whomever you meet. And he said: The stingy person (البخيل) is he who is stingy with the greeting (السلام).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: The person closest (أولى) to Allah and His Messenger is the one who begins the greeting (بالسلام).

And narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Verily, Allah loves the spreading (افشاء) of the greeting (السلام).

I say: The spreading (الإفشاء) is that one greets whomever he meets, whoever that person may be.

And narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): There are three upon whom the response of a group is given, even if the recipient is one person: upon sneezing, one says: "May Allah have mercy upon you (يرحمكم الله)," even if there is no one else with him; and when a man greets another man, he says: "Peace be upon you (السلام عليكم);" and when a man supplicates for another man, he says: "May Allah grant you well-being (عافاكم الله)," even if he is alone, for there is someone else with him.

I say: What is meant by the response (الرد) is that which includes the initiation (الابتداء), and by "someone else" (الغير) at the end of the narration, He meant the angels (الملائكة). The better response (الرد بالأحسن) in the greeting (السلام) is to add "and the mercy of Allah (ورحمة الله)." If the one offering the greeting (المسلم) has already said that, then one adds "and His blessings (وبركاته)," which is the limit, so one responds in kind (بالمثل).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Commander of the Faithful (prayers and peace of Allah be upon him) passed by a group of people and greeted them.
