They said: "Upon you be peace, and the mercy of Allah, and His blessings, and His forgiveness, and His pleasure (عليك السلام ورحمة الله وبركاته ومغفرته ورضوانه)." The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said to them: "Do not exceed with us what the angels said to our father Abraham (peace be upon him). They only said: وَرَحْمَتُ اللهِ وَبَرَكاتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ (and the mercy of Allah and His blessings are upon you, members of the household)."

And it is narrated: A man said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Peace be upon you (السلام عليك)." He said: "And upon you be peace and the mercy of Allah (وعليك السلام ورحمة الله)." Another said: "Peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah (السلام عليك ورحمة الله)." He said: "And upon you be peace and the mercy of Allah and His blessings (وعليك السلام ورحمة الله وبركاته)." Another said: "Peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah and His blessings (السلام عليك ورحمة الله وبركاته)." He said: "And upon you (وعليك)." The man said: "You have diminished me! Where is what Allah said?"—and he recited the Ayah. He [the Prophet] said: "You did not leave me any excess (فضلاً), so I responded to you in kind (مثله)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever says "Peace be upon you (السلام عليكم)," that is ten good deeds (حسنات). And whoever says "Peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah (السلام عليكم ورحمة الله)," that is twenty good deeds. And whoever says "Peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah and His blessings (السلام عليكم ورحمة الله وبركاته)," that is thirty good deeds.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Part of the completion (تمام) of the greeting (التحية) for the resident (المقيم) is shaking hands (المصافحة) (1), and the completion of the greeting (التسليم) upon the traveler (المسافر) is embracing (المعانقة) (2).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Do not initiate the greeting (التسليم) to the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب), and if they greet you, say: "And upon you (وعليكم)."

And narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Three people are not greeted: the one walking to the Friday prayer (الجمعة), the one in the toilet (بيت الخلاء), and the one in the bathhouse (حمام).

In al-Khisal, narrated from him [Al-Sadiq], from his father (peace be upon him): Do not greet the Jews (اليهود), nor the Christians (النصارى), nor the Zoroastrians (المجوس), nor the idolaters (عبدة الأوثان), nor those at tables where wine is drunk (شراب الخمر), nor the player of chess (الشطرنج) and backgammon (النرد), nor the effeminate man (المخنث) (3), nor the poet who slanders chaste women (يقذف المحصنات), nor the one praying (المصلي)—and that is because the one praying cannot respond to the greeting, for the greeting from the one offering it (المسلم) is voluntary (تطوع), but the response to him is obligatory (فريضة)—nor the consumer of usury (آكل الربا), nor a man sitting upon excrement (غائط), nor the one who is in the bathhouse (الحمام), nor the openly immoral person (الفاسق المعلن بفسقه). إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ حَسِيباً ** (Indeed Allah is Reckoner of all things). He will hold you accountable (يحاسبكم) for the greeting (التحية) and other matters.

(1) Raba' (ربع) like mana' (منع) waqf (وقف) (Q).

٨٧ ** اللهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ اللهِ حَدِيثاً (87) Allah—there is no god except Him—will surely gather you on the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt. And who is truer in statement than Allah? A denial (إنكار). ٨٨ ** فَما لَكُمْ فِي الْمُنافِقِينَ فِئَتَيْنِ ** (88) What is the matter with you that you are two factions regarding the hypocrites? What is the matter with you that you have split into two groups concerning them, and have not agreed upon their disbelief. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning a people who came from Mecca and declared Islam, then returned to Mecca and declared polytheism (الشرك), then traveled to al-Yamamah. The Muslims differed regarding waging war against them due to their disagreement concerning their Islam and their polytheism (شركهم). وَاللهُ أَرْكَسَهُمْ (١) He returned them to disbelief by abandoning them, so they reverted. ** بِما كَسَبُوا أَتُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَهْدُوا مَنْ أَضَلَّ اللهُ That you make him one of the guided ones. وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللهُ فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُ سَبِيلاً إلى الهدى. (1) because of what they have earned. Do you seek to guide him whom Allah has led astray? And whomever Allah leads astray, you will never find a way for him to guidance.

(٨٩) وَدُّوا لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ كَما كَفَرُوا (89) They are eager that you should disbelieve just as they have disbelieved, They wished that you would disbelieve like their disbelief, ** فَتَكُونُونَ سَواءً** so that you will be equal. in misguidance.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: And indeed, the devils among mankind possess cunning (حيلة), deceit (مكراً), trickery (خدائع), and whispering (وسوسة) from some of them to others. They desire, if they are able, to turn the people of truth away from that with which Allah has honored them, namely, the support (النصرة) in the religion of Allah, the religion whose people Allah has not made the devils among mankind to be among, intending that the enemies of Allah and the people of truth should become equal in doubt (الشك), denial (الإِنكار), and rejection (التكذيب), so that they will be equal, just as the Almighty Allah described in His Book.

وَدُّوا لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ كَما كَفَرُوا فَتَكُونُونَ سَواءً فَلا تَتَّخِذُوا مِنْهُمْ أَوْلِياءَ حَتَّى يُهاجِرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ They are eager that you should disbelieve just as they have disbelieved, so that you will be equal. So do not take them as allies until they migrate in the way of Allah. So do not take them as allies, even if they believe, until they migrate with a sound migration (هجرة صحيحة) that is for Allah, not for any worldly purpose (غرض من أغراض الدنيا). ** فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا ** But if they turn away, from the faith accompanied by the upright migration, then seize them and kill them wherever you find them, like the rest of the disbelievers. ** وَلا تَتَّخِذُوا مِنْهُمْ وَلِيًّا وَلا نَصِيراً and do not take any of them as a friend or a helper. Meaning: Avoid them completely (رأساً) and do not accept from them any guardianship (ولاية) or help (نصرة).

(٩٠) إِلَّا الَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ إِلى قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ مِيثاقٌ (except those who join a people between whom and you there is a treaty) This is an exception to His saying: "then seize them and kill them," meaning: except those who reach (يَنْتَهُونَ إِلَى) a people who have made a covenant (عَهْدًا) with you and abandon fighting you. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He is Hilal ibn 'Uwaym al-Aslami, who made a pact (وَاثَقَ) on behalf of his people with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He said in his truce (مُوَادَعَتِهِ): "We shall not wrong (نَحِيف) (2) anyone, O Muhammad, who comes to us, nor shall you wrong anyone who comes to you." So, Allah, the Glorified, forbade exposing anyone to whom they had given a covenant. أَوْ جاؤُكُمْ حَصِرَتْ صُدُورُهُمْ ** (or who come to you, their breasts constrained) meaning: constricted (ضَاقَتْ). In al-'Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means constriction (الضِّيق). أَنْ يُقاتِلُوكُمْ أَوْ يُقاتِلُوا قَوْمَهُمْ (from fighting you or fighting their own people.) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning Banu Mudlij. They came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "Indeed, our breasts are constrained (حَصِرَتْ صُدُورُنَا) from testifying that you are the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. So, we are neither with you nor with our people against you." So, He promised them until He finished dealing with the Arabs, then He would invite them; if they responded, [good], otherwise He would fight them. In al-Qummi, regarding His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: ** وَدُّوا لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ كَما كَفَرُوا (They wish you would disbelieve just as they have disbelieved) to the end of the verse: It was revealed concerning Ashja' (1) and Banu Damrah (2). Their story was that when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم set out for Badr for the appointed time, He passed near their lands. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had previously demanded (صَادَرَ) (3) from Banu Damrah and made a truce (وَادَعَهُمْ) (4) with them. The Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Messenger of Allah, Banu Damrah are near us, and we fear they might oppose us by going to Medina or assist Quraysh against us. Should we not start with them?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "No, they are the most dutiful of the Arabs toward their parents (أَبَرُّ الْعَرَبِ بِالْوَالِدَيْنِ), the best at maintaining kinship ties (أَوْصَلَهُمْ لِلرَّحِمِ), and the most faithful to their covenant (أَوْفَاهُمْ بِالْعَهْدِ)." The lands of Ashja' were near the lands of Banu Damrah, and they were a clan of Kinanah. There was an alliance (حِلْف) between Ashja' and Banu Damrah based on mutual consideration (الْمُرَاعَاة) and security (الْأَمَان). The lands of Ashja' became barren (أَجْدَبَتْ) while the lands of Banu Damrah became fertile (أَخْصَبَتْ), so Ashja' moved to the lands of Banu Damrah. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was informed of their movement toward Banu Damrah, He prepared to march toward Ashja' to fight them, due to the truce that existed between Him and Banu Damrah. Then Allah revealed: ** وَدُّوا لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ كَما كَفَرُوا (They wish you would disbelieve just as they have disbelieved) the verse. Then He made an exception for Ashja' and said: ** إِلَّا الَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ إِلى قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ مِيثاقٌ أَوْ جاؤُكُمْ حَصِرَتْ صُدُورُهُمْ أَنْ يُقاتِلُوكُمْ أَوْ يُقاتِلُوا قَوْمَهُمْ (except those who join a people between whom and you there is a treaty, or who come to you, their breasts constrained from fighting you or fighting their own people.) the verse. The settlements (مَحَالُّهَا) of Ashja' were al-Bayda', al-Hill, and al-Mustabah. They had approached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and they feared, due to their proximity to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, that He might send someone to raid them. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had also feared that they might inflict some harm upon His borders (أَطْرَافِهِ), and He intended to march against them. While He was thus engaged, Ashja' arrived, led by Mas'ud ibn Ruhaylah, numbering seven hundred. They camped at Shi'b Sal' (1) in the month of Rabi' in the sixth year. The Messenger of Allah called Usayd ibn Husayn and said to him: "Go with a group of your companions until you see what brought Ashja'." Usayd went out with three men from his companions and stood before them, asking: "What brought you?" Mas'ud ibn Ruhaylah, who was the chief of Ashja', stood up, greeted Usayd and his companions, and said: "We came to make a truce (لِنُوَادِعَ) with Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." Usayd returned to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and informed Him. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The people feared that I would raid them, so they desired peace between me and them." Then He sent them ten camel-loads of dates, presenting them before Him, and then said: "How excellent a thing is a gift (الْهَدِيَّة) before a need (الْحَاجَة)." Then He came to them and said: "O assembly of Ashja', what brought you?" They said: "Our dwelling place is close to yours, and there is no group among our people fewer in number than us. We are constrained (ضِقْنَا) from fighting you due to the proximity of our dwelling, and we are constrained from fighting our people due to our small number among them. So, we came to make a truce with you." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم accepted this from them and made a truce with them. They stayed for their day, then returned to their lands. This verse was revealed concerning them: ** إِلَّا الَّذِينَ يَصِلُوا ** (except those who join) the verse. وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَسَلَّطَهُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ (Had Allah wished, He would have given them power over you) by strengthening their hearts, expanding their breasts, and removing the terror from them, فَلَقاتَلُوكُمْ (and they would have surely fought you.) and they would not have refrained from you. فَإِذَا اعْتَزَلُوكُمْ فَلَمْ يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ (So if they keep out of your way and do not fight you) meaning: if they do not expose themselves to you, ** وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَمَ (and offer you peace,) meaning: submission (الِاسْتِسْلَام) and compliance (الِانْقِيَاد), فَما جَعَلَ اللهُ لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سَبِيلاً (then Allah has not given you any way against them.) meaning: He has not permitted you to seize or kill them. In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The practice (السِّيرَة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم before the revelation of Surah al-Bara'ah was that He would only fight those who fought Him, and only wage war against those who waged war against Him and sought Him out. And Allah, the Glorified, had revealed concerning this: ** فَإِنِ اعْتَزَلُوكُمْ فَلَمْ يُقاتِلُوكُمْ وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَمَ فَما جَعَلَ اللهُ لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سَبِيلاً (So if they keep out of your way and do not fight you and offer you peace, then Allah has not given you any way against them.) The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would not fight anyone who withdrew from Him and kept away, until Surah Bara'ah was revealed to Him, and He was commanded to kill the polytheists, whether they withdrew or did not withdraw, except for those whom the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had made a covenant with on the Day of the Conquest of Mecca for a specific period, among them Safwan ibn Umayyah and Suhayl ibn 'Amr. The narration is long and is mentioned in its entirety in Surah Bara'ah.

(١) وَاللهُ أَرْكَسَهُمْ بِما كَسَبُوا (And Allah has overturned them because of what they earned) meaning: He returned them to their disbelief (كُفْرِهِمْ) due to their deeds (أَعْمَالِهِمْ), derived from al-Raks (الرَّكْس), which is the returning of something inverted. And arkastuhu (أَرْكَسْتُهُ) with the alif means I returned it upon its head. Rakasahu (رَكَسَهُ) and arkasahu (أَرْكَسَهُ) have the same meaning. And rakastu (رَكَسْتُ) the thing raksan (رَكْسًا) (from the qatl pattern) means I inverted it and returned its beginning to its end. And so-and-so irtakasa (ارْتَكَسَ) in a matter from which he had been saved (م)

(٢) In the Hadith: "We, the assembly of Prophets, do not testify to injustice (الْحَيْف)." Meaning: against oppression (الظُّلْم) and tyranny (الْجَوْر), such as testifying against someone who withholds from some of his children but not others, or against someone who divorces outside the Sunnah, or against usury (الرِّبَاء), and the like. The one who is unjust (الْحَائِف) in his judgment is the one who is tyrannical (الْجَائِر) in it. And hāfa yahīfu (حَافَ يَحِيفُ) means he acted unjustly (جَارَ). From this is injustice (الْحَيْف) in a bequest (الْوَصِيَّة), which is one of the major sins (الْكَبَائِر). It has been interpreted as bequeathing a third, and perhaps this is meant for emphasis (مَجْمَع)

(١) Ashja' ibn Rayth ibn Ghatafan, the father of a tribe (ق)

(٢) Banu Damrah, the clan of 'Amr ibn Umayyah al-Damri (ق)

(٣) Sādarahu 'alā kadhā: He demanded it from him (ق)

(٤) Wāda'ahum: He made peace with them. Tawāda'ā (تَوَادَعَا) means they made peace with each other (ق)

٩١ ** سَتَجِدُونَ آخَرِينَ يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَأْمَنُوكُمْ وَيَأْمَنُوا قَوْمَهُمْ** (91) You will find others who desire to be secure from you and secure from their own people.) It was said that they used to manifest Islam so that they might be secure from the Muslims, but when they returned to their people, they disbelieved.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning 'Uyaynah ibn Hisn al-Fazari. His lands had become barren, so he came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and made a pact with him that he would reside in the valley (ببطن) of Nakhl (1) and that no one would interfere with him. He was a cursed hypocrite (منافقاً ملعوناً), and he is the one whom the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم named "the obeyed fool" (الأحمق المطاع). And al-Qummi narrated the like of it. ** كُلَّما رُدُّوا إِلَى الْفِتْنَةِ ** (Every time they are brought back to temptation,) (They were) called to disbelief and to fighting the Muslims. ** أُرْكِسُوا فِيها ** (they are plunged into it.) They returned to it and were turned back into it with the ugliest turning (أقبح قلب). ** فَإِنْ لَمْ يَعْتَزِلُوكُمْ وَيُلْقُوا إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَمَ وَيَكُفُّوا أَيْدِيَهُمْ ** (So if they do not keep out of your way, and do not offer you peace, and do not restrain their hands,) So if these people do not keep out of fighting you, and do not surrender to you, and do not restrain their hands from fighting you, ** فَخُذُوهُمْ (then seize them) So capture them. ** وَاقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ ثَقِفْتُمُوهُمْ (and kill them wherever you find them.) Wherever you gain power over them. ** وَأُولئِكُمْ جَعَلْنا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطاناً مُبِيناً (Against them We have given you a clear authority.) A manifest dominance (تسلطاً ظاهراً) and a clear proof (حجة واضحة) for attacking them with killing and captivity, due to the manifestation of their enmity, disbelief, and treachery.

(1) Sal' (سلع) is a mountain in Medina. The saying of al-Jawhari (الجوهريّ) that al-Sal' (السلع) is incorrect because it is a proper noun (علم) and a mountain belonging to Hudhayl (لهذيل) and a fortress in the Valley of Moses (وادي موسى) in the region of al-Shuwayk (الشويك) (ق).

(٩٢) وَما كانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ (It is not for a believer) And it is not right for a believer, nor is it proper for him, nor does it suit his condition, ** أَنْ يَقْتُلَ مُؤْمِناً (to kill a believer) unjustly, ** إِلَّا خَطَأً (except by mistake), because he is susceptible to error (الخطأ). And al-Qummi means: nor even by mistake (خطأ).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning 'Ayyash (2) ibn Abi Rabi'ah al-Makhzumi, the brother of Abu Jahl, because he had embraced Islam and, after his conversion, killed a Muslim man, though he did not know of his Islam. The slain man was al-Harith ibn Yazid Abu Bunayshah (3) al-'Amiri. He killed him at al-Harrah (4) after the Hijrah. He (al-Harith) was one of those who had turned him back from the Hijrah and used to torture 'Ayyash along with Abu Jahl.

وَمَنْ قَتَلَ مُؤْمِناً (and whoever kills a believer) خَطَأً فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ (by mistake, [the atonement is] the freeing of a slave), then upon him is the freeing of a believing slave (رقبة مؤمنة) in what is between him and Allah. This is according to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), as narrated by al-'Ayyashi.

In al-Kafi and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Every emancipation (العتق) permits the newborn (المولود) except in the atonement (كفّارة) for killing, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: ** فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُؤْمِنَةٍ (the freeing of a believing slave). By this, He means one who affirms [faith] and has reached the age of accountability (الحنث) (1).

And al-'Ayyashi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked how the believing [slave] (المؤمنة) is recognized. He said: By the innate disposition (الفطرة) (2).

وَدِيَةٌ مُسَلَّمَةٌ إِلى أَهْلِهِ (and blood money delivered to his family), meaning, paid to the guardians (أولياء) of the slain, ** إِلَّا أَنْ يَصَّدَّقُوا (unless they remit it as charity), meaning, they remit the blood money (الدية) to him as charity. He named the remission of the blood money as charity (صدقة), urging [people] towards it and drawing attention to its merit.

And in the Hadith: Every good deed (معروف) is charity (صدقة).

Al-'Ayyashi: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about the error (الخطأ) for which blood money (الدية) and atonement (الكفّارة) are due, which is when a man strikes another man but does not intend to kill him. He said: "Yes." It was asked: "What if he throws something and it hits a man?" He said: "That is the error (الخطأ) about which there is no doubt, and upon him is the atonement (الكفّارة) and the blood money (الدية)."

فَإِنْ كانَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ عَدُوٍّ لَكُمْ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُؤْمِنَةٍ. (But if he belongs to a people who are enemies of yours, and he is a believer, then [the atonement is] the freeing of a believing slave.)

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding a Muslim man in the land of polytheism (أرض الشرك) whom the Muslims killed, and the Imam (الإِمام) later learned of it, he said: "He must free a believing slave (رقبة مؤمنة) in his place." That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَإِنْ كانَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ عَدُوٍّ لَكُمْ (But if he belongs to a people who are enemies of yours) [the rest of] the Ayah. And al-'Ayyashi added: And there is no blood money (دية) upon him.

وَإِنْ كانَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ who are disbelievers (كفرة) ** بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ مِيثاقٌ (with whom you have a treaty), meaning, a covenant (عهد), فَدِيَةٌ مُسَلَّمَةٌ إِلى أَهْلِهِ وَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُؤْمِنَةٍ (then [the atonement is] blood money delivered to his family and the freeing of a believing slave). The killer is obligated to pay atonement (كفّارة) for his killing. This is according to al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ (But whoever cannot afford it), meaning, a slave (رقبة), by not possessing him or the means to acquire him, ** فَصِيامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتابِعَيْنِ تَوْبَةً مِنَ اللهِ وَكانَ اللهُ عَلِيماً (let him fast for two successive months—a repentance from Allah. And Allah is All-knowing), of his condition, ** حَكِيماً (All-wise) in what He has commanded concerning his affair.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If a man is obligated to fast for two successive months (صيام شهرين متتابعين) and he breaks the fast or falls ill during the first month, then he must repeat the fast. But if he fasts the first month and fasts part of the second month, and then something occurs for which he has an excuse, then he must make up [the remaining days] (يقضي).

I say: Meaning, he makes up what remains due upon him.

(٩٣) وَمَنْ يَقْتُلْ مُؤْمِناً مُتَعَمِّداً فَجَزاؤُهُ جَهَنَّمُ خالِداً فِيها وَغَضِبَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَلَعَنَهُ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُ عَذاباً عَظِيماً. (93) But whoever kills a believer intentionally, his requital shall be hell, to remain in it forever, and Allah shall be wrathful at him and curse him, and He shall prepare for him a great punishment.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked about a believer who kills a believer intentionally (متعمداً), whether he has repentance (توبة). He said: "If he killed him because of his faith (لإِيمانه), then there is no repentance for him. But if he killed him out of anger or for the sake of something from the things of this world (أشياء الدنيا), then his repentance is that retribution (يقاد منه) be exacted from him. And if it was not known about him (the killer), he should go to the guardians (أولياء) of the slain person and confess to them the killing of their companion. If they forgive him and do not kill him, he must give them the blood money (الدية), free a slave (أعتق نسمة), fast two consecutive months (شهرين متتابعين), and feed sixty needy people (ستين مسكيناً), as repentance to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ)."

And narrated from him عليه السلام: "The believer remains in ease (فسحة) regarding his religion as long as he has not shed unlawful blood (دماً حراماً)." And he said: "The intentional killer (قاتل) of a believer will not be granted success (يوفق) for repentance (للتوبة)."

And in it (al-Kafi), and in al-Ma'ani and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him عليه السلام: "Whoever kills a believer because of his religion (على دينه), that is the intentional killer (المتعمد) about whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said in His Book: وَأَعَدَّ لَهُ عَذاباً عَظِيماً (and He shall prepare for him a great punishment)." It was asked: "What about a man between whom and another man something occurs, so he strikes him with a sword and kills him?" He said: "That is not the intentional killer (المتعمد) about whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَجَزاؤُهُ جَهَنَّمُ (his requital shall be hell)."

And in al-Ma'ani, regarding the Almighty's saying: فَجَزاؤُهُ (his requital) (1) جَهَنَّمُ خالِداً فِيها (shall be hell, to remain in it forever), He said: "If He requites him."

(٩٤) ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** (94) O you who have faith! When you journey in the way of Allah, you journeyed for the purpose of raiding (الغزو) ** فَتَبَيَّنُوا so seek the clarification (بيان) of the matter and distinguish between the disbeliever (الكافر) and the believer (المؤمن). And it was recited (وقرئ): فَتَثَبَّتُوا (then verify) in both places, meaning: stop and proceed slowly until you know who deserves to be killed. The two meanings (المعنيان) are close to one another. This means: do not hasten to kill someone who has outwardly shown his Islam, based on your assumption that there is no reality (حقيقة) to that. وَلا تَقُولُوا لِمَنْ أَلْقى إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلامَ to someone who greets you with the greeting of peace (تحية السلام). And it was recited (وقرئ): السلم (peace/submission) without the alif (ألف), and both are in the meaning of submission (الاستسلام) and compliance (الانقياد). السلام was also interpreted as the greeting of Islam (تحية الإِسلام).

And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) attributed the recitation of السلام to Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) لَسْتَ مُؤْمِناً and that you only did that out of fear of being killed. ** تَبْتَغُونَ عَرَضَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا you seek his wealth (ماله) which is quickly vanishing debris (حطام سريع الزوال), and that is what incites you to haste (العجلة) and abandoning verification (التثبت). فَعِنْدَ اللهِ مَغانِمُ كَثِيرَةٌ which suffice you from killing people like him for the sake of his wealth (ماله). كَذلِكَ كُنْتُمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ when you first entered Islam and uttered the two words of testimony (كلمتي الشهادة), and by them your blood and wealth were protected, without it being known whether your hearts agreed with your tongues. ** فَمَنَّ اللهُ عَلَيْكُمْ by becoming well-known for faith (الإيمان) and steadfastness (الاستقامة) in the religion (الدين). فَتَبَيَّنُوا And deal with those entering Islam as Allah dealt with you, and do not rush to kill them based on the assumption that they entered it merely out of precaution (اتقاءً) and fear (خوفاً). The repetition of this phrase is an emphasis to magnify the matter and to establish the ruling based on the condition mentioned regarding them. ** إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيراً** knowing it and the purpose behind it, so do not fall into (1) killing, and be cautious regarding it.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): It was revealed when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned from the Battle of Khaybar (غزوة خيبر) and sent Usamah ibn Zayd (أسامة بن زيد) with cavalry to some Jews in the region of Fadak (فدك) to invite them to Islam. There was a man among the Jews called Mirdas ibn Nahik al-Fadaki (مرداس بن نهيك الفدكي) in one of the villages. When he sensed the cavalry of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he gathered his wealth (ماله) and his family (أهله) and went to the side of the mountain. He began saying: "I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." Usamah ibn Zayd passed by him, stabbed him, and killed him. When he returned to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he informed Him of that. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "You killed a man who bore witness that there is no god but Allah and that I am the Messenger of Allah!" He replied: "O Messenger of Allah, he only said it seeking refuge (تعوذاً) from being killed." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Did you not split open the covering over his heart? Neither did you accept what he said with his tongue, nor did you know what was in his soul." Usamah swore after that that he would never kill anyone who said: "I bear witness that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." Thus, he stayed behind from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in his wars. And Allah revealed concerning that: وَلا تَقُولُوا لِمَنْ أَلْقى إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلامَ (and do not say to someone who offers you peace) the Ayah.

I say: In this narration, there is that which indicates the hypocrisy (نفاق) of Usamah and his seeking the transient goods of the worldly life (عرض الحياة الدنيا). The saying of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, "nor did you know what was in his soul," suffices as an excuse (عذراً) for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in his wars, for He (the Commander of the Faithful) had known that from Allah and from His Messenger. Furthermore, obedience to the Imam (peace be upon him) is obligatory (واجبة), so Usamah has no excuse (عذر) for staying behind from Him. In the narration of the general populace (العامّة): Mirdas added السلم عليكم (peace be upon you) to the two words (of testimony), and this supports the recitation of السلام and its interpretation as the greeting of Islam (تحية الإسلام).

footnote (١) *Al-Tahāfut* (*التهافت*) means falling down and following one after another. *Al-Tahāfut* (*التهافت*) means falling down little by little (M). The meaning intended is: do not commit killing without deliberation.

(٩٥) لا يَسْتَوِي الْقاعِدُونَ from the war مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ غَيْرُ أُولِي الضَّرَرِ the healthy ones. And it was read in the accusative case (منصوباً), meaning: while they are free from the disability (الضرر) that prevents them from going forth.

(95) Those who stay back among the faithful, other than those who are disabled, are not equal

In al-Majma': It was revealed concerning Ka'b ibn Malik from Banu Salimah, Murarah ibn Rabi' from Banu 'Amr ibn 'Awf, and Hilal ibn Umayyah from Banu Waqif, who stayed behind from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the Day of Tabuk (1). And Allah excused those who were disabled (أولي الضرر), who was 'Abdullah ibn Umm Maktum. He said that Abu Hamzah al-Thumali narrated it in his Tafsir.

And in al-Awali: Zayd ibn Thabit narrated that in the verse negating equality between the Mujahidun (those who strive) and those who stay back (القاعدين), there was no exception other than those who were disabled (أولي الضرر). So Ibn Umm Maktum came, and he was blind, weeping, and said: "O Messenger of Allah, what about those who cannot perform Jihad?" Then the revelation came upon him a second time, and then it was lifted (2) from him عليه السلام.

So He said: "Recite غَيْرُ أُولِي الضَّرَرِ (other than those who are disabled)." So I appended it, and by Him in Whose hand is my soul, it is as if I am looking at the place where it was appended, near a fissure (صدع) (3) in the shoulder blade (الكتف) (4).

وَالْمُجاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ بِأَمْوالِهِمْ وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ (This is) an encouragement for the one who stays back (القاعد) to perform Jihad.

and the strivers in the way of Allah with their possessions and their lives.

فَضَّلَ اللهُ الْمُجاهِدِينَ بِأَمْوالِهِمْ وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ عَلَى الْقاعِدِينَ دَرَجَةً وَكُلًّا

Allah has graced the strivers with their possessions and their lives over the stay-backs by a degree. And to each

from the stay-backs (القاعدين) and the strivers (المجاهدين) وَعَدَ اللهُ الْحُسْنى which is the best recompense, namely Paradise, due to the goodness of their belief (عقيدتهم) and the sincerity of their intention (نيتهم).

Allah has promised the best reward.

In al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "You have indeed left behind in Medina people who, whenever you traveled a distance or crossed a valley, were with you. They are those whose intentions (نياتهم) were sound, whose hearts (جيوبهم) were sincere, and whose souls longed for Jihad. And they were prevented from traveling by disability (ضرراً) or something else." وَفَضَّلَ اللهُ الْمُجاهِدِينَ عَلَى الْقاعِدِينَ أَجْراً عَظِيماً.

But Allah has graced the strivers over the stay-backs with a great reward.

footnote (١) Tabuk (*تبوك*), like *Rasul* (*رسول*), is a place in Syria (*الشام*). From it to Medina is fourteen stages (*مرحلة*), and to Damascus is eleven stages. The Battle of Tabuk (*غزوة تبوك*) is named after it (*م*).

footnote (٢) *Sarā 'anhu* (*سرى عنه*): It was lifted/uncovered (*انكشف*) (*q*).

footnote (٣) *Al-Ṣad'* (*الصدع*): The breaking (*الكسر*), the splitting (*الشق*), the cutting (*القطع*), the separation (*التفرق*), and the intense joining such that the joined object almost breaks off due to the intensity of the joining, and the clarification (*الإبانة*), the manifestation (*الإظهار*), and the elucidation of the matter such that no doubt mixes with it. All are possible meanings in this context, so reflect well.

footnote (٤) What is meant by *al-Katf* (*الكتف*) is the shoulder bone (*عظم الكتب*), for sometimes the verses were written upon the shoulder bone at the time of their revelation instead of parchment (*القرطاس*), and then fixed in another place. And by *ṣad' al-katf* (*صدع الكتف*) is meant its prominence (*كعبه*), which is the place of its clarification (*إبانته*). And by *al-mulḥaq* (*الملحق*) (with *fatḥ* on the *ḥā'* and *fatḥ* or *ḍammah* on the *mīm*) is meant the place of joining (*اللحوق*) and appending (*الإلحاق*). Meaning: when the shoulder bone was filled with a page of the preceding verses, there was no place to append this verse except near the fissure (*صدع*) of the shoulder bone, which is its prominence (*كعبه*), so I appended it to it, fixed therein.

(٩٦) دَرَجاتٍ مِنْهُ وَمَغْفِرَةً وَرَحْمَةً. (degrees from Him, and forgiveness and mercy.)

In al-Majma' (المجمع), it is narrated in the Hadith: that Allah, the Glorified, favored the strivers (المجاهدين) over the sedentary (القاعدين) by seventy degrees (درجة). Between every two degrees is the distance of a journey of seventy خريفاً (autumns/years) (1) for the swift, conditioned (الضمير) horse (الفرس الجواد) (2). It was said: He repeated the favoring of the strivers (المجاهدين) and exaggerated in it, both generally and specifically, in exaltation of Jihad (الجهاد) and to encourage it. And it was said: The first [mention of favor] is what He bestowed upon them (3) in the world (الدنيا) of spoils (الغنيمة), victory (الظفر), and beautiful mention (جميل الذكر), and the second is what He appointed for them in the Hereafter (الآخرة). And it was said: The degree (الدرجة) is the elevation of their status with Allah, and the degrees (الدرجات) are their stations (منازلهم) in Paradise (الجنة).

And it was said: The first sedentary ones (القاعدون) are the blind/infirm (الأضراء) (4). And the second sedentary ones (القاعدون) are those who were permitted to remain behind, due to the sufficiency of others. And it was said: The first strivers (المجاهدون) are those who strove against the disbelievers (الكفّار), and the latter ones are those who strove against their own soul (نفسه), as occurred in the Hadith: "We have returned from the Lesser Jihad (الجهاد الأصغر) to the Greater Jihad (الجهاد الأكبر)."

I say: And it is probable that what is meant by the first [group] is one people (قوماً) and by the latter [group] is another people (آخرين), for what is between one striver (المجاهد) and another striver (المجاهد) is like what is between the heaven and the earth. وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً ** (And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.) He forgives what might have been neglected by them and shows mercy to them by granting reward (الثواب).

(١) In the Hadith: "The poor of my Ummah (أمتي) will enter Paradise (الجنة) forty خريفاً (autumns/years) before the rich." Al-Kharif (الخريف) is the known season among the seasons of the year, between summer (الصيف) and winter (الشتاء). According to the calculation of the astronomers (المنجّمين), it is ninety-one days and one-eighth, which is half of آب (August), أيلول (September), تشرين الأول (October), and half of تشرين الثاني (November). It was said: What is meant by his saying (peace be upon him), "by forty خريفاً," is forty years, because al-Kharif (الخريف) occurs only once in a year, so if forty خريفاً have passed, forty years have passed. And in Ma'ani al-Akhbar (معاني الأخبار), al-Kharif (الخريف) is seventy years. And from this is what was narrated by the Chief of the Narrators (رئيس المحدثين) with his chain of transmission to Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him). He said: "Indeed, a servant remained in the Fire (النار) for seventy خريفاً, and al-Kharif (الخريف) is seventy years." [End quote]. And in places in the books of Hadith: al-Kharif (الخريف) is a thousand years (ألف عام), and the year (العام) is a thousand years (ألف سنة). And in some narrations: I said, "And what is al-Kharif (الخريف), may I be made your ransom?" He said: "A corner (زاوية) in Paradise (الجنة) where the rider travels for forty years (أربعين عاماً)."

(مجمع)

(٢) He said in al-Qamus (القاموس): Ḍammara al-khayl taḍmīran (ضمر الخيل تضميراً) means he fed the horses (الخيل) sustenance after they became fat, like aḍmarahā (أضمرها). And in al-Majma' (المجمع): Taḍmīr al-khayl (تضمير الخيل) is to continuously feed them fodder until they become fat, and then only feed them sustenance so that they become light, and this is over a period of forty days. Until he said: And it was said: It is to fasten their saddles upon them and cover them with blankets (الأجلة) until they sweat beneath them, so their leanness departs and their flesh becomes firm. [End quote]. And perhaps what is meant is the strong horse (الفرس القوي), swift in movement, and frequent in running (كثير العدو).

(٣) Khawwala Allahu al-shay' (خول الله الشيء) means He made him possess it, and khawwalahu ni'matan (خوله نعمة) means He gave him a blessing (نعمة). (م)

(٤) Al-Ḍarīr (الضرير) is the one whose sight is gone (الذاهب البصر), and the plural is aḍrā' (أضراء). And [it also means] the sick, emaciated person. (ق)

(٩٧) The Almighty's saying: ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ ** (Indeed, those whom the angels take away) This verb (تَوَفَّاهُمُ) admits the possibility of the past tense and the present tense. It was also recited (تَوَفَّتْهُمْ). ** ظالِمِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (while they are wronging themselves) is in the state of their wronging themselves by abandoning the emigration (الهجرة) and agreeing with the disbelievers (الكفرة).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked about the saying of Allah, the Almighty: ** اللهُ يَتَوَفَّى الْأَنْفُسَ حِينَ مَوْتِها (Allah takes the souls at the time of their death) and His saying: ** قُلْ يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ مَلَكُ الْمَوْتِ (Say, 'The angel of death, who has been charged with you, will take you away') and His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: ** تَوَفَّتْهُ رُسُلُنا (Our messengers take him away) and His saying, the Almighty: ** الَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ (those whom the angels take away) So, sometimes He attributes the action to Himself, sometimes to the Angel of Death (ملك الموت), sometimes to the messengers (الرسل), and sometimes to the angels (الملائكة). He (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, Allah, the Almighty, is too glorious and magnificent to undertake that Himself. The action of His messengers and His angels is His action, because they act by His command. So, He chose from the angels messengers (رسلاً) and envoys (سفرة) between Him and His creation. They are those concerning whom Allah said: ** اللهُ يَصْطَفِي مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ رُسُلاً وَمِنَ النَّاسِ (Allah chooses messengers from the angels and from mankind) Thus, whoever is among the people of obedience (الطاعة), the angels of mercy (ملائكة الرحمة) undertake the seizing of his soul. And whoever is among the people of disobedience (المعصية), the angels of vengeance (ملائكة النقمة) undertake the seizing of his soul. The Angel of Death has assistants (أعوان) from the angels of mercy and vengeance who act upon his command. Their action is his action, and everything they bring about is attributed to him. If their action is the action of the Angel of Death, then the action of the Angel of Death is the action of Allah, because He takes away the souls by the hand of whomever He wills, and He gives and withholds, and He rewards and punishes by the hand of whomever He wills. Indeed, the action of His trustees (امنائه) is His action, just as He said: وَمَا تَشَاؤُونَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ** (But you do not wish unless Allah wishes)."

In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about that and said: "Indeed, Allah, the Almighty, appointed assistants (أعواناً) for the Angel of Death from the angels who seize the souls. This is comparable to the chief of police (صاحب الشرطة) (1) who has human assistants whom he sends out for his needs. So, the angels take them away, and the Angel of Death takes them away from the angels, along with what he himself seizes, and Allah, the Almighty, takes them away from the Angel of Death."

In al-Tawhid: The Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him) was asked about that and said: "Indeed, Allah, the Almighty, manages affairs however He wills and entrusts whomever He wills of His creation with whatever He wills. As for the Angel of Death, Allah entrusts him specifically with whomever He wills. And He entrusts His messengers from the angels specifically with whomever He wills of His creation. And the angels whom Allah, Mighty is His remembrance, named, He entrusts all of them specifically with whomever He wills of His creation. Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, manages affairs however He wills. Not all knowledge can the possessor of knowledge explain to all people, because among them are the strong and the weak, and because some of it can be borne by you, and some of it cannot be borne except by one for whom Allah makes its bearing easy and whom He assists in it from among His special saints (أوليائه). It is sufficient for you to know that Allah is the Giver of Life (المحيي) and the Giver of Death (المميت), and that He takes away the souls by the hand of whomever He wills of His creation, whether they are His angels or others."

I say: Due to the obscurity (غموض) of this issue, he (peace be upon him) said what he said. The secret therein is that the one who seizes the soul of the plant, causes its death (متوفيه), and raises it to the heaven of animality (سماء الحيوانية) is the soul specific to the animal (النفس المختصة بالحيوان). This soul is one of the assistants (أعوان) of the angels entrusted by Allah's permission with this action, utilizing the sensitive and motive powers (القوى الحساسة والمحركة). Likewise, the one who seizes the soul of the animal, causes its death, and raises it to the heaven of the human degree (سماء الدرجة الإنسانية) is the soul specific to man (النفس المختصة بالإنسان), which is the Word of Allah (كلمة الله) named the Holy Spirit (الروح القدس). Its function is to bring forth souls from the prime matter potentiality (القوّة الهيولانية) to the Acquired Intellect (العقل المستفاد) by the command of Allah, and to convey the spirits (الأرواح) to the proximity of Allah and the world of the Hereafter's Kingdom (عالم الملكوت الأخروي). These are the ones intended by the angels (الملائكة) and the messengers (الرسل). As for man, insofar as he is man, the one who seizes his soul is the Angel of Death: ** قُلْ يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ مَلَكُ الْمَوْتِ (Say, 'The angel of death, who has been charged with you, will take you away') As for the intellectual rank (المرتبة العقليّة), the one who seizes it is Allah, the Glorified: اللهُ يَتَوَفَّى الْأَنْفُسَ (Allah takes the souls). ** يا عِيسى إِنِّي مُتَوَفِّيكَ وَرافِعُكَ إِلَيَّ وَمُطَهِّرُكَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَرْفَعِ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ دَرَجاتٍ قالُوا (O Jesus! I shall take you away and raise you up toward Myself, and I shall purify you of the faithless. Allah will raise in rank those of you who have faith and those who have been given knowledge.) That is, the angels, rebuking them: ** فِيمَ كُنْتُمْ (In what state were you?) meaning: In what state were you regarding the affair of your religion? ** قالُوا كُنَّا مُسْتَضْعَفِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ (They will say, 'We were oppressed in the land.') meaning: The people who associate partners with Allah oppressed us in our land and our countries due to their large numbers and strength, and they prevented us from believing in Allah and following His Messenger. They excused themselves for what they were rebuked for by citing their weakness and inability to emigrate (الهجرة) or to manifest the religion and exalt its word. ** قالُوا (They will say) meaning: The angels, contradicting them: ** أَلَمْ تَكُنْ أَرْضُ اللهِ واسِعَةً فَتُهاجِرُوا فِيها (Was not Allah’s earth vast enough for you to migrate in it?) meaning: So that you might leave your land and your homes and separate yourselves from those who prevented you from believing, going to another region, just as the emigrants (المهاجرون) did to Medina and Abyssinia (الحبشة). ** فَأُولئِكَ مَأْواهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَساءَتْ مَصِيراً** (The refuge of such will be hell, and it is an evil destination.) It is said that this was revealed concerning people in Mecca who embraced Islam but did not emigrate when emigration was obligatory.

In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are Qays ibn al-Fakihah ibn al-Mughirah, al-Harith ibn Zam'ah ibn al-Aswad, Qays ibn al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah, Abu al-'As ibn Munabbih ibn al-Hajjaj, and 'Ali ibn Umayyah ibn Khalaf.

And al-Qummi [narrated that] it was revealed concerning those who withdrew from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and did not fight alongside him. So the angels said to them at the time of death: ** فِيمَ كُنْتُمْ قالُوا كُنَّا مُسْتَضْعَفِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ** (In what state were you? They will say, 'We were oppressed in the land.') meaning: We did not know with whom the truth lay. So Allah said: ** أَلَمْ تَكُنْ أَرْضُ اللهِ واسِعَةً فَتُهاجِرُوا فِيها ** (Was not Allah’s earth vast enough for you to migrate in it?) meaning: The religion of Allah and the Book of Allah are vast, so you should have looked into them.

I say: There is no contradiction between the two narrations, because the first is an exegesis (تفسير) and the second is an interpretation (تأويل), and the verse encompasses both of them.

In Nahj al-Balaghah, he (peace be upon him) said: "The term 'oppressed' (الاستضعاف) does not apply to one to whom the proof (الحجة) has reached, and whose ear has heard it, and whose heart has retained it."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked, "What do you say about the oppressed (المستضعفين)?" He replied, appearing somewhat alarmed: "Have you left anyone to be oppressed? Where are the oppressed? By Allah, this matter of yours has traveled from the young women (العواتق) (1) to the young women in their private chambers, and the water-carriers (السقاءات) have spoken of it in the streets of Medina."

And from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about the weak (الضعفاء), and he wrote: "The weak person (الضعيف) is one for whom no proof (حجة) has been raised and who does not know the disagreement (الإختلاف). But if he knows the disagreement, then he is not weak."

I say: In the verse there is an indication of the obligation of emigration (الهجرة) from a place where a man cannot establish his religion. And narrated from the Prophet (may the peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his progeny): "Whoever flees with his religion from one land to another, even if it is a span of land, deserves Paradise and will be the companion of Abraham (peace be upon him) and Muhammad (may the peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his progeny)."

footnote (١) *الشرطة* (*al-Shurtah*) is the plural of *الشرط* (*al-Shart*) (like *صرد*). They are the first battalion (*كتيبة*) to witness war and prepare for death, and they are a group of the governors' assistants (*أعوان الولاة*) (M). They are called *شرطي* (*Shurti*) (like *تركي* and *جهني*). They were named thus because they marked themselves with signs (*علامات*) by which they were recognized (Q).

(98) The Almighty's saying: ** إِلَّا الْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ الرِّجالِ وَالنِّساءِ وَالْوِلْدانِ (except the oppressed men, women, and children) This is a disconnected exception (استثناء منقطع) because they are not included in the relative pronoun (الموصول), its pronoun (ضميره), or the reference (الإشارة) to it. ** لا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ حِيلَةً وَلا يَهْتَدُونَ سَبِيلاً. (who cannot devise a stratagem, nor find a way)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He is the one who cannot devise a stratagem (حيلة) by which he can repel disbelief (الكفر) from himself, nor is he guided to a way (سبيلاً) toward faith (الايمان). He is unable to believe or disbelieve. He (peace be upon him) said: The children (الصبيان) and those among men and women whose intellects (عقول) are like the intellects of children.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked who they are. He said: Your women and your children. Then he said: Have you seen Umm Ayman? I bear witness that she is among the people of Paradise (أهل الجنة), yet she did not know what you are upon.

And in al-Ma'ani and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), is a narration close to the first حديث (narration), and at its end: "The pen (القلم) is lifted from them."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** لا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ حِيلَةً (who cannot devise a stratagem) toward establishing (النصب) [the truth], so they establish it, وَلا يَهْتَدُونَ سَبِيلاً (nor find a way) toward the truth (الحق) so they may enter into it. These people will enter Paradise (الجنة) through good deeds (أعمال حسنة) and by avoiding the forbidden things (المحارم) which Allah has prohibited, but they will not attain the stations (منازل) of the righteous (الأبرار).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the oppressed (المستضعفين). He said: The simple-minded woman (البلهاء) (1) in her chamber (خدرها) (2), and the servant whom you tell to pray (صلي) and she prays, knowing nothing except what you told her; and the الجليب who knows nothing except what you told him; and the extremely old person (الكبير الفاني) (3) and the child (الصغير).

I say: The الجليب is the one who is brought (يُجْلَب) from one land to another.

footnote (١) He said in *al-Majma'*: *al-Awatik* (*العواتق*) among women is the plural of *‘Atiq* (*عاتق*), which is the young woman when she first reaches maturity. It is also said to be the one who has not separated from her mother, has not married, but has reached maturity and grown up. [End of quote]. What is referred to by this is the matter of guardianship (*الولاية*) and the water-carriers (*السقاءات*), the women from the desert dwellers (*أهل البوادي*) who provide water and milk to visitors (*الزوار*) and pilgrims (*الحجاج*). For if oppression (*استضعاف*) is found, they are more deserving of being described by it than anyone else.

(99) فَأُولئِكَ عَسَى اللهُ أَنْ يَعْفُوَ عَنْهُمْ وَكانَ اللهُ عَفُوًّا غَفُوراً (For those, Allah may well excuse them, and Allah is all-excusing, all-forgiving.) He is the One Who overlooks (صَفْح) the sins of His servants, removing (سَائِرًا) their sins from them.

(100) وَمَنْ يُهاجِرْ He separates from the people of polytheism (أَهْلِ الشرك) and flees with his religion (بِدِينِهِ) from his homeland to the land of Islam (أَرْضِ الإِسلام) فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** in the path (مِنْهَاج) of His religion ** يَجِدْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مُراغَماً كَثِيراً a place of transformation (مُتَحَوَّلًا) from al-raghām (الرغام), which is the dust (التراب), and a deliverance (مُخْلِصًا) from error (الضلال) ** وَسَعَةً in provision (الرزق) and the manifestation (إِظْهَار) of the religion. And thereby He will humiliate (فَيَرْغَم) the noses (أُنُوف) of those among his people who restricted him. ** وَمَنْ يَخْرُجْ مِنْ بَيْتِهِ مُهاجِراً إِلَى اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ثُمَّ يُدْرِكْهُ الْمَوْتُ فَقَدْ وَقَعَ أَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللهِ وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً. (And whoever leaves his home, migrating toward Allah and His Apostle, and is then overtaken by death, his reward shall certainly fall on Allah, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.)

In al-Majma', narrated from Abu Hamzah al-Thumali: When the verse of migration (آية الهجرة) was revealed, a man from the Muslims heard it—he was Junda' or Jundub ibn Damrah, and he was in Mecca. He said: "By Allah, I am not among those whom Allah has exempted. I find strength, and I know the way." He was severely ill. He said to his sons: "By Allah, I will not spend the night in Mecca until I leave it, for I fear that I might die in it." So they went out carrying him on a bed until, when he reached al-Tan'im, he died. Then the verse was revealed.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Muhammad ibn Abi 'Umayr, who said: Zurarah ibn A'yan sent his son 'Ubayd to Medina to inquire for him about the news of Abu al-Hasan Musa ibn Ja'far (peace be upon him) and 'Abd Allah al-Aftas. Zurarah died before 'Ubayd Allah returned to him. Muhammad ibn Abi 'Umayr said: Muhammad ibn Hakim narrated to me, saying: I mentioned Zurarah and his sending 'Ubayd to Medina to Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him). He said: "I certainly hope that Zurarah is among those concerning whom Allah said: وَمَنْ يَخْرُجْ مِنْ بَيْتِهِ مُهاجِراً إِلَى اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ (the Ayah)."

(١٠١) وَإِذا ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (101) And when you travel in the land (You journeyed) ** فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ أَنْ تَقْصُرُوا مِنَ الصَّلاةِ there is no sin upon you in shortening the prayer by halving the four-unit prayers (الرباعيات). When Allah commanded al-Jihad and al-Hijrah, He clarified the prayer of travel and fear. It was said that perhaps they were accustomed to performing the full prayer (الإتمام), and it was likely that it might occur to them that they incurred a deficiency (نقصاناً) by shortening it (التقصير). So He removed the sin (الجناح) from them so that their souls might be pleased with the shortening (القصر) and they might be reassured by it.

In al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Zurarah and Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said: We said to Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him): What do you say concerning the prayer during travel? How is it, and how many units is it? He said: Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: ** وَإِذا ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ أَنْ تَقْصُرُوا مِنَ الصَّلاةِ And when you travel in the land, there is no sin upon you in shortening the prayer Thus, shortening (التقصير) during travel became obligatory (واجباً), just as performing the full prayer (التمام) is obligatory during residence (الحضر). They said: We said to Him: Allah the Almighty only said: ** فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ (there is no sin upon you), and He did not say, "Do it." How did He make that obligatory, just as He made the full prayer obligatory during residence? He said: Did not Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, say: ** إِنَّ الصَّفا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعائِرِ اللهِ فَمَنْ حَجَّ الْبَيْتَ أَوِ اعْتَمَرَ فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِما Indeed, the Safa and the Marwah are among Allah’s sacraments. So whoever makes pilgrimage to the House or performs the ʿumrah, there is no sin upon him to circuit between them Do you not see that circumambulation (الطواف) between them is an obligatory duty (واجب مفروض) because Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, mentioned it in His Book and His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم performed it? Likewise, shortening (التقصير) during travel is something the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم performed and Allah mentioned in His Book. They said: We asked Him: So, whoever prays four units during travel, must he repeat it or not? He said: If the verse of shortening (آية التقصير) was recited to him and explained to him, and he prayed four units, he must repeat it (أعاد). But if it was not recited to him and he did not know it, there is no repetition (إعادة) upon him. And all obligatory prayers (الصلوات) during travel are two units (ركعتان) for every prayer, except for the Maghrib prayer, for it is three units, and there is no shortening (تقصير) in it. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم left it as three units both during travel and residence (الحضر). And it is added in al-Faqih: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم traveled to Dhu Khushub (ذي خشب), which is a day's journey from Medina, being two barids (بريدان), twenty-four miles (ميلاً). He shortened the prayer (قصر) and broke the fast (أفطر), and thus it became a Sunnah (practice). And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم named the people who fasted when He broke the fast as the disobedient (العصاة) until the Day of Resurrection. And we certainly know their sons and the sons of their sons until this very day of ours.

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: The obligatory prayer (فرض) for the traveler is two units, not a shortening (قصر).

أقول : I say: And the minimum distance of travel (سفر) in which one shortens the prayer is eight farasikh (فراسخ), going and coming, as is understood from the infallible narrations (الأخبار المعصومية). And this has been hidden from most of our companions (أصحابنا), as they claimed that this distance is considered only for the outward journey (الذهاب خاصّة). And we have verified that in our book entitled al-Wafi (الوافي) and others. ** إِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَنْ يَفْتِنَكُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا if you fear that the faithless may persecute you regarding yourselves or your religion. And this condition is based on what was prevalent at that time, for the shortening (القصر) is established even in a state of security (الامن) as well.

In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: It refers to the two units, from which one unit is reduced, meaning in the state of fear (حال الخوف). إِنَّ الْكافِرِينَ كانُوا لَكُمْ عَدُوًّا مُبِيناً Indeed, the faithless are a manifest enemy to you. whose enmity is apparent.

(١٠٢) ** وَإِذا كُنْتَ فِيهِمْ (102) And when you are among them, (among your companions who are traveling in the land, fearing their enemy might attack them) and you establish the prayer for them by leading them, ** فَلْتَقُمْ طائِفَةٌ مِنْهُمْ مَعَكَ وَلْيَأْخُذُوا أَسْلِحَتَهُمْ فَإِذا سَجَدُوا فَلْيَكُونُوا مِنْ وَرائِكُمْ let a group of them stand with you, and let them take their arms. When they prostrate, let them be behind you, (guarding you). ** وَلْتَأْتِ طائِفَةٌ أُخْرى لَمْ يُصَلُّوا فَلْيُصَلُّوا مَعَكَ وَلْيَأْخُذُوا حِذْرَهُمْ وَأَسْلِحَتَهُمْ وَدَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ تَغْفُلُونَ عَنْ أَسْلِحَتِكُمْ وَأَمْتِعَتِكُمْ فَيَمِيلُونَ عَلَيْكُمْ مَيْلَةً واحِدَةً ** And let another group, who have not prayed, come and pray with you, and let them take their precautions and their arms. The faithless wish that you were oblivious of your arms and your baggage, so that they might assault you in a single charge. (their caution and vigilance). They wished to gain an opportunity (غرة) from you during your prayers, so they could charge you with a single attack. This is a clarification of why they were commanded to take up arms. ** وَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِنْ كانَ بِكُمْ أَذىً مِنْ مَطَرٍ أَوْ كُنْتُمْ مَرْضى أَنْ تَضَعُوا أَسْلِحَتَكُمْ There is no sin upon you if you are troubled by rain or if you are sick, to lay down your arms, (A concession for them to lay them down if carrying them becomes difficult due to rain or sickness). ** وَخُذُوا And take حِذْرَكُمْ your precautions, lest the enemy attack you. ** إِنَّ اللهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَذاباً مُهِيناً ** Indeed Allah has prepared a humiliating punishment for the faithless. (A disgracing punishment.)

Al-Qummi narrates: This verse was revealed when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم set out for al-Hudaybiyyah, intending to go to Mecca. When the news reached Quraysh, they sent Khalid ibn al-Walid with one hundred horsemen to intercept the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He was confronting the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the mountains. Whenever they were on a part of the road and the Dhuhr prayer was due, Bilal would call the adhan, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would lead the people in prayer. Khalid ibn al-Walid said: "If we had charged them while they were praying, we would have struck them, for they do not interrupt the prayer. But now another prayer will come to them, which is dearer to them than the light of their eyes. If they enter into it, we will charge them." Then Gabriel descended with the prayer of fear (صلاة الخوف) through this verse. So, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم divided his companions into two groups. One group stood facing the enemy, having taken their arms, and the other group prayed with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم while He was standing. They then moved and stood in the position of their companions. Then those who had not prayed came, and the Messenger of Allah led them in the second rak'ah (which was their first). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sat down, and His companions stood up and prayed their second rak'ah, and He greeted them with the salutation (سلم عليهم).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah prayed the prayer of fear (صلاة الخوف) with his companions during the Battle of Dhat al-Riqa'. He divided his companions into two groups: He stationed one group facing the enemy and the other group behind Him. He pronounced the takbir and they pronounced the takbir. He recited and they listened silently. He bowed and they bowed. He prostrated and they prostrated. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم remained standing, and they prayed one rak'ah for themselves. Then they greeted one another with the salutation (سلم بعضهم على بعض). Then they went out to their companions and stood facing the enemy. Their companions came and stood behind the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He prayed one rak'ah with them, then He recited the tashahhud and greeted them with the salutation (سلم عليهم). They stood up and prayed one rak'ah for themselves, then they greeted one another with the salutation (سلم بعضهم على بعض).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the prayer of fear (صلاة الخوف). He said: The Imam stands, and a group of his companions comes and stands behind him, while another group stands facing the enemy. The Imam prays one rak'ah with them, then He stands up, and they stand up with Him. He remains standing, and they pray the second rak'ah. Then they greet one another with the salutation (يسلم بعضهم على بعض). Then they depart and stand in the position of their companions. The others come and stand behind the Imam. He prays the second rak'ah with them. Then the Imam sits, and they stand up and pray another rak'ah. Then He greets them with the salutation (يسلم عليهم), and they depart upon His salutation.

He said: And in the Maghrib prayer, it is similar. The Imam stands, and a group comes and stands behind him. Then He prays one rak'ah with them. Then He stands up, and they stand up. The Imam remains standing, and they pray two rak'ahs. Then they recite the tashahhud and greet one another with the salutation (يسلم بعضهم على بعض). Then they depart and stand in the position of their companions. The others come and stand in the position of their companions behind the Imam. He prays one rak'ah with them, reciting during it. Then He sits and recites the tashahhud. Then He stands up, and they stand up with Him, and He prays another rak'ah with them. Then He sits, and they stand up and complete another rak'ah. Then He greets them with the salutation (يسلم عليهم).

(١٠٣) فَإِذا قَضَيْتُمُ الصَّلاةَ (103) When you have finished the prayer When you have finished your prayer while you are fighting your enemy, ** فَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ قِياماً وَقُعُوداً وَعَلى جُنُوبِكُمْ (remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying on your sides.) Supplicate to Allah in these states, perhaps He will grant you victory over your enemy and make you triumphant over him. This is similar to His Almighty saying: ** إِذا لَقِيتُمْ فِئَةً فَاثْبُتُوا وَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ كَثِيراً لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (When you encounter a host, stand firm, and remember Allah greatly so that you may be felicitous.) ** فَإِذَا اطْمَأْنَنْتُمْ (But when you are secure,) When you have settled in your homelands and resided in your cities, ** فَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ (maintain the prayer.) Then complete the prayer which you were permitted to shorten and lighten during travel and fear, and complete its limits (حدودها). ** إِنَّ الصَّلاةَ كانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتاباً مَوْقُوتاً** (Indeed the prayer has been enjoined upon the faithful for specified times.)

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means obligatory (مفروضاً), and it does not mean the time of its lapse (وقت فوتها). If that time passes and he then prays it, this prayer of his is not considered performed (مؤداة). If it were so, Sulayman ibn Dawud would have perished when he prayed it outside its time. Rather, whenever he remembers it, he prays it.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** مَوْقُوتاً (for specified times.) Meaning fixed (ثابتاً). It is not the case that if you hasten it slightly or delay it slightly, it will harm you, as long as you do not commit that neglect (الإضاعة). For Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says concerning a people: ** أَضاعُوا الصَّلاةَ وَاتَّبَعُوا الشَّهَواتِ فَسَوْفَ يَلْقَوْنَ غَيًّا (who neglected the prayers and followed [their] desires. They will soon encounter error.)

(104) ** وَلا تَهِنُوا فِي ابْتِغاءِ الْقَوْمِ (Do not falter in the pursuit of the people) Do not weaken in seeking the people who are the enemies of Allah and your enemies. ** إِنْ تَكُونُوا تَأْلَمُونَ (If you suffer pain) from the wounds inflicted upon you by them, ** فَإِنَّهُمْ يَأْلَمُونَ (they too suffer pain) also from what afflicts them of that (pain), ** كَما تَأْلَمُونَ وَتَرْجُونَ مِنَ اللهِ ما لا يَرْجُونَ (just as you suffer pain, and you hope from Allah what they do not hope for) such as the triumph of the religion and the deserving of reward. Thus, you are more worthy and deserving of waging war and fighting them than they are of fighting you. ** وَكانَ اللهُ عَلِيماً ** (and Allah is all-knowing) of the welfare (مصالح) of His creation, ** حَكِيماً (all-wise) in His management (تدبيره) of them.

Al-Qummi narrates: When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned from the Battle of Uhud and entered Medina, Gabriel descended upon him and said: "O Muhammad, Allah commands you to go out in pursuit of the people, and none shall go out with you except those who have wounds." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded a caller to announce: "O company of the Emigrants (المهاجرين) and the Helpers (الأنصار), whoever has a wound, let him go out, and whoever does not have a wound, let him remain." So they came forward, bandaging their wounds and treating them. Then Allah sent down to His Prophet: ** وَلا تَهِنُوا (the Ayah). And He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ** إِنْ يَمْسَسْكُمْ قَرْحٌ فَقَدْ مَسَّ الْقَوْمَ قَرْحٌ مِثْلُهُ (If a wound has afflicted you, a similar wound has certainly afflicted the people) up to His saying: ** شُهَداءَ ** (martyrs). So they went out despite the pain and wounds they suffered.

(١٠٥) ** إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ بِالْحَقِّ لِتَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ بِما أَراكَ اللهُ** (105) Indeed We have sent down to you the Book with the truth, so that you may judge between the people by what Allah has shown you. By what He made known to you and revealed to you.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): By Allah, Allah did not delegate authority (فَوَّضَ) to any of His creation except to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and to the Imams. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ** إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ بِالْحَقِّ لِتَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ بِما أَراكَ اللهُ (Indeed We have sent down to you the Book with the truth, so that you may judge between the people by what Allah has shown you), and this (authority) is applicable (جَارِيَة) to the successors (الأوصياء).

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, He said to Abu Hanifah: "And you claim that you are a man of opinion (صَاحِبُ رَأْي)." And the opinion (الرأي) from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was correct (صَوَابًا), while from anyone besides him it is an error (خَطَأ), because Allah said: "So judge between them by what Allah has shown you (بِمَا أَرَاكَ اللهُ)," and He did not say that to anyone else. وَلا تَكُنْ لِلْخائِنِينَ (And do not be an advocate for the treacherous) — for their sake and defending them — خَصِيماً (an advocate) for the innocent (لِلْبَرَاء).

(١٠٦) ** وَاسْتَغْفِرِ اللهَ (106) and seek forgiveness of Allah. for what you intended (to do) ** إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful for whoever seeks His forgiveness.

Al-Qummi narrates: The reason for its revelation was that a group of the Ansar from Banu Ubayriq—three brothers who were hypocrites (منافقين): Bishr, Mubashshir, and Bishr—broke into the house of the uncle of Qatadah ibn al-Nu'man. Qatadah was a participant in the Battle of Badr (بدرياً). They took food that he had prepared for his family, a sword, and a shield. Qatadah complained about this to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, a group of people broke into my uncle's house and took food he had prepared for his family, a sword, and a shield. They are a wicked household (أهل بيت سوء)." A believing man named Labid ibn Sahl was associated with them in opinion. Banu Ubayriq said to Qatadah: "This is the work of Labid ibn Sahl." This reached Labid, so he took his sword and went out to them, saying: "O Banu Ubayriq, do you accuse me of theft (السرق) when you are more deserving of it than I am, and you are the hypocrites (المنافقون) who satirize the Messenger of Allah and attribute him to Quraysh? You must clarify this, or I will fill my sword with you!" They appeased him and said to him: "Go back, may Allah have mercy on you, for you are innocent of that." Then Banu Ubayriq went to a man from their clan named Usayd ibn 'Urwah, who was eloquent and articulate (منطيقاً بليغاً). He went to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, Qatadah ibn al-Nu'man deliberately targeted a household among us, a people of honor (شرف), standing (حسب), and lineage (نسب), and accused them of theft, bringing against them what is not true of them." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم became distressed by this. Qatadah came to Him, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم turned to him and said: "You deliberately targeted a household of honor, standing, and lineage, and accused them of theft." He reprimanded him severely. Qatadah became distressed by this and returned to his uncle, saying: "I wish I had died and not spoken to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, for He spoke to me with what I disliked." His uncle said: "Allah is the Helper (الله المستعان)." So Allah revealed concerning this to His Prophet: ** إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ (the Ayahs).

In al-Majma' there is a narration close to this. It states: Bishr was nicknamed Abu Tu'mah. He used to compose poetry and satirize the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with it, and then claim that so-and-so said it.

In al-Jawami': It is narrated that Abu Tu'mah ibn Ubayriq stole a shield (درعاً) from his neighbor named Qatadah ibn al-Nu'man and hid it with a Jewish man. The shield was taken from the Jew's house, and the Jew said: "Abu Tu'mah gave it to me." So Banu Ubayriq came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and asked Him to argue on behalf of their companion, saying: "If you do not do so, he will perish and be disgraced, and the Jew will be acquitted." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم intended to do so and to punish the Jew, so [the Ayahs] were revealed. The narrations transmitted by the general public (العامّة) are similar in meaning, with additions.

(١٠٧) وَلا تُجادِلْ عَنِ الَّذِينَ يَخْتانُونَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ (107) Do not argue on behalf of those who betray their souls.

He made disobedience a betrayal of the soul, just as it was made an injustice against it, because its evil consequence returns upon it.

** إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ مَنْ كانَ خَوَّاناً** Indeed Allah does not like someone who is a betrayer,

one who is excessive in betrayal, persisting in it, sinful (أَثِيماً), and engrossed in it.

(١٠٨) يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ النَّاسِ (They seek to hide from the people) They conceal themselves from them out of shame and fear, ** وَلا يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ اللهِ (but they do not seek to hide from Allah) and they are not ashamed before Him, while He is more deserving that one should be ashamed before Him and fear Him, ** وَهُوَ مَعَهُمْ إِذْ يُبَيِّتُونَ (and He is with them when they conspire) They plan and fabricate falsehoods by night, ** ما لا يَرْضى مِنَ الْقَوْلِ (what He does not approve of in speech.) which is the accusation of the innocent. Al-Qummi said: It means the action (الفعل), so the word (القول) was used in place of the action (الفعل). وَكانَ اللهُ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطاً** (And Allah comprehends what they do.) Nothing escapes Him.

** ها أَنْتُمْ هؤُلاءِ جادَلْتُمْ عَنْهُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا فَمَنْ يُجادِلُ اللهَ عَنْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ أَمْ مَنْ يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكِيلاً ** (4:109) Ah! You are the ones who argued on their behalf in the life of the world. But who will argue with Allah on their behalf on the Day of Resurrection, or who will be their advocate? —an advocate (محامياً عنهم) who protects them (يحميهم) from the punishment of Allah (من عذاب الله). (١١٠) وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءاً ** an ugly deed (قبيحاً) by which he harms others (يسوء به غيره) ** أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُ ** with that which pertains specifically to him and does not transgress beyond him (بما يختص به ولا يتعداه) ** ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللهَ ** through repentance (بالتوبة) ** يَجِدِ اللهَ غَفُوراً ** for his sins (لذنوبه) ** رَحِيماً ** being gracious towards him (متفضلاً عليه). (4:110) Whoever commits an evil or wrongs himself, then implores Allah for forgiveness, will find Allah all-forgiving, all-merciful. In Nahj al-Balaghah: "Whoever is granted seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار) is not deprived of forgiveness (المغفرة)." Then he recited ** الْآيَةَ .

(111) ** وَمَنْ يَكْسِبْ إِثْماً فَإِنَّما يَكْسِبُهُ عَلى نَفْسِهِ (And whoever commits a sin, commits it only against himself.) Its evil consequence (وبال) does not pass beyond him. ** وَكانَ اللهُ عَلِيماً حَكِيماً ** (And Allah is all-knowing, all-wise.) So He is Knower of his action, Wise in His recompense (مجازاته).

(112) ** وَمَنْ يَكْسِبْ خَطِيئَةً (And whoever commits a fault) A sin (ذنب) committed unintentionally (على غير عمد) ** أَوْ إِثْماً (or a sin,) A sin (ذنب) he committed intentionally (تعمده), like Bishr (كبشير) ** ثُمَّ يَرْمِ بِهِ بَرِيئاً ** (and then casts it upon an innocent person,) As Bishr accused Lubaid or the Jew ** فَقَدِ احْتَمَلَ بُهْتاناً وَإِثْماً مُبِيناً (has certainly borne a calumny and a manifest sin.) Due to accusing the innocent person and exonerating the sinful soul (النفس الخاطئة).

(١١٣) وَلَوْ لا فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكَ وَرَحْمَتُهُ (And were it not for Allah’s grace upon you and His mercy) by informing you through revelation of what they were upon, ** لَهَمَّتْ طائِفَةٌ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُضِلُّوكَ (a faction of them had resolved to mislead you) from judging with the truth, despite their knowledge of the situation. The intent therein is not to negate their resolve (همهم) but rather to negate its effect upon him (the Prophet). ** وَما يُضِلُّونَ إِلَّا أَنْفُسَهُمْ (And they mislead no one but themselves) because its consequence is upon them, وَما يَضُرُّونَكَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ (and they will not harm you in the least), for Allah is protecting you, supporting you, and aiding you. And what occurred to your mind was reliance on the apparent matter, not a deviation in judgment. ** وَأَنْزَلَ اللهُ عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَعَلَّمَكَ ما لَمْ تَكُنْ تَعْلَمُ (And Allah has sent down to you the Book and the Wisdom, and taught you what you did not know) of the hidden matters. ** وَكانَ فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكَ عَظِيماً (And Allah’s grace upon you has been great) since there is no grace greater than Prophethood (النبوّة).

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: Indeed, some people from the close relatives (الأدنين) of Bishr said: "Let us go to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and speak to him concerning our companion and excuse him, for our companion is innocent." When Allah sent down: ** يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَلا يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ اللهِ وَهُوَ مَعَهُمْ (They seek to hide from the people, but they cannot hide from Allah, and He is with them) up to His saying: ** وَكِيلاً (trustee). Then the relatives (رهط) of Bishr approached him and said: "O Bishr, seek forgiveness from Allah and repent from the sin." He said: "By Him Whom I swear by, no one stole it except Labid." Then was revealed: ** وَمَنْ يَكْسِبْ خَطِيئَةً أَوْ إِثْماً ثُمَّ يَرْمِ بِهِ بَرِيئاً فَقَدِ احْتَمَلَ بُهْتاناً (And whoever commits a fault or a sin, and then casts the blame for it upon an innocent person, has certainly borne a calumny) وَإِثْماً مُبِيناً (and a manifest sin). Then Bishr disbelieved and joined Mecca. And Allah sent down concerning the group (النفر) who excused Bishr and came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to excuse him: ** وَلَوْ لا فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكَ وَرَحْمَتُهُ (the Ayah). And concerning Bishr, while he was in Mecca, was revealed: ** وَمَنْ يُشاقِقِ الرَّسُولَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ الْهُدى وَيَتَّبِعْ غَيْرَ سَبِيلِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ نُوَلِّهِ ما تَوَلَّى وَنُصْلِهِ جَهَنَّمَ وَساءَتْ مَصِيراً**. (Whoever opposes the Messenger after the guidance has become manifest to him, and follows a path other than the faithful’s, We shall abandon him to what he has turned to, and land him in hell—and what a bad destination it is!)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), regarding the Almighty's saying: ** إِذْ يُبَيِّتُونَ ما لا يَرْضى مِنَ الْقَوْلِ (when they plotted by night what was displeasing to Him in their speech), He meant so-and-so (فلاناً), and so-and-so (فلاناً), and Abu Ubaydah al-Jarrah (أبا عبيدة الجراح).
