In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a narration: And Allah has clarified the stories of the deviators (المغيرين) by His saying: إِذْ يُبَيِّتُونَ ما لا يَرْضى مِنَ الْقَوْلِ (when they plotted by night what was displeasing to Him in their speech), after the loss of the Messenger, [they plotted] what they would use to straighten the crookedness (أود) (١) of their falsehood, just as the Jews and Christians did after the loss of Moses and Jesus, by changing the Torah and the Gospel and distorting the words from their proper places (مواضعه).

** لا خَيْرَ فِي كَثِيرٍ مِنْ نَجْواهُمْ إِلَّا مَنْ أَمَرَ بِصَدَقَةٍ أَوْ مَعْرُوفٍ ** (114) There is no good in much of their secret talks, except for him who enjoins charity, or fairness) a beautiful matter (أمر جميل) أَوْ إِصْلاحٍ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ (or reconciliation between people) — bringing them together through affection (بالمودة).

In al-Kafi, al-Ayyashi, and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means by al-ma'ruf (المعروف) the loan (القرض).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, (2) Allah has made al-tamahhul (التمحل) (3) obligatory in the Quran. He was asked: "What is al-tamahhul (التمحل)?" He said: "That your face be more expansive than the face of your brother, so you practice al-tamahhul (التمحل) towards him." And this is His saying, the Almighty: ** لا خَيْرَ فِي كَثِيرٍ مِنْ نَجْواهُمْ **.

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah has made the zakat (زكاة) of your status (جاهكم) obligatory upon you, just as He has made the zakat (زكاة) of what your hands possess obligatory upon you.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Speech (الكلام) is three things: truth (صدق), falsehood (كذب), and reconciliation between people (إصلاح بين الناس). And He explained reconciliation (الإصلاح) as: You hear from a man speech that reaches him, and his soul becomes corrupted (فتخبث نفسه), so you meet him and say: "I heard so-and-so say such-and-such good things about you," contrary to what you actually heard from him.

And in al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), from his father, from his forefathers, from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): Three things in which falsehood (الكذب) is permissible: stratagem (المكيدة) in war, your promise to your wife, and reconciliation between people (الإصلاح بين الناس).

وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذلِكَ (And whoever does that) — meaning the three matters, or the commanding of them — ** ابْتِغاءَ مَرْضاتِ اللهِ فَسَوْفَ نُؤْتِيهِ أَجْراً عَظِيماً ** (and does that, seeking the pleasure of Allah, We shall soon grant him a great reward.) And it was recited with the ya' (بالياء).

(١١٥) وَمَنْ يُشاقِقِ الرَّسُولَ يخالفه مِنْ بَعْدِ ما تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ الْهُدى (115) And whoever opposes the Apostle, after the guidance has become clear to him, He opposes Him. Meaning, the truth (الحق) has become manifest to him. ** وَيَتَّبِعْ غَيْرَ سَبِيلِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ and follows a path other than the path of the faithful, That which they are upon, namely the upright religion (الدين الحنيفي). ** نُوَلِّهِ ما تَوَلَّى We shall let him follow what he has followed, We shall make him a governor (والياً) over what he has followed of misguidance (الضلال), by abandoning him and leaving him alone with what he has chosen. وَنُصْلِهِ جَهَنَّمَ وَساءَتْ مَصِيراً. and We shall cast him into hell, and it is an evil destination. Al-Qummi: It was revealed concerning Bishr, as has passed.

(١١٦) ** إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ ما دُونَ ذلِكَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ (116) Indeed Allah does not forgive that a partner should be ascribed to Him, but He forgives anything besides that to whoever He wishes. Its repetition is either for emphasis or due to the story of Bashir (بشير). ** وَمَنْ يُشْرِكْ بِاللهِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلالاً بَعِيداً ** and whoever ascribes a partner to Allah has certainly strayed into a deep error. away from the truth. (١١٧) إِنْ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ (117) They invoke besides Him What these polytheists invoke and what they worship besides Allah are nothing but females (إِنَاثاً), meaning al-Lāt (اللات), al-ʿUzzā (والعزى), and Manāt (ومناة) the third, the other, and Asāf (وأَساف) and Nāʾilah (ونائلة). It is said that every tribe had an idol that they worshipped and called it the female (أُنْثَى) of the children of so-and-so. This is what was said. In al-Majmaʿ, narrated from the Tafsir of Abū Ḥamzah al-Thumālī (أبي حمزة الثمالي), he said: In every one of them there was a female devil (شَيْطَانَة) who would appear to the custodians (لِلسَّدَنَة) (2) and speak to them. That was from the doing of Iblīs (إبليس), who is the devil (الشيطان) Whom Allah the Almighty mentioned and cursed. وَإِنْ يَدْعُونَ and they invoke meaning, they worship by worshipping them ** إِلَّا شَيْطاناً مَرِيداً nothing but a rebellious devil. because he is the one who commanded them to worship them and incited them to do so, thus their obedience in that was worship of him. And al-Marīd (المريد) is the one who is outside of obedience, who clings to no good.

(1) Khubuth al-shayʾ (خبث الشيء) means khubthan (خبثاً) from the category of qaruba (قرب) and khabāthah (خباثة), the opposite of ṭālib (طالب), so it is khabīth (خبيث) (م)

(2) Sadana (سدن) means sadnan (سدناً) and sadānah (سدانة), which is the service of the Kaʿbah (الكعبة) or the house of the idol (بيت الصنم) (ث)

(١١٨) لَعَنَهُ اللهُ (whom Allah has cursed) He removed him far from goodness (الخير). وَقالَ (And he said), meaning Satan, لَأَتَّخِذَنَّ مِنْ عِبادِكَ نَصِيباً مَفْرُوضاً (I will surely take a definite share of Your servants) a portion decreed for me and obligated (مَفْرُوضاً). He said this out of enmity (عداوة) and hatred (بغضاً).

In al-Majma', narrated from Tafsir al-Thumali, from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم concerning this Ayah: Ninety-nine of the children of Adam are in the Fire and one is in Paradise. And in another narration: From every thousand, one is for Allah, and the rest of them are for the Fire and for Iblis.

(١١٩) وَلَأُضِلَّنَّهُمْ (I will surely lead them astray) from the truth وَلَأُمَنِّيَنَّهُمْ (and fill them with vain desires) with false desires (الأماني الباطلة) such as long life, and that there is no resurrection and no punishment ** وَلَآمُرَنَّهُمْ فَلَيُبَتِّكُنَّ آذانَ الْأَنْعامِ (and I will surely command them, so they will slit the ears of the cattle) It was said that they would slit their ears when they had given birth to five litters and the fifth was male, and they forbade themselves from benefiting from them. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): they will surely cut the ear from its root. وَلَآمُرَنَّهُمْ فَلَيُغَيِّرُنَّ خَلْقَ اللهِ (and I will surely command them, so they will alter the creation of Allah) Narrated from him (peace be upon him): He means the religion (دين) of Allah and His command (أمر). And in it (al-Majma') and supporting it is His saying, glory be to Him: ** فِطْرَتَ اللهِ الَّتِي فَطَرَ النَّاسَ عَلَيْها لا تَبْدِيلَ لِخَلْقِ اللهِ. (the nature of Allah with which He has imbued mankind. There is no altering Allah’s creation) I say: And what increases its support is His saying, mighty is His majesty, immediately following that: ** الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ (the upright religion) And their (peace be upon them) interpretation of ** فِطْرَتَ اللهِ as Islam. And perhaps every alteration of Allah's creation from its proper form (وجهه), whether in appearance (صورة) or attribute (صفة), without permission (اذن) from Allah, is included therein, such as their gouging out (١) the eye of the stud camel (الفحل) that had stayed long among them, and exempting it from riding, and the castration (خصاء) of slaves, and every mutilation (مثلة). And the interpretation as the religion (الدين) and the command (الأمر) does not contradict this, because all of that is included within them both. وَمَنْ يَتَّخِذِ الشَّيْطانَ وَلِيًّا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (Whoever takes Satan for a guardian instead of Allah) by preferring obedience to him over obedience to Allah, mighty is His majesty, فَقَدْ خَسِرَ خُسْراناً مُبِيناً (has certainly incurred a manifest loss) since he wasted his capital and exchanged his place in Paradise for his place in the Fire.

(١٢٠) يَعِدُهُمْ (He promises them) what He does not fulfill. ** وَيُمَنِّيهِمْ (and fills them with desires) what they do not attain. ** وَما يَعِدُهُمُ الشَّيْطانُ إِلَّا غُرُوراً (The promises that Satan makes them are nothing but delusion.) And that is the manifestation of benefit in what contains harm. This promise is either through corrupt thoughts (الخواطر الفاسدة) or through the tongue of his allies (أوليائه).

In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When this Ayah was revealed: ** وَالَّذِينَ إِذا فَعَلُوا فاحِشَةً أَوْ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ ذَكَرُوا اللهَ فَاسْتَغْفَرُوا لِذُنُوبِهِمْ (And those who, when they commit an indecency or wrong themselves, remember Allah and plead for forgiveness for their sins) Iblis ascended a mountain in Mecca called Thawr, and he screamed with his loudest voice to his ‘afarit (عفاريته), so they gathered around him. They said: "O our master (سيدنا), why have you called us?" He said: "This Ayah has been revealed, so who is responsible for it (فمن لها)?" An ‘ifrit (عفريت) from the devils stood up and said: "I am responsible for it." He said: "With what means (بما ذا)?" He replied to him: "With such and such." He said: "You are not responsible for it." Another stood up and said the same, and he said: "You are not responsible for it." Then al-Waswas al-Khannas (الوسواس الخناس) said: "I am responsible for it." He said: "With what means (بما ذا)?" He said: "I will promise them and fill them with desires until they commit the sin (الخطيئة). Then, when they commit the sin (الخطيئة), I will make them forget seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار)." He said: "You are responsible for it." So, he entrusted him with it until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

(1) Al-Saq’ (السقؤ) with the hamzah (الهمزة) means splitting (الشق). It is said: Faqā’tu ‘aynuhu, afqa’uhā (فقأت عينه أفقؤها), meaning: I split it (شققتها) (M).

(١٢١) أُولئِكَ مَأْواهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَلا يَجِدُونَ عَنْها مَحِيصاً (121) The Fire will be their abode, and they will find no escape from it. A turning away and an escape. (١٢٢) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ سَنُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقًّا وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ اللهِ قِيلاً (122) But those who have faith and do righteous deeds—We shall admit them into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever. Allah’s promise is true, and who is truer in speech than Allah? An eloquent confirmation.

(123) ** لَيْسَ بِأَمانِيِّكُمْ وَلا أَمانِيِّ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ. (It is not by your aspirations, nor by the aspirations of the People of the Book.) Al-Qummi (said): It is not what you wish for, nor what the People of the Book wish for, meaning that you will not be punished for your deeds. ** مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءاً يُجْزَ بِهِ (Whoever commits an evil will be requited for it) swiftly or eventually. In al-Uyun: Isma'il said to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "O my father, what do you say concerning the sinner among us and among others?" He (peace be upon him) replied: ** لَيْسَ بِأَمانِيِّكُمْ وَلا أَمانِيِّ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءاً يُجْزَ بِهِ. (It is not by your aspirations, nor by the aspirations of the People of the Book. Whoever commits an evil will be requited for it.) And in al-Majma', narrated from Abu Hurayrah, he said: When this verse was revealed, we wept and grieved, and we said: "O Messenger of Allah, this verse has left nothing (unpunished)!" He said: "As for that, by Him in Whose hand is my soul, it is indeed revealed just as it is. But rejoice, and be moderate (قَارِبُوا), and be upright (سَدِّدُوا). No calamity afflicts any one of you but that Allah expiates his sin thereby, even a thorn that one of you is pricked with in his foot." I say: The meaning of قَارِبُوا (be moderate) and سَدِّدُوا (be upright) is: be moderate (اقتصدوا) in your affairs and seek uprightness (السداد) and rectitude (الاستقامة) in your deeds, without exaggeration (غلو) or deficiency (تقصير). And there are many narrations from the People of the House (peace be upon them) concerning the meaning of this حديث (narration). And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When this verse was revealed: ** مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءاً يُجْزَ بِهِ ** (Whoever commits an evil will be requited for it), some of the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "How severe a verse this is!" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "Are you not afflicted in your souls, your wealth, and your offspring?" They said: "Yes." He said: "This is among what Allah writes good deeds (الحسنات) for you and wipes out evil deeds (السيئات) for you thereby." And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, when it is part of Allah the Almighty's decree to honor a servant who has a sin, He afflicts him with need (الحاجة). If He does not do that to him, He intensifies death upon him so that He may compensate him for that sin (the حديث). ** وَلا يَجِدْ لَهُ ** (and he will not find for himself) ** مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ وَلِيًّا (besides Allah any guardian) who protects him ** وَلا نَصِيراً** (nor any helper) who repels the punishment from him.

(١٢٤) وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِنَ الصَّالِحاتِ (124) Whoever does righteous deeds, some of them, مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثى وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُولئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ whether male or female, and he is a believer—they shall enter Paradise And it was recited (وقرئ) with the ḍammah on the yāʾ and the fatḥah on the khāʾ [i.e., يُدْخَلُونَ - they shall be made to enter]. وَلا يُظْلَمُونَ نَقِيراً and they will not be wronged so much as the speck on a date-stone. by the diminution of anything from the reward (الثواب). The naqīr (النقير) is the speck (النقطة) that is in the date-stone (النواة).

(١٢٥) ** وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ دِيناً مِمَّنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ (125) And who has a better religion than him who submits his will to Allah He sincerely devoted his soul to Allah ** وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ while he is a doer of good? Bringing forth good deeds (الحسنات). And in the Prophetic Hadith: Al-Ihsan (الإحسان) is that you worship Allah as if you see Him, and if you do not see Him, then surely He sees you. وَاتَّبَعَ مِلَّةَ إِبْراهِيمَ and follows the creed of Abraham which is the religion of Islam and is agreed upon as sound, meaning, he followed his religion, his conduct (سيرته), and his way (طريقته), حَنِيفاً as a ḥanīf inclined away from all other religions. ** وَاتَّخَذَ اللهُ إِبْراهِيمَ خَلِيلاً And Allah took Abraham as a friend. He chose him and distinguished him with the honor (كرامة) of intimate friendship (الخلة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, took Abraham as a servant (عبداً) before He took him as a Prophet (نبياً), and indeed Allah took him as a Prophet before He took him as a Messenger (رسولاً), and indeed Allah took him as a Messenger before He took him as an intimate friend (خليلاً), and indeed Allah took him as an intimate friend before He made him an Imam (إماماً).

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, took Abraham as an intimate friend (خليلاً), the glad tidings of the intimate friendship (الخلة) came to him. Then the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) came to him in the form of a fair young man, wearing two white garments, his head dripping with water and oil. Abraham entered the house and met him coming out of the house. Abraham was a jealous man (غيوراً), and when he went out for a need, he would lock his door and take his key with him, then return and open it. Suddenly, he saw a man standing there, the most handsome of men. He took him by the hand and said: "O servant of Allah, who admitted you into my house?" He replied: "Its Lord admitted me into it." Abraham said: "Its Lord has more right to it than I do. Who are you?" He said: "The Angel of Death." Abraham (peace be upon him) was startled and said: "Have you come to seize my soul?" He said: "No, but Allah has taken a servant as an intimate friend (خليلاً), so I have come with the glad tidings for him." Abraham said: "Who is He? Perhaps I may serve Him until I die." He said: "You are He." So, he entered upon Sarah and told her: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty has taken me as an intimate friend (خليلاً)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Abraham was the father of guests (أبا أضياف). When they were not with him, he would go out seeking them, locking his door and taking the keys, searching for guests. He returned to his house and suddenly found a man, or something resembling a man, inside the house. He said: "O servant of Allah, by whose permission did you enter this house?" He replied: "I entered it by the permission of its Lord." He repeated this three times. Abraham (peace be upon him) recognized that it was Gabriel (جبرئيل). He praised his Lord, then Gabriel said: "My Lord sent me to a servant among His servants whom He is taking as an intimate friend (خليلاً)." Abraham (peace be upon him) said: "Inform me who He is, so that I may serve Him until I die." Gabriel said: "It is you." Abraham asked: "And why is that?" Gabriel replied: "Because you never asked anyone for anything, ever, and you were never asked for anything, ever, and you said 'No'."

And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, Abraham (peace be upon him) was the first for whom sand (الرمل) was transformed into flour (دقيقاً). This was because he intended to visit a friend of his in Egypt to borrow food, but did not find him at home. He disliked returning with the donkey empty, so he filled his sack (جرابه) with sand. When he entered his house, he left the donkey alone with Sarah out of shyness, and he entered the house and slept. Sarah opened the sack and found the finest flour possible. She baked and presented him with delicious food. Abraham (peace be upon him) asked: "Where did you get this from?" She replied: "From the flour you carried from your Egyptian intimate friend (خليلك المصري)." Abraham said: "Indeed, He is My intimate friend (خليلي), but He is not Egyptian." For that reason, he was granted the intimate friendship (الخلة). So, he thanked Him, praised Him, and ate.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in a Hadith: Our saying that Abraham (peace be upon him) is the intimate friend (خليل) of Allah is derived from al-Khullah (الخلة) (1). And al-Khullah (الخلة) means poverty (الفقر) and destitution (الفاقة). Thus, he was an intimate friend (خليلاً) to his Lord, poor toward Him, devoted (منقطعاً), and abstaining (متعففاً), turning away (معرضاً), and self-sufficient (مستغنياً) from all others. This was because when he was intended to be thrown into the fire and was cast by the catapult (المنجنيق), Allah sent Gabriel (جبرئيل) and said to him: "Overtake My servant." So, Gabriel came to him and met him in the air and said: "Entrust me with whatever you wish, for Allah has sent me to help you." Abraham replied: "Rather, Allah is sufficient for me, and [He is] the best trustee (حسبي الله ونعم الوكيل). I ask none other than Him, and I have no need except for Him." So, He named him His intimate friend (خليله), meaning His poor one (فقيره), His needy one (محتاجه), and the one devoted to Him, turning away from all besides Him. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: And if the meaning of that is taken from al-Khullah (الخلة), which is that he penetrated His meanings (تخلل معانيه) and became aware of secrets that none other was aware of, then its meaning is the one who knows Him and His affairs. And this does not necessitate likening Allah to His creation. Do you not see that if he had not devoted himself to Him, he would not have been His intimate friend (خليله), and if He did not inform him of His secrets, He would not have been His intimate friend (خليله)?

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah took Abraham as a friend only because he never sought anyone and never asked anyone, ever, other than Allah.

And in al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Due to the abundance of his prostrations (سجوده) upon the earth.

And from al-Hadi (peace be upon him): Due to the abundance of his prayers (صلواته) upon Muhammad and his Household (may the prayers of Allah be upon them all).

And from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Due to his feeding of food (اطعامه الطعام) and his prayer at night while people were asleep.

I say: There is no contradiction among these narrations, because they all share the meaning of his devotion (انقطاعه) to Allah and his self-sufficiency (استغنائه) from all besides Him, and that this was the reason for Allah taking him as an intimate friend (خليلاً). What indicates this meaning is what is mentioned in some narrations: That the angels said to one another: "Our Lord has taken an intimate friend (خليلاً) from a drop [of semen], and He has given him a great and abundant kingdom (ملكاً عظيماً جزيلاً)." So, Allah the Almighty revealed to the angels: "Go to the most ascetic (أزهدكم) and the chief (رئيسكم) among you." Agreement fell upon Gabriel (جبرئيل) and Michael (ميكائيل). They descended to Abraham on a day when he was gathering his sheep. Abraham (peace be upon him) had four thousand shepherds (راع), four thousand dogs, around the neck of every dog was a collar weighing a mann (منّ) of red gold, forty thousand milking sheep (غنمة حلابة), and whatever Allah willed of horses (الخيل) and camels (الجمال). The two angels stood at the two ends of the gathering. One of them said, with a pleasant (1) voice: Subbuh Quddus (سبوح قدوس) (Glorified, Holy). The second answered him: Rabb al-Mala'ikati wa-r-Ruh (ربّ الملائكة والروح) (Lord of the angels and the Spirit). Abraham said: "Repeat those words, and you shall have half of my wealth." Then he said: "Repeat them, and you shall have my wealth, my children, and my body." Then the angels of the heavens cried out: "This is generosity! This is generosity!" Then they heard a caller from the Throne (العرش) saying: "The intimate friend (الخليل) is in agreement with his Intimate Friend (خليله)."

(1) Al-Khullah (الخلة): Need (الحاجة), poverty (الفقر), and destitution (الخصاصة) (Q)

(١٢٦) وَلِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ (To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth.) as creation (خلقاً), command (أمراً), and ownership (ملكاً). So He is independent of all His creation, and all His creation are in need of Him. وَكانَ اللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُحِيطاً (And Allah comprehends all things.) in knowledge (علماً) and power (قدرةً).

footnote (١) اللذة نقيض الألم ج لذات لذه وبه لذاذاً ولذاذة والتذه وبه استلذه وجده لذيذاً (ق)

** وَيَسْتَفْتُونَكَ ** And they ask you for the fatwa (الفتوى), meaning the clarification of the ruling, ** فِي النِّساءِ** concerning women, regarding their inheritance.

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was asked about women, what share of the inheritance belonged to them. So Allah revealed the quarter (الربع) and the eighth (الثمن). قُلِ اللهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ فِيهِنَ ** He clarifies for you what you asked concerning their affair, وَما يُتْلى عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الْكِتابِ meaning, and He also clarifies for you what is recited to you in the Quran ** فِي يَتامَى النِّساءِ اللَّاتِي لا تُؤْتُونَهُنَ you do not give them ** ما كُتِبَ لَهُنَ of the inheritance. The people of Jahiliyyah (الجاهلية) did not allow the minor or the woman to inherit. They used to say: "We only allow inheritance for those who fight and defend the sanctity (الحريم)." So Allah the Almighty revealed the verses of the prescribed shares (آيات الفرائض) which are at the beginning of the Surah, and this is the meaning of His saying: ** لا تُؤْتُونَهُنَّ ما كُتِبَ لَهُنَ. Thus it is in al-Majma' narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him).

And al-Qummi added: They used to consider that good in their religion. When Allah revealed the prescribed shares of inheritance (فرائض المواريث), they felt great distress (وجداً شديداً) (1) because of it. So they said: "Let us go to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and mention this to Him, perhaps He will abandon it or change it." So they came to Him and said: "O Messenger of Allah, the young girl receives half of what her father and brother left, and the small boy is given inheritance, yet neither of them rides a horse, nor acquires (يحوز) (2) the spoils of war (الغنيمة), nor fights the enemy." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I have been commanded with this." وَتَرْغَبُونَ أَنْ تَنْكِحُوهُنَ from marrying them.

In al-Qummi: A man would have an orphan girl under his guardianship (في حجره). If she was ugly (دميمة) (3) and worthless (ساقطة), meaning foolish (حمقاء), the man would be averse to marrying her, but he would not give her her wealth so that someone else might marry her for her wealth. He would prevent her from marriage and wait for her death so that he could inherit her. So Allah forbade that. وَالْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ And He gives you a ruling concerning the downtrodden ** مِنَ الْوِلْدانِ among the small boys, that you give them their rights, because they are included in what is recited to you: "And give the orphans their wealth," as previously mentioned. ** وَأَنْ تَقُومُوا لِلْيَتامى بِالْقِسْطِ And He gives you a ruling concerning that you maintain justice toward the orphans regarding their persons and their wealth. ** وَما تَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ** concerning the affair of women, orphans, and other matters, فَإِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِهِ عَلِيماً Allah is indeed aware of it, a promise for whoever prefers good in that matter.

(1) Wajada (وجد) in sadness (الحزن) is wajdan (وجداً) with a fathah (بالفتح), and tawajadtu li-fulān (توجدت لفلان) means I grieved for him (حزنت له) (al-Majma').

(2) Al-Hawz (الحوز) is gathering (الجمع). Whoever annexes something to himself has acquired (حازه) it, acquiring (يحوزه) it by hawzan (حوزاً) or hiyāzah (حيازة) (Sihah).

(3) Al-Damīmah (الدميمة) is the one ugly in appearance (القبيحة المنظر). Al-Sāqitah (الساقطة) is the one who has no rank (لا رتبة لها). Al-Hamqā' (الحمقاء) is an explanation for al-Sāqitah, and she is the one whose intellect is deficient (قل عقلها). The summary of the intended meaning is that when the ugly girl (القبيحة) had neither beauty, nor rank, nor maturity (رشد), the man would be averse to marrying her, but desiring her wealth, he would not allow her to marry until she died so that he could inherit her.

** وَيَسْتَفْتُونَكَ فِي النِّساءِ قُلِ اللهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ فِيهِنَّ وَما يُتْلى عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الْكِتابِ فِي يَتامَى النِّساءِ اللَّاتِي لا تُؤْتُونَهُنَّ ما كُتِبَ لَهُنَّ وَتَرْغَبُونَ أَنْ تَنْكِحُوهُنَّ وَالْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ الْوِلْدانِ وَأَنْ تَقُومُوا لِلْيَتامى بِالْقِسْطِ وَما تَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِهِ عَلِيماً (١٢٧) ** (127) They ask you for a ruling concerning women. Say: 'Allah gives you a ruling concerning them, and what is recited to you in the Book concerning the female orphans whom you do not give what is ordained for them, and you desire to marry them, and concerning the downtrodden among children, and that you maintain justice toward the orphans. And whatever good you do, Allah is indeed aware of it.'

(١٢٨) وَإِنِ امْرَأَةٌ خافَتْ مِنْ بَعْلِها (128) If a woman fears from her husband) expected, due to what appeared to her of signs (المخايل), ill-will (نُشُوزاً), meaning an aversion from her, a distancing from her company, a dislike for her, and a withholding of her rights, ** أَوْ إِعْراضاً (or aversion) by reducing his sitting with her and conversing with her, ** فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِما أَنْ يُصْلِحا بَيْنَهُما صُلْحاً. (there is no sin upon them if they reconcile between themselves amicably.)

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the woman who is with the man, and he dislikes her. So he says to her: "I want to divorce you." So she says to him: "Do not do it, for I dislike being gloated over (by others). But look after my night (turn), and do with it what you wish. And whatever else there is besides that, it is (1) Yours, and leave me in my state." And this is the Almighty's saying: ** فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِما أَنْ يُصْلِحا بَيْنَهُما صُلْحاً. This is the reconciliation (الصلح).

And al-Qummi narrated what is similar in meaning, along with mentioning the reason for the revelation. وَالصُّلْحُ خَيْرٌ (and reconciliation is better) than separation (الفرقة) and bad companionship (سوء العشرة). ** وَأُحْضِرَتِ الْأَنْفُسُ الشُّحَ** (Avarice is ingrained in [human] souls) because they are naturally disposed to it. So the woman hardly allows/forgives the husband's aversion from her and his shortcoming in her right, nor does the man allow/forgive keeping her and fulfilling her right as he ought to, if he dislikes her or loves another woman.

Al-Qummi said regarding ** وَأُحْضِرَتِ الْأَنْفُسُ الشُّحَ (Avarice is ingrained in [human] souls): "Some souls chose it (avarice), and some did not choose it." وَإِنْ تُحْسِنُوا (But if you are virtuous in conduct) in companionship ** وَتَتَّقُوا (and are wary [of Allah]) ill-will (النشوز), aversion (الإِعراض), and diminishing the right, ** فَإِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ (then Allah is indeed) of what you do, whether it be virtuous conduct (الإِحسان) or dispute (الخصومة), All-aware (خَبِيراً), and He will recompense you for it.

(١٢٩) وَلَنْ تَسْتَطِيعُوا أَنْ تَعْدِلُوا بَيْنَ النِّساءِ (You will not be able to maintain parity among women) (You will not be able) to equalize between them in love (المحبة) and affection (المودة) in the heart, as passed in the beginning of the Surah from al-Kafi. And al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi narrated it from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). And in al-Majma, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): That its meaning is equalization (التسوية) in all matters from all aspects, وَلَوْ حَرَصْتُمْ (even if you are eager) for that with all eagerness (الحرص), for indeed that is not within your power (إليكم), nor do you possess it (تملكونه), nor are you burdened with it (تكلفونه), nor will you be held accountable for it (تؤاخذون به). In al-Majma, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: That He used to divide (His time) among His wives and say: "O Allah, this is my division (قسمتي) in what I possess, so do not blame me for what You possess and I do not possess." فَلا تَمِيلُوا كُلَّ الْمَيْلِ (So do not incline with a total inclination) By abandoning what is possible (المستطاع) and being unjust (الجور) towards the one who is undesirable (المرغوب عنها), for indeed what cannot be attained completely (كله) should not be abandoned completely (كله). فَتَذَرُوها كَالْمُعَلَّقَةِ (leaving her in a state of suspense) (Leaving her) like the one suspended (المُعَلَّقَة), who is neither married (ذات بعل) nor widowed/unmarried (أيماً). In al-Majma, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) from his forefathers (peace be upon them): That the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to divide (His time) among His wives during His illness, and He would be carried around among them. He said: And it is narrated: That Ali (peace and blessings be upon him) had two wives, and when it was the day of one, he would not perform ablution (يتوضأ) in the house of the other.

footnote (١) The summary is that she reconciles with her husband regarding the allowance (*إباحة*) of her rights concerning marriage (*الزوجية*), cohabitation (*المضاجعة*), maintenance (*النفقة*), dowry (*المهر*), and the like, either entirely or partially, according to what they both agree upon (*تراضيا عليه*).

(١٣٠) وَإِنْ تُصْلِحُوا (130) And if you make peace regarding what you corrupt of their affairs (i.e., the wives' affairs) ** وَتَتَّقُوا and be wary regarding what is to come (in the future), then Allah is indeed All-forgiving, All-merciful. He forgives you what has passed before you. وَإِنْ يَتَفَرَّقا يُغْنِ اللهُ كُلًّا مِنْ سَعَتِهِ But if the two separate, Allah will enrich each of them out of His bounty. It is said: It means that if each of them refuses reconciliation with the other, and they separate by divorce, Allah will enrich each of them, making them independent of the other, through a substitute (ببدلٍ) or consolation (سلوٍ) from His wealth (غناه) and power (قدرته), and He will provide for him/her from His grace (فضله). And Allah is All-bounteous, All-wise.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): A man complained to him about poverty (الحاجة), so he commanded him to marry. Then the poverty intensified for him, so he commanded him to separate (divorce), and he became wealthy (أثرى) (1) and his condition improved. So he (al-Sadiq عليه السلام) said to him: "I commanded you with two commands that Allah commanded." The Almighty said: وَأَنْكِحُوا الْأَيامى And marry off the single among you up to His saying: إِنْ يَكُونُوا فُقَراءَ يُغْنِهِمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** If they are poor, Allah will enrich them out of His grace. And He said: وَإِنْ يَتَفَرَّقا يُغْنِ اللهُ كُلًّا مِنْ سَعَتِهِ But if the two separate, Allah will enrich each of them out of His bounty.**

** وَلِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ (131) And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. It is not difficult for Him to grant independence after separation, nor comfort after desolation, serving as an alert regarding the perfection of His power and the vastness of His dominion. وَلَقَدْ وَصَّيْنَا الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ from the Jews and the Christians and others, وَإِيَّاكُمْ أَنِ اتَّقُوا اللهَ. and [We advise] you to be wary of Allah.**

In Misbah ash-Shari'ah, Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said regarding this Ayah: "Allah has gathered what those who advise one another (المتواصون) advise (1), from the first and the last generations, in one characteristic, which is piety (التقوى). And in it is the sum of every righteous act of worship, and by it, whoever reached the highest ranks (الدرجات العلى) reached them." وَإِنْ تَكْفُرُوا فَإِنَّ لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ But if you are faithless, [you should know that] to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. He is the Owner of the entire dominion. He is not harmed by your disbelief (كفرانكم) and your disobedience (عصيانكم), just as He is not benefited by your gratitude (شكركم) and your piety (تقواكم). And He only advised you out of His mercy, not out of His need. ** وَكانَ اللهُ غَنِيًّا independent of creation and their acts of worship, حَمِيداً ** in His essence, whether He is praised or not praised.

(1) A man becomes wealthy (أثرى) when his wealth increases (ص).

**(١٣٢) وَلِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (132) To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth.

Everything indicates, by its need, His Self-Sufficiency (غناه), and by what flowed upon it of existence (الوجود) and perfection (الكمال), that He is Praiseworthy (حميداً). وَكَفى بِاللهِ وَكِيلاً (and Allah suffices as trustee.) (He is) a Preserver (حافظاً) of all; the weight of an atom (مثقال ذرة) in them (the heavens and the earth) does not escape Him. And it was said that it refers back to His saying: ** يُغْنِ اللهُ كُلًّا مِنْ سَعَتِهِ (Allah will enrich each of them out of His abundance), for He entrusts Himself with the sufficiency (كفايتهما) of both of them, and what is between them is a confirmation (تقرير) of that.

(١٣٣) إِنْ يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ (133) If He wishes, He will take you away, He will annihilate you. ** أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ وَيَأْتِ بِآخَرِينَ O mankind, and bring others [in your stead]. and He will bring into existence other people in your place. ** وَكانَ اللهُ عَلى ذلِكَ Allah is indeed able to do that. regarding that, which is annihilation (الإِعدام) and creation (الإيجاد), ** قَدِيراً of profound power (بليغ القدرة); no desired thing can frustrate Him.

In al-Majma' it is narrated: When this Ayah was revealed, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم struck his hand upon the back of Salman رضي الله عنه and said: "They are the people of this one," meaning the non-Arabs (عجم) of Persia (الفرس).

(١٣٤) مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ ثَوابَ الدُّنْيا (Whoever desires the reward of the world) like one who strives (يُجَاهِدُ) for spoils (الْغَنِيمَة). ** فَعِنْدَ اللهِ ثَوابُ الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ (with Allah is the reward of the world and the Hereafter) So let him seek both rewards (الثَّوَابَيْنِ) entirely from Allah. What is wrong with him that he contents himself with the baser of the two and abandons the nobler of the two, especially since if he sought the nobler, the baser would not escape him?

In al-Kafi and al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq, from his father, from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) that he said: The wise men (الْحُكَمَاء) and the jurists (الْفُقَهَاء)—when some of them corresponded with others—would write with three things, and no fourth thing accompanied them: Whoever has the Hereafter as his concern (هِمَّتُهُ), Allah suffices him in his concern regarding the world. And whoever rectifies his inner self (سَرِيرَتَهُ), Allah rectifies his outer self (عَلَانِيَتَهُ). And whoever rectifies that which is between him and Allah, Allah rectifies that which is between him and the people.

And in al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The world (الدُّنْيَا) is both seeking and sought. So, whoever seeks the world, death seeks him until it removes him from it. And whoever seeks the Hereafter, the world seeks him until it fulfills his provision (رِزْقَهُ). وَكانَ اللهُ سَمِيعاً بَصِيراً (and Allah is All-hearing, All-seeing) He is Knower of the objectives (الْأَغْرَاض), so He recompenses each according to his intention (قَصْدِهِ).

footnote (١) تواصى القوم أي أوصى بعضهم بعضاً (ص) (1) تَوَاصَى الْقَوْمُ (The people mutually advised) means some of them advised others (أَوْصَى بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضاً) (ص)

(١٣٥) ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُونُوا قَوَّامِينَ بِالْقِسْطِ (135) O you who have faith, be maintainers of justice, [Be] diligent in upholding justice, striving earnestly to establish it, ** شُهَداءَ لِلَّهِ [witnesses for Allah,] with truth, establishing your testimonies for the sake of Allah, ** وَلَوْ عَلى أَنْفُسِكُمْ [even if it is against yourselves,] even if the testimony is against yourselves, by admitting [the truth] against them (yourselves), ** أَوِ الْوالِدَيْنِ وَالْأَقْرَبِينَ إِنْ يَكُنْ [or your parents or your relatives. If he be] the one testified against or the one testified for ** غَنِيًّا أَوْ فَقِيراً [rich or poor,] then do not refrain from establishing testimony for the rich against the poor due to the self-sufficiency of the one testified for and the poverty of the one testified against, nor from establishing testimony for the poor against the rich out of contempt for the poor, or reverence for the rich, or fear of him, or shyness toward him. ** فَاللهُ أَوْلى بِهِما [Allah is a better guardian of them.] [Allah is a better guardian] of both of them—the rich and the poor—and more watchful over them. ** فَلا تَتَّبِعُوا الْهَوى أَنْ تَعْدِلُوا [So do not follow your desires, lest you should swerve.] [Lest you should swerve] (أَنْ تَعْدِلُوا) means: lest you deviate from the truth (العدول), or it means: for the sake of being just (العدل) in the testimony. This is a prohibition against following desires in establishing it, such as observing friendship, enmity, alienation, tribalism (عصبية), or anything else. وَإِنْ تَلْوُوا [And if you twist] your tongues away from the testimony of truth أَوْ تُعْرِضُوا** [or turn away] from rendering it.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ** إِنْ تَلْوُوا (1) means: that you alter (تبدلوا) the testimony. ** أَوْ تُعْرِضُوا means: that you conceal it (تكتموها).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** إِنْ تَلْوُوا means: the command (الأمر). ** أَوْ تُعْرِضُوا means: from what you have been commanded with.

And it was recited (وقرئ) as in talū (ان تلوا), meaning: if you undertake (وليتم) the establishment of the testimony. ** فَإِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيراً** [then Allah is indeed well aware of what you do.] and will recompense you for it.

(١٣٦) ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا with their tongues (ألسنتهم) and their outward appearance (ظاهرهم) ** آمَنُوا with your hearts (قلوبكم) and your inner being (باطنكم) ** بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَالْكِتابِ الَّذِي نَزَّلَ عَلى رَسُولِهِ meaning the Quran ** وَالْكِتابِ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ مِنْ قَبْلُ the Torah (التوراة) and the Gospel (الإنجيل) and others. The genus (الجنس) is intended by it. And it was recited with the passive voice (على البناء للمفعول) in both (instances of the word 'Book'). ** وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِاللهِ وَمَلائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ And whoever disbelieves in anything (بشيء) of that ** فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلالاً بَعِيداً from the intended goal (المقصد) such that he can scarcely return to his path (طريقه).

يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَالْكِتابِ الَّذِي نَزَّلَ عَلى رَسُولِهِ وَالْكِتابِ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِاللهِ وَمَلائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلالاً بَعِيداً (١٣٦) (O you who have believed, believe in Allah and His Apostle and the Book which He has sent down to His Apostle and the Book which He sent down earlier. And whoever disbelieves in Allah and His angels, His Books, His apostles, and the Last Day has certainly strayed a far straying.)

(١٣٧) The Almighty's saying: ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (Indeed, those who have believed) like the Jews who believed in Musa (peace be upon him), and like the hypocrites who believed in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. ** ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا (then disbelieved) then the Jews worshipped the Calf, and the hypocrites apostatized. ** ثُمَّ آمَنُوا (then believed) they returned to faith. ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا then the Jews disbelieved in 'Isa (Jesus), and the hypocrites apostatized once again. ** ثُمَّ ازْدادُوا كُفْراً ** (then increased in disbelief) in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and they persisted (١) in error (الغي) and insisted upon it until they died.

Al-Qummi narrated that it was revealed concerning those who believed in the Messenger of Allah with mere affirmation (إقراراً) but not sincere belief (تصديقاً), then disbelieved when they wrote the document among themselves that they would never return the authority (الأمر) to his Household (أهل بيته) ever. Then, when the Wilayah (الولاية) was revealed and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took the covenant from them for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), they believed with mere affirmation (إقراراً) but not sincere belief (تصديقاً). Then, when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed away, they disbelieved, and thus increased in disbelief.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: "They are the two, and the third, and the fourth, and 'Abd al-Rahman, and Talhah, and they were seven (the Hadith)." And He mentioned therein the stages (مراتب) of their faith and their disbelief.

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It was revealed concerning so-and-so, and so-and-so, and so-and-so. They believed in the Messenger of Allah in the beginning, then they disbelieved when the Wilayah (الولاية) was presented to them, when He said: 'For whomever I am his Mawla (مولاه), 'Ali is his Mawla (مولاه).'" Then they believed through the pledge of allegiance (البيعة) to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), when they said to him: "By the command of Allah and the command of His Messenger," and they pledged allegiance to him. Then they disbelieved when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed away, and they did not affirm the pledge of allegiance (البيعة). Then they increased in disbelief by taking the pledge of allegiance (البيعة) for themselves from those who had pledged allegiance to him. Thus, nothing of faith remained in them.

And in another narration from both of them (peace be upon them): "It was revealed concerning 'Abd Allah ibn Abi Sarh, whom 'Uthman sent to Egypt." He said: "And they increased in disbelief until nothing of faith remained in him."

And in another [narration]: "Whoever claims that wine (الخمر) is forbidden (حرام) then drinks it, and whoever claims that fornication (الزنا) is forbidden (حرام) then commits fornication, and whoever claims that Zakat (الزكاة) is a right (حقّ) but does not render it: لَمْ يَكُنِ اللهُ لِيَغْفِرَ لَهُمْ وَلا لِيَهْدِيَهُمْ سَبِيلاً (Allah will not forgive them, nor will He guide them to a way) to Paradise, because their insights (بصائرهم) have been blinded from the truth, so returning to it is not possible for them.

(١) Lawaytu al-habl (لويت الحبل): I twisted the rope. Wa lawa al-rajul ra'sahu (ولوى الرجل رأسه) and alwa bi-ra'sihi (وألوى برأسه): He inclined and turned away (from Sihah (صحاح)).

(١٣٨) ** بَشِّرِ الْمُنافِقِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُمْ عَذاباً أَلِيماً (138) Inform the hypocrites that there is a painful punishment for them. (١٣٩) ** الَّذِينَ يَتَّخِذُونَ الْكافِرِينَ أَوْلِياءَ مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَيَبْتَغُونَ عِنْدَهُمُ الْعِزَّةَ (139) those who take the faithless as allies instead of the faithful. Do they seek honour from them? They seek honor (يَتَعَزَّزُونَ) through their alliance with them. ** فَإِنَّ الْعِزَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعاً (Indeed, all honour belongs to Allah.) No one gains honor except the one whom Allah honors. He has already decreed honor for His allies, just as He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ** وَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ وَلِرَسُولِهِ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (Yet all might belongs to Allah and His Apostle and the faithful,) Al-Qummi (said): It was revealed concerning Banu Umayyah when they allied themselves upon the condition that they would not return the authority (الأمر) to Banu Hashim.

footnote (١) *تمادى في الذنوب* (He persisted in sins) means when he insisted (*لَجَّ*), continued (*دَاوَمَ*), and expanded (*تَوَسَّعَ*) in them. Similar examples are *تمادى في الجهل* (He persisted in ignorance) and *تمادى في غيه* (He persisted in his error/misguidance). (*مجمع*)

(١٤٠) وَقَدْ نَزَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الْكِتابِ (The Quran) ** أَنْ إِذا سَمِعْتُمْ آياتِ اللهِ يُكْفَرُ بِها وَيُسْتَهْزَأُ بِها فَلا تَقْعُدُوا مَعَهُمْ حَتَّى يَخُوضُوا فِي حَدِيثٍ غَيْرِهِ**. (4:140) He has already sent down to you in the Book that when you hear the signs of Allah being denied and ridiculed, do not sit with them until they engage in a subject other than that.

In al-Qummi: ** آياتِ اللهِ** (the signs of Allah) are the Imams (peace be upon them).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), concerning its interpretation: "If you hear a man denying the truth (الحق), lying about it, and slandering (يقع) its people (أهله), then stand up from his presence and do not sit with him."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Allah has made it obligatory upon the hearing (السمع) to keep itself pure from listening to what Allah has forbidden, and to turn away from what is not lawful for it of what Allah has prohibited, and from listening attentively to what angers Allah. So He said concerning that: ** وَقَدْ نَزَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (He has already sent down to you) ** الْآيَةَ ** (the Ayah)."

He said: "Then He made an exception for the case of forgetfulness, so He said: ** وَإِمَّا يُنْسِيَنَّكَ الشَّيْطانُ فَلا تَقْعُدْ بَعْدَ الذِّكْرى مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ إِنَّكُمْ إِذاً مِثْلُهُمْ** (4:140) Should Satan make you forget, do not sit, after the remembrance, with the wrongdoing lot. Indeed, in that case you will be like them."

(You will be like them) in disbelief (الكفران) if you approve of it, and if not, then in sin (الإثم) due to your ability to denounce (الإِنكار) and turn away (الإِعراض).

** إِنَّ اللهَ جامِعُ الْمُنافِقِينَ وَالْكافِرِينَ (those who sit and those sat with) فِي جَهَنَّمَ جَمِيعاً**. (4:140) Indeed Allah will gather the hypocrites and the faithless all together in hell.

(١٤١) الَّذِينَ يَتَرَبَّصُونَ بِكُمْ (Those who wait [for a turn of fortune] for you) They await the occurrence of some matter concerning you.

** فَإِنْ كانَ لَكُمْ فَتْحٌ مِنَ اللهِ قالُوا أَلَمْ نَكُنْ مَعَكُمْ** (If there is a victory for you from Allah, they say, 'Were we not with you?') [meaning] supporting you, so give us a share in what you have gained as spoils.

** وَإِنْ كانَ لِلْكافِرِينَ نَصِيبٌ** (But if the faithless have a share) of the war

** قالُوا ** (they say) to the disbelievers

** أَلَمْ نَسْتَحْوِذْ عَلَيْكُمْ ('Did we not prevail over you?') Did we not overcome you and have the power to kill you, yet we spared you? And al-istihwādh (الاستحواذ) means domination (الاستيلاء). The expected grammatical form (القياس) was that it should be said أَلَمْ نَسْتَحِذْ, but it came according to the original form (على الأصل).

** وَنَمْنَعْكُمْ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (and defend you from the faithful?) by abandoning them against you, through suggesting things that weakened their hearts, and by being slow in supporting them, and by being spies (عيوناً) for you until they turned away from you and you overcame them. So, include us in what you have attained. It is said that the victory of the Muslims was only called a 'victory' (فتحاً) and the success of the disbelievers a 'share' (نصيباً) due to the baseness (لخسة) of their fortune, for it is restricted to a worldly matter (أمر دنيوي) that quickly vanishes.

** فَاللهُ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ** (Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection) He will decide between you with the truth.

** وَلَنْ يَجْعَلَ اللهُ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ سَبِيلاً ** (and Allah will never grant the faithless any way [to prevail] over the faithful.) by means of proof (بالحجة), even if it is permissible for them to overcome them by force (بالقوة).

في العيون عن الرضا عليه السلام : قيل له ان في سواد الكوفة قوماً يزعمون أن النبيّ صلّى الله عليه وآله وسلم لم يقع عليه السهو فقال كذبوا لعنهم الله ان الذي لا يسهو هو الله الذي لا إله إلّا هو قيل وفيهم قوم يزعمون أن الحسين بن علي صلوات الله عليهما لم يقتل وأنّه القي شبهة على حنظلة بن سعد الشاميّ وأنّه رفع إلى السماء كما رفع عيسى بن مريم عليه السلام ويحتجون بهذه الآية ** وَلَنْ يَجْعَلَ اللهُ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ سَبِيلاً فقال كذبوا عليهم غضب الله ولعنته وكفروا بتكذيبهم النبيّ صلّى الله عليه وآله وسلم في إخباره بأن الحسين عليه السلام سيقتل والله لقد قتل الحسين بن علي صلوات الله عليهما وقتل من كان خيراً من الحسين أمير المؤمنين والحسن بن عليّ عليهما السلام وما منا الا مقتول واني والله لمقتول باغتيال (١) من يغتالني أعرف ذلك بعهد معهود إليّ من رسول الله أخبره به جبرئيل عن ربّ العالمين. فاما قوله عزّ وجلّ ** وَلَنْ يَجْعَلَ اللهُ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ سَبِيلاً فانه يقول لن يجعل الله لكافر على مؤمن حجة ولقد أخبر الله تعالى عن كفّار قتلوا نبيين بغير حقّ ومع قتلهم إياهم لن يجعل الله لهم على أنبيائه سبيلاً من طريق الحجة.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): It was said to Him: "There are people in the vicinity (سواد) of Kufa who claim that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was not subject to forgetfulness (السهو)." He said: "They lie, may Allah curse them. Indeed, the One Who is not subject to forgetfulness is Allah, the One besides Whom there is no god." It was said: "And among them are people who claim that al-Husayn ibn Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both) was not killed, and that a likeness (شبهة) was cast upon Hanzalah ibn Sa'd al-Shami, and that He was raised to heaven just as Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him) was raised, and they use this Ayah as proof: ** وَلَنْ يَجْعَلَ اللهُ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ سَبِيلاً (and Allah will never grant the faithless any way [to prevail] over the faithful.)" He said: "They lie, upon them be the wrath and curse of Allah. They have committed disbelief (كفروا) by denying the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in His informing that al-Husayn (peace be upon him) would be killed. By Allah, al-Husayn ibn Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both) was certainly killed, and one who was better than al-Husayn was killed: the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and al-Hasan ibn Ali (peace be upon them both). And there is none among us except that he is killed. And I, by Allah, will certainly be killed by the assassination (باغتيال) (1) of the one who assassinates me. I know this through a covenant entrusted to me from the Messenger of Allah, which Gabriel informed Him of, from the Lord of the worlds." As for His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: ** وَلَنْ يَجْعَلَ اللهُ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ سَبِيلاً (and Allah will never grant the faithless any way [to prevail] over the faithful.), He says that Allah will not grant a disbeliever any proof (حجة) over a believer. And Allah, the Exalted, has indeed informed about disbelievers who killed two Prophets unjustly (بغير حقّ), yet despite their killing them, Allah did not grant them any way (سبيلاً) over His Prophets by way of proof (من طريق الحجة).

footnote (١) It occurred among the people: He slandered them (*وقيعة اغتابهم*) (M).

(١٤٢) إِنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ يُخادِعُونَ اللهَ وَهُوَ خادِعُهُمْ ** (Indeed, the hypocrites seek to deceive Allah, but He is deceiving them) Its interpretation passed in the beginning of Sūrat al-Baqarah. وَإِذا قامُوا إِلَى الصَّلاةِ قامُوا كُسالى (and when they stand up for prayer, they stand up lazily) being heavy/sluggish (متثاقلين), like one compelled to perform the act. And it was recited كَسَالَى with fatḥah. ** يُراؤُنَ النَّاسَ ** (They show off to the people) so that they may assume them to be believers. ** وَلا يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً (and do not remember Allah except a little) Since the one who shows off does not act except in the presence of the one to whom he shows off.

In al-Kāfī, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Whoever remembers Allah in secret (في السر) has remembered Allah much (كثيراً). Indeed, the hypocrites used to remember Allah openly (علانية) but did not remember Him in secret (في السر). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: يُراؤُنَ النَّاسَ وَلا يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً (They show off to the people and do not remember Allah except a little)."

(١٤٣) ** مُذَبْذَبِينَ بَيْنَ ذلِكَ (143) wavering in between that, Wavering between faith (الإِيمان) and disbelief (الكفر), derived from al-dhubdhabah (الذبذبة), which is making something unstable (مضطرباً). Its origin is al-dhabb (الذب) meaning expulsion (الطرد). And it was recited with a kasrah on the dhāl (الذال), meaning they make their hearts or their religion waver (يذبذبون). ** لا إِلى هؤُلاءِ وَلا إِلى هؤُلاءِ neither to these nor to those. They do not become entirely with the believers, nor entirely with the disbelievers, likewise. They manifest faith (الإِيمان) just as the believers manifest it, but they do not harbor it (in their hearts) as they (the believers) harbor it. And they harbor disbelief (الكفر) just as the disbelievers harbor it, but they do not manifest it as they (the disbelievers) manifest it. ** وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللهُ فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُ سَبِيلاً And whomever Allah leads astray, you will never find any way for him. A path (طريقاً) or a way (مذهباً). Similar to His saying: وَمَنْ لَمْ يَجْعَلِ اللهُ لَهُ نُوراً فَما لَهُ مِنْ نُورٍ**. (24:40) And for him whom Allah does not appoint a light, there is no light for him.

footnote (١) غاله الشيء واغتاله إذا أخذه من حيث لم يدر إلى أن قال واغتاله قتله غيلة (ص)

(١٤٤) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَتَّخِذُوا الْكافِرِينَ أَوْلِياءَ (O you who have faith! Do not take the faithless as allies) ** مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (instead of the faithful) For indeed, it is the deed of the hypocrites (المنافقين) and their distinguishing mark (شعارهم). أَتُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَجْعَلُوا لِلَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ سُلْطاناً مُبِيناً (Do you desire to give Allah a manifest authority over yourselves?) a clear proof (حجة بينة). For indeed, befriending the disbelievers (موالاة الكافرين) is the evidence of hypocrisy (النفاق).

(١٤٥) إِنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الْأَسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ (145) Indeed, the hypocrites will be in the lowest reach of the Fire, For the Fire has reaches (دركات), just as Paradise has degrees (درجات). It was named this because they are successive (متداركة), continuous (متتابعة), some of them above others. And the lowest of them is that which is in the bottom of Hell (جهنم). ** وَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُمْ نَصِيراً** and you will never find any helper for them, who extracts them from it.

(١٤٦) ** إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا (146) (except those who repent) from hypocrisy (النفاق) ** وَأَصْلَحُوا (and reform) what they corrupted of their secrets (أسرارهم) and their states (أحوالهم) during the state of hypocrisy (النفاق) ** وَاعْتَصَمُوا بِاللهِ (and hold fast to Allah) and trusted in Him and adhered firmly to His religion ** وَأَخْلَصُوا دِينَهُمْ لِلَّهِ (and dedicate their religion sincerely to Allah) not desiring by their obedience anything except His Countenance (وجهه). Then those are with the believers, counted among them in both abodes (الدارين). ** وَسَوْفَ يُؤْتِ اللهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَجْراً عَظِيماً** (and soon Allah will give the faithful a great reward) so they will share with them in it.

(١٤٧) ** ما يَفْعَلُ اللهُ بِعَذابِكُمْ إِنْ شَكَرْتُمْ وَآمَنْتُمْ** (147) What will Allah do with your punishment if you are grateful and believe?

He seeks satisfaction through it from anger (غيظاً), or repels harm through it, or attracts benefit through it. Glory be to Him! He is the Self-sufficient (الغني), the Exalted (المتعال), above benefit and harm. He only punishes the one who persists (المصر) in his disbelief (كفره) because his persistence in it is like a bad temperament (سوء مزاج) that leads to illness (مرض). So if it is removed by faith (الإِيمان) and gratitude (الشكر), and he purifies his soul from it, he is freed from its consequence (تبعته). And gratitude (الشكر) was only mentioned first because the observer perceives the blessing (النعمة) first, so he offers an ambiguous gratitude (شكراً مبهماً), then he scrutinizes the matter until he knows the Bestower (المنعم) and believes in Him. Thus it was said.

** وَكانَ اللهُ شاكِراً** (147) and Allah is thankful,

meaning: rewarding, accepting the little and giving the abundant (الجزيل) for the little (القليل).

** عَلِيماً** (147) all-knowing.

of the reality (حق) of your gratitude (شكركم) and your faith (ايمانكم).

(١٤٨) لا يُحِبُّ اللهُ الْجَهْرَ بِالسُّوءِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ إِلَّا مَنْ ظُلِمَ. (Allah does not like the evil that is voiced, except by one who has been wronged)

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ** لا يُحِبُّ اللهُ (Allah does not like) means cursing (الشتم) in seeking vengeance (الانتصار), ** إِلَّا مَنْ ظُلِمَ (except by one who has been wronged), so there is no harm for him to seek vengeance against the one who wronged him with that which is permissible to seek vengeance with in the religion (في الدين). And in it (the context), and similar to it, is وَانْتَصَرُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ ما ظُلِمُوا. (and they defend themselves after they have been wronged.)

And al-Qummi narrated something close to this. He said, and in another narration regarding its interpretation: If a man comes to you and says about you what is not in you—of goodness, praise, and righteous deeds (العمل الصالح)—do not accept it from him and call him a liar, for he has wronged you.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It refers to the guest (الضيف) who stays with a man, and the man does not treat him well (فلا يحسن ضيافته). There is no blame upon him (فلا جناح عليه) if he mentions the evil of what he did.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: Whoever hosts a group of people and treats them poorly (فأساء ضيافتهم), he is among those who have wronged, so there is no blame upon them (فلا جناح عليهم) for what they say about him.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): ** الْجَهْرَ بِالسُّوءِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ (the evil that is voiced) means that a man mentions what is truly in him (the wrongdoer). And وَكانَ اللهُ سَمِيعاً (and Allah is All-hearing) of what is voiced of evil speech, عَلِيماً** (All-knowing) of the truthfulness of the truthful and the lie of the liar, and He will recompense everyone according to his deed.

(1) His saying, Almighty is He, ** مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (instead of the faithful) in this context and elsewhere, serves as an explanatory attribute (الصفة الموضحة), indicating that the guardianship (ولاية) of the disbelievers (الكفّار) does not combine with the attribute of faith (وصف الإِيمان).

(١٤٩) إِنْ تُبْدُوا خَيْراً you manifest obedience (طاعة) and righteousness (براً) ** أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ أَوْ تَعْفُوا عَنْ سُوءٍ ** (or conceal it, or excuse an evil) while you have the power (قدرتكم) to retaliate (الانتقام), without proclaiming the evil (الجهر بالسوء) of the saying/speech (القول), and this is what is intended to be mentioned, and what preceded it is a prelude (تمهيد) to it. And for this reason, He followed it with His saying: ** فَإِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَفُوًّا قَدِيراً (Allah is indeed all-excusing, all-powerful.) He has never ceased to multiply forgiveness (العفو) for the disobedient (العصاة) despite the perfection of His power (كمال قدرته) to retaliate (الانتقام). And this is an encouragement (حث) for the wronged person (المظلوم) to forgive (العفو) after He permitted him to seek victory/justice (الانتصار), urging him toward noble character (مكارم الأخلاق).

(150) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْفُرُونَ بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يُفَرِّقُوا بَيْنَ اللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ (Indeed those who disbelieve in Allah and His apostles, and seek to separate Allah from His apostles) by believing in Allah and disbelieving in His apostles, ** وَيَقُولُونَ نُؤْمِنُ بِبَعْضٍ وَنَكْفُرُ بِبَعْضٍ (and say, 'We believe in some and disbelieve in some') We believe in some of the prophets (الأنبياء) and disbelieve in some, just as the Jews did: they affirmed Moses (peace be upon him) and those prophets who preceded him, but they denied Jesus and Muhammad (peace be upon him). And just as the Christians did: they affirmed Jesus and those who preceded him, but they denied Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace). ** وَيُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَتَّخِذُوا بَيْنَ ذلِكَ ** (and seek to take a way in between that) between faith (الإِيمان) and disbelief (الكفر), ** سَبِيلاً (a way) a path (طريقاً) leading to misguidance (الضلالة), even though faith in Allah is not complete except through faith in all of His apostles and affirming them in everything they conveyed from Him. Thus, the one who disbelieves in part of that is a disbeliever in the whole. ** فَما ذا بَعْدَ الْحَقِّ إِلَّا الضَّلالُ فَأَنَّى تُصْرَفُونَ**. (What is there after the truth except error? So where are you being led away?)

(١٥١) أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْكافِرُونَ (151) It is they who are the faithless, The complete ones (الكاملون) in disbelief (الكفر) ** حَقًّا (151) truly. This is an emphasis (تأكيد) lest it be imagined that their saying, "We believe in some," removes them from the category (حيز) of the disbelievers (الكفّار). ** وَأَعْتَدْنا لِلْكافِرِينَ عَذاباً مُهِيناً ** (151) And We have prepared for the faithless a humiliating punishment. Which humiliates them and disgraces them. Al-Qummi said: They are those who affirmed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم but denied the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام.

(١٥٢) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَلَمْ يُفَرِّقُوا بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ (152) And those who believe in Allah and His apostles and do not make any distinction between any of them Rather, they believe in all of them. ** أُولئِكَ سَوْفَ يُؤْتِيهِمْ (it is they whom He will soon give) We shall give them (نعطيهم) their rewards (أُجُورَهُمْ), which were promised to them. The reward (الثواب) is named recompense (أجراً) to indicate their entitlement (استحقاقهم) to it. And the prefixing with سَوْفَ (soon) is to indicate that it will inevitably (لا محالة) come to pass, even if it is delayed. And يُؤْتِيهِمْ (He will give them) was read with the ياء (yā'). ** وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً ** (and Allah is all-forgiving) He has always pardoned (يعفي) the transgressions (المعاصي) that occurred on their part, All-merciful (رَحِيماً), bestowing upon them various kinds of favor (الإِنعام).

(١٥٣) ** يَسْئَلُكَ أَهْلُ الْكِتابِ أَنْ تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ كِتاباً مِنَ السَّماءِ** (153) The People of the Book ask you to bring down to them a book from the sky.

In al-Majma', it is narrated: That Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf and a group of Jews said: "O Muhammad, if you are a prophet, bring us a book from the sky all at once (جملة), just as Moses (peace be upon him) brought the Torah all at once (جملة)." So, the following was revealed:

** فَقَدْ سَأَلُوا مُوسى أَكْبَرَ مِنْ ذلِكَ ** (They have certainly asked Moses for something greater than that.)

Meaning: Do not let their request to you—that you bring down the Book from the sky—seem great to you, for they asked Moses for something greater than that, even after He had brought them the manifest proofs (البينات الظاهرة) and the dazzling miracles (المعجزات الباهرة). Although this request was made by their forefathers, it is attributed to them because they adhered to their doctrine (مذهبهم) and followed them. The purpose is that their root (عرقهم) (1) is firmly established in that, and what they have proposed to you is not the first of their ignorances (جهالاتهم).

** فَقالُوا أَرِنَا اللهَ جَهْرَةً** (and said, 'Show us Allah manifestly.')

openly (عياناً).

** فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ بِظُلْمِهِمْ** (Thereupon the thunderbolt seized them for their wrongdoing,)

due to their wrongdoing (بظلمهم), which was their obstinacy (تعنتهم) and their asking for what is impossible (لما يستحيل).

** ثُمَّ اتَّخَذُوا الْعِجْلَ** (Then they took up the calf)

they worshipped it (عبدوه).

** مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّناتُ ** (after the manifest proofs had come to them.)

the dazzling miracles (المعجزات الباهرات).

** فَعَفَوْنا عَنْ ذلِكَ** (Yet We excused that,)

due to the vastness of Our mercy (لسعة رحمتنا).

** وَآتَيْنا مُوسى سُلْطاناً مُبِيناً** (and We gave Moses a manifest authority.)

a clear proof (حجة بينة) that demonstrates his truthfulness.

(١٥٤) وَرَفَعْنا فَوْقَهُمُ الطُّورَ بِمِيثاقِهِمْ وَقُلْنا لَهُمُ ادْخُلُوا الْبابَ سُجَّداً وَقُلْنا لَهُمْ لا تَعْدُوا فِي السَّبْتِ وَأَخَذْنا مِنْهُمْ مِيثاقاً غَلِيظاً (154) And We raised the Mount above them for their covenant, and We said to them, 'Enter the gate prostrating,' and We said to them, 'Do not transgress the Sabbath,' and We took a solemn covenant from them.

** وَرَفَعْنا فَوْقَهُمُ الطُّورَ (And We raised the Mount above them) — the mountain (الجبل). ** بِمِيثاقِهِمْ (for their covenant) — so that they would accept it. ** وَقُلْنا لَهُمُ (And We said to them) — upon the tongue of Moses (peace be upon him): "Enter the gate (ادْخُلُوا الْبابَ)," meaning the gate of hittah (باب حطة). ** سُجَّداً وَقُلْنا لَهُمْ (prostrating, and We said to them) — upon the tongue of Moses and David. ** لا تَعْدُوا فِي السَّبْتِ (Do not transgress the Sabbath) — do not exceed, on the day of the Sabbath (يوم السبت), what was made permissible for you to what was made forbidden for you. ** وَأَخَذْنا مِنْهُمْ مِيثاقاً غَلِيظاً (and We took a solemn covenant from them) — concerning that.

** فَبِما نَقْضِهِمْ مِيثاقَهُمْ (155) (155) So, for their breaking their covenant, The meaning is: So they made a covenant and then broke it, and We did to them what We did because of their breaking it. The (ما) is augmentative (مزيدة) for emphasis (للتأكيد). It is also permissible that the (باء) (the preposition 'bi') relates to [the previous verse] حَرَّمْنا عَلَيْهِمْ طَيِّباتٍ (We forbade them good things) which preceded it. وَكُفْرِهِمْ بِآياتِ اللهِ (and their defying the signs of Allah) means: by His proofs (حججه) and evidences (أدلته). ** وَقَتْلِهِمُ الْأَنْبِياءَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍ. and their killing the prophets unjustly, Al-Qummi said: These people did not kill the prophets; rather, their ancestors killed them. But these people were pleased with that, so Allah obligated the killing upon them due to the action of their ancestors. Likewise, whoever is pleased with an action, it becomes incumbent upon him (لزمه), even if he did not perform it. ** وَقَوْلِهِمْ قُلُوبُنا غُلْفٌ (and their saying, 'Our hearts are uncircumcised,') meaning: receptacles (أوعية) for knowledge (للعلوم), or [covered] in coverings (أكنة), as its interpretation has passed. ** بَلْ طَبَعَ اللهُ عَلَيْها بِكُفْرِهِمْ (rather Allah has set a seal upon them for their faithlessness,) meaning: He made them veiled (محجوبة) from knowledge, abandoned them (خذلها), and prevented them from receiving the divine aid (التوفيق) necessary for reflecting upon the signs (الآيات) and remembering through the admonitions (المواعظ). ** فَلا يُؤْمِنُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً (so they do not believe except a few) meaning: a few of them, or a slight belief (إيماناً قليلاً) which is insignificant (لا عبرة به) due to its deficiency (لنقصانه).

footnote (١) His saying: their root (*عرقهم*) is deeply established (*راسخ*) in that, meaning: their origin (*أصلهم*) is fixed upon it, and it has been imprinted upon the hearts of these people, such that it is as if they are brought up upon insistence (*الإبرام*) and argumentation (*المحاجة*).

(١٥٦) وَبِكُفْرِهِمْ (156) and for their disbelief, in Jesus, peace be upon him, ** وَقَوْلِهِمْ عَلى مَرْيَمَ بُهْتاناً عَظِيماً and for their saying against Mary a great calumny, meaning their attributing her to fornication (الزنا).

In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Verily, the contentment (رضى) of people cannot be possessed, and their tongues cannot be controlled. Did they not attribute to Mary, the daughter of 'Imran, that she conceived Jesus, peace be upon him, from a carpenter (1) named Joseph (يوسف)?

** وَقَوْلِهِمْ إِنَّا قَتَلْنَا الْمَسِيحَ عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ رَسُولَ اللهِ (157) and for their saying, 'We killed the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, the apostle of Allah' meaning, 'the apostle of Allah' according to their claim (بِزَعْمِهِمْ). Its parallel is: ** إِنَّ رَسُولَكُمُ الَّذِي أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ لَمَجْنُونٌ (Indeed, your apostle who has been sent to you is surely mad.) And that was when Allah raised Him to Himself: ** وَما قَتَلُوهُ وَما صَلَبُوهُ وَلكِنْ شُبِّهَ لَهُمْ (Yet they did not kill him, nor did they crucify him, though it was made to appear to them.) The mention of this story has already passed in Sūrat Āl ʿImrān, at the Almighty's saying: ** إِذْ قالَ اللهُ يا عِيسى إِنِّي مُتَوَفِّيكَ وَرافِعُكَ إِلَيَ ** (When Allah said, 'O Jesus! I shall take you away and raise you up toward Myself.) It is said that Allah only condemned them for what the discourse indicated: their audacity toward Allah, their intention to kill His Prophet who was supported by overwhelming miracles, and their boasting (بِتَجَحْجُحِهِمْ) (2) about it—not merely for this saying of theirs, according to their own reckoning. ** وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ لَفِي شَكٍّ مِنْهُ (and those who differed concerning him are surely in doubt about him.) It is said that when that event occurred, people differed. Some of the Jews said that he (Jesus) was a liar, so we truly killed him. Others hesitated, and some of them said: "If this was Jesus (peace be upon him), then where is our companion?" And some of them said: "The face is the face of Jesus (peace be upon him), but the body is the body of our companion." And those who heard from him (Jesus) that Allah would raise him to the heaven said: "He was raised to the heaven." And a group said: "The human nature (النَّاسُوت) was crucified, and the divine nature (اللاهُوت) ascended (1)." ** ما لَهُمْ بِهِ مِنْ عِلْمٍ إِلَّا اتِّباعَ الظَّنِ (They do not have any knowledge of that, following nothing but conjecture.) Rather, they follow conjecture (الظَّنّ). ** وَما قَتَلُوهُ يَقِيناً (Certainly, they did not kill him.) (meaning) a certain killing (قَتْلاً يَقِيناً) as they claimed, or it is an affirmation of the negation of the killing, meaning truly (حَقّاً).

(١٥٨) بَلْ رَفَعَهُ اللهُ إِلَيْهِ (Rather, Allah raised Him up toward Himself) A refutation and denial of his killing, and an affirmation of His raising him.

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah has regions (بِقَاعاً) in His heavens. So, whoever is ascended with to a region (بُقْعَة) among them, he has been ascended with to Him. Do you not hear Allah saying in the story of Jesus son of Mary: ** بَلْ رَفَعَهُ اللهُ إِلَيْهِ** (Rather, Allah raised Him up toward Himself)?"

Al-Qummi (said): He was raised while wearing a woolen cloak (مِدْرَعَة) upon him.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), said: "Jesus son of Mary was raised with a woolen cloak (مِدْرَعَة) made from the spinning of Mary, the weaving of Mary, and the sewing of Mary. When he reached the heaven, he was called out to: 'O Jesus, cast off from yourself the adornment of the world.'"

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, Jesus son of Mary came to Jerusalem (بَيْتِ الْمَقْدِس) and remained there, calling them and encouraging them toward what is with Allah for thirty-three years, until the Jews sought him and claimed that they tortured him and buried him alive in the earth. And some of them claimed that they killed him and crucified him. But Allah would not grant them authority over him. Rather, it was made to appear so to them (شُبِّهَ لَهُمْ), and they were not able to torture him and bury him, nor to kill him and crucify him, because if they had been able to do that, it would have been a denial of His saying. But Allah raised him up toward Him after He caused him to die (تَوَفَّاهُ). The beginning of this narrative has already preceded in Surat Āl ‘Imrān."

** وَكانَ اللهُ عَزِيزاً (and Allah is All-mighty) He is not overcome regarding what He wills, All-wise (حَكِيماً) in what He decreed for His servants.

(١٥٩) ** وَإِنْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ إِلَّا لَيُؤْمِنَنَّ بِهِ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ (159) There is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death.**

It was said: It means that there is not a single one among the Jews and Christians but will surely believe that Jesus (peace be upon him) is the servant of Allah and His Messenger before he dies, even if it is at the moment his soul departs, though his belief will not benefit him. A narration to this effect is transmitted from them (the Imams) (peace be upon them).

وَيَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ يَكُونُ and on the Day of Resurrection He will be

عَلَيْهِمْ شَهِيداً a witness against them.

So He will bear witness against the Jews for their denial, and against the Christians that they claimed Him to be the son of Allah.

And al-Qummi narrated from Shah ibn Hawshab, who said: Al-Hajjaj said to me: "O Shah, there is an Ayah in the Book of Allah that has baffled me." I said: "O Emir, which Ayah is it?" He said: وَإِنْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ إِلَّا لَيُؤْمِنَنَّ بِهِ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ ** (There is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death). "By Allah, I pass by a Jew or a Christian whose neck is struck, then I observe him (1) with my eye, and I do not see him move his lips until he expires (2)." I said: "May Allah set the Emir right, it is not as you have interpreted it." He asked: "How is it then?" I replied: "Indeed, Jesus (peace be upon him) will descend to the world before the Day of Resurrection, and there will remain no follower of any religion, neither a Jew nor anyone else, but will believe in him before his death, and he will pray behind al-Mahdi (peace be upon him)." He said: "Woe unto you! Where did you get this from, and where did you bring it from?" I replied: "Muhammad ibn Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) narrated it to me." He said: "You have brought it from a pure spring (عين صافية)."

Al-Qummi said: And it was narrated: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returns (إذا رجع), all people will believe in Him.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding its interpretation: There is no one from any of the religions who dies but sees the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) in truth, from the first and the last generations.

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The belief of the People of the Book is only in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

And in al-Majma', regarding one of its meanings: ** لَيُؤْمِنَنَ (will surely believe) in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم before the death of the person from the People of the Book. He said: And our companions (أصحابنا) narrated it...

And in al-Jawami', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): It is forbidden for a soul to depart its body until it sees Muhammad and Ali. And the narrations concerning this meaning are numerous (3).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "This was revealed specifically concerning us. There is no man from the progeny of Fatimah who dies or departs from the world until he acknowledges the Imam and his Imamate, just as the sons of Jacob acknowledged Joseph when ** قالُوا تَاللهِ لَقَدْ آثَرَكَ اللهُ عَلَيْنا** (They said, 'By Allah, Allah has indeed preferred you over us.')."

I say: It means that the progeny of Fatimah are the ones intended by "the People of the Book" here, and that is due to His saying, the Glorified: ** ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا الْكِتابَ الَّذِينَ اصْطَفَيْنا مِنْ عِبادِنا (Then We made those of Our servants whom We chose heirs to the Book), for they are the ones intended by "those whom We chose" (الْمُصْطَفَيْنَ) there, as will be mentioned when interpreting it.

(1) رمقه بعينه رمقاً (from the category of قتل): He prolonged his gaze at him (al-Majma').

(2) خمد المريض (The sick person expired): He fainted or died (M).

(3) Among them is what the Imamiyyah narrated: that those who are dying from all religions see the Messenger of Allah and His successors at the time of death. And they narrate concerning this from Ali (peace be upon him) that He said to al-Harith al-Hamdani: "O Harith of Hamdan, whoever dies will see me, whether a believer or a hypocrite, face to face. His eye will recognize me, and I will recognize him with My eye."

(١٦٠) فَبِظُلْمٍ مِنَ الَّذِينَ هادُوا (160) So, because of the wrongdoings of those who were Jews, So, because of a great wrongdoing from them, ** حَرَّمْنا عَلَيْهِمْ طَيِّباتٍ أُحِلَّتْ لَهُمْ (We forbade them good things which had been lawful for them). It was said that these are the things mentioned in His glorious saying: وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ هادُوا حَرَّمْنا كُلَّ ذِي ظُفُرٍ (And to those who were Jews We forbade every animal with an undivided hoof) (the Ayah).

In al-Kafi, al-Ayyashi, and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever planted wheat (حنطة) in a land and did not purify his crop (يزك زرعه), and his crop came out with much barley (الشعير), this is due to the injustice of his action in owning the land (رقبة الأرض) or due to injustice toward his farmers (مزارعيه) and tenants (أكرته).
