This is because Allah says: ** فَبِظُلْمٍ مِنَ الَّذِينَ هادُوا حَرَّمْنا عَلَيْهِمْ (So, because of the wrongdoings of those who were Jews, We forbade them) طَيِّباتٍ أُحِلَّتْ لَهُمْ (good things which had been lawful for them), meaning the meats of camels (الإِبل), cattle (البقر), and sheep (الغنم), وَبِصَدِّهِمْ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ كَثِيراً (2) (and because of their hindering many from the way of Allah).

(١٦١) وَأَخْذِهِمُ الرِّبَوا وَقَدْ نُهُوا عَنْهُ وَأَكْلِهِمْ أَمْوالَ النَّاسِ بِالْباطِلِ (4:161) and for taking usury—though they had been forbidden from it—and for consuming people’s wealth wrongfully. by way of bribery (الرشوة) and other forbidden means (الوجوه المحرمة). ** وَأَعْتَدْنا لِلْكافِرِينَ مِنْهُمْ عَذاباً أَلِيماً ** (4:161) We have prepared for the faithless among them a painful punishment. excluding those who repented and believed.

لكِنِ الرَّاسِخُونَ فِي الْعِلْمِ مِنْهُمْ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَما أُنْزِلَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ وَالْمُقِيمِينَ الصَّلاةَ (162) But those of them who are well-grounded in knowledge, and the faithful, believe in what has been sent down to you, and what was sent down before you, and those who maintain the prayer, It was said that it means: and they believe in those who maintain the prayer (بِالْمُقِيمِينَ الصَّلاةَ), meaning the Prophets (الأنبياء). And it was said, rather, it is in the accusative case (نصب) due to praise (المدح). And it was read in the anomalous readings (في الشواذ) in the nominative case (بالرفع). ** وَالْمُؤْتُونَ الزَّكاةَ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ and those who give the zakat, and those who believe in Allah وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ أُولئِكَ سَنُؤْتِيهِمْ أَجْراً عَظِيماً and the Last Day—them We shall give a great reward. Because they combine sound faith (الإِيمان الصحيح) and righteous deed (العمل الصالح).

footnote واسمه وما فعلا ، والمراد برؤيتهم في ذلك الحال العلم بثمرة ولايتهم وعداوتهم علم اليقين بعلامات يجدونها من نفوسهم ومشاهدة أحوال يدركونها كما قد روي : ان الإنسان إذا عاين الموت أري في تلك الحالة ما يدله على أنّه من أهل الجنة أو من أهل النار. and His Name and what He did. What is meant by their seeing in that state is the certain knowledge (علم اليقين) of the fruit of their loyalty (ولايتهم) and their enmity (عداوتهم), through signs (علامات) they find within themselves and the witnessing of states (أحوال) they perceive, just as it has been narrated: that when a person witnesses death, he is shown in that state what guides him to whether he is one of the people of Paradise or one of the people of the Fire.

footnote (١) في الحديث ذكر الأكار بالفتح والتشديد وهو الزراع والأكرة بالضم الحفرة وبها سمي الأكار وأكرت النهر من باب ضرب شققته (م) (1) In the Hadith, mention is made of الأكار (al-akkār) with fathah and tashdid (doubling), and he is the farmer (الزراع). And الأكرة (al-ukrah) with dammah (vowel 'u') is the pit (الحفرة), and by it the farmer (الأكار) was named. And I dug (أكرت) the river, from the category (باب) of ضَرَبَ (ḍaraba), meaning I split it (شققته) (M).

footnote (٢) وبمنعهم عباد الله عن دينه وسبيله التي شرعها لعباده صداً كثيراً وكان صدهم عن سبيل الله بقولهم على الله الباطل وادعائهم ان ذلك على الله وتبديلهم كتاب الله وخريفهم معانيه عن وجوهه وأعظم من ذلك كله جحدهم نبوة محمّد صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم تركهم بيان ما عملوه من أمره لمن جهله من الناس عن مجاهد وغيره (مجمع البيان) (2) And by their preventing the servants of Allah from His religion and His path (سبيله) which He legislated for His servants, a great prevention (صداً كثيراً). And their prevention from the path of Allah (سبيل الله) was by their saying falsehood (الباطل) against Allah, and their claim that this was from Allah, and their altering the Book of Allah (كتاب الله) and deviating its meanings (معانيه) from their proper aspects (وجوهه). And greater than all of that was their denial (جحدهم) of the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and their abandoning the explanation of what they knew concerning His command to those among the people who were ignorant of it, narrated from Mujahid (مجاهد) and others (مجمع البيان).

(١٦٣) إِنَّا أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ كَما أَوْحَيْنا إِلى نُوحٍ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ (163) We have indeed revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him,

It was said that this is a response to the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) regarding their proposal that a book be sent down to them from the heaven, and an argument against them, [stating] that His affair concerning revelation is like the rest of the prophets who preceded him.

وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلى إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْماعِيلَ وَإِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَالْأَسْباطِ وَعِيسى وَأَيُّوبَ وَيُونُسَ وَهارُونَ وَسُلَيْمانَ وَآتَيْنا داوُدَ زَبُوراً and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, and Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and We gave David the Psalms.

And it was read (وقرئ) with the ḍammah on the zāy (زُبُوراً).

(١٦٤) وَرُسُلاً And We sent messengers قَدْ قَصَصْناهُمْ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَرُسُلاً لَمْ نَقْصُصْهُمْ عَلَيْكَ وَكَلَّمَ اللهُ مُوسى تَكْلِيماً (whom We have recounted to you before, and messengers whom We have not recounted to you. And Allah spoke to Moses a direct speaking.)

It is said that this is the ultimate rank of revelation (الوحي), which He specified for Moses among them. However, Allah favored Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم by granting him the equivalent of what He granted to every single one of them.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from them (peace be upon them) [Imams]: "Indeed, I revealed إِلَيْكَ كَما أَوْحَيْنا إِلى نُوحٍ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ (to you, just as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him)." Thus, every revelation was gathered for him.

And in al-Kafi (1), narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "I was given the long chapters (السور الطِوَل) in place of the Torah (التوراة), and I was given the chapters of the hundreds (المئين) in place of the Gospel (الإِنجيل), and I was given the oft-repeated chapters (المثاني) in place of the Psalms (الزبور), and I was favored with the Mufassal (the detailed section), which consists of sixty-eight chapters."

And in it [i.e., al-Kafi], and in al-Ikmal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "There were prophets between Adam and Noah, some hidden (مستخفين) and some manifest (مستعلنين). For this reason, their mention was concealed in the Quran, and they were not named, unlike those prophets who were manifest and named. This is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَرُسُلاً قَدْ قَصَصْناهُمْ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَرُسُلاً لَمْ نَقْصُصْهُمْ عَلَيْكَ (whom We have recounted to you before, and messengers whom We have not recounted to you), meaning He did not name the hidden ones (المستخفين) as He named the manifest ones (المستعلنين) among the prophets."

And in al-Khisal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, Allah held a private conversation (ناجى) with Moses (peace be upon him) with one hundred thousand words and twenty-four thousand words over three days and their nights. Moses (peace be upon him) neither ate nor drank during them. When he returned to the Children of Israel and heard their speech, he despised them (1) because of the sweetness of the speech of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, that had reached his ears."

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) in a tradition: "So He went out with them to Mount Sinai (طور سيناء) and stationed them at the foot of the mountain. Moses ascended the Mount (الطور) and asked Allah, the Exalted, to speak to him and to let them hear His speech. So Allah, exalted be His remembrance, spoke to him, and they heard His speech from above and below, from the right and the left, from behind and in front. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, originated it (أحدثه) in the tree, then made it emanate from it so that they heard it from every direction (من جميع الوجوه)."

And from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): كَلَّمَ اللهُ مُوسى تَكْلِيماً (And Allah spoke to Moses a direct speaking) was without limbs (جوارح), tools (أدوات), lips (شفة), or uvula (لهوات). Glory be to Him and Exalted is He far above such attributes (الصفات).

And from him (peace be upon him) in a tradition, when a man asked him about the verses (الآيات) that were confusing to him: "The speech of Allah is not of a single type. Part of it is what Allah spoke to the Messengers, part of it is what He cast into their hearts, part of it is a vision (رؤياً) seen by the Messengers, and part of it is revelation (وحي) and sending down (تنزيل) that is recited and read. All of it is the speech of Allah. So, suffice yourself with what I have described to you from the Book of Allah, for the meaning of the speech of Allah is not of a single type, as part of it is what the messengers of the heaven convey to the messengers of the earth."

And in al-Ihtijaj, concerning the conversation between the Jews and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: They said: "Moses is better than you." He asked: "Why?" They replied: "Because Allah, the Exalted, spoke to him four thousand words, but He did not speak to you at all." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, I have been granted something superior to that." They asked: "And what is that?" He replied: "His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: سُبْحانَ الَّذِي أَسْرى بِعَبْدِهِ لَيْلاً (The Glorified is He Who carried His servant on a night journey) (the Ayah)." The complete tradition will come in Surat Bani Isra'il, if Allah wills.

(1) The explanation of this tradition by the Author (may his secret be sanctified) has already preceded in the First Introduction, so refer to it.

(١٦٥) رُسُلاً مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنْذِرِينَ لِئَلَّا يَكُونَ لِلنَّاسِ عَلَى اللهِ حُجَّةٌ بَعْدَ الرُّسُلِ (Apostles, bearers of good news and warners, so that mankind may not have any argument against Allah, after the apostles.) lest they say: "Why did You not send us an apostle who would alert us and teach us what we did not know." وَكانَ اللهُ عَزِيزاً (And Allah is all-mighty) He is not overcome in what He wills. حَكِيماً (all-wise) in what He ordains.

(١٦٦) لكِنِ اللهُ يَشْهَدُ بِما أَنْزَلَ إِلَيْكَ (166) But Allah bears witness to what He has sent down to you It was said that when إِنَّا أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ (Indeed, We have revealed to you) was revealed, they said: "We do not bear witness to this for you." So, the following was revealed: أَنْزَلَهُ بِعِلْمِهِ (He has sent it down with His knowledge) (meaning) that you are worthy of it. وَالْمَلائِكَةُ يَشْهَدُونَ and the angels bear witness also. وَكَفى بِاللهِ شَهِيداً and Allah is sufficient as a witness even if no one else bears witness to it.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): لكِنِ اللهُ يَشْهَدُ بِما أَنْزَلَ إِلَيْكَ (But Allah bears witness to what He has sent down to you) was only revealed concerning Ali (في علي) in the verse.

footnote (١) مقته مقتاً من باب قتل أبغضه أشدّ البغض عن أمر قبيح فهو مقيت وممقوت (م)

(١٦٧) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ قَدْ ضَلُّوا ضَلالاً بَعِيداً (167) Indeed those who disbelieve and bar [others] from the way of Allah have certainly strayed into a deep error. Because they combined disbelief (الضلال) and misguidance (الإِضلال), and because the one who misguides others is deeper in error and further removed from abandoning it. (١٦٨) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَظَلَمُوا (168) Indeed those who disbelieve and are wrongful, They combined the two لَمْ يَكُنِ اللهُ لِيَغْفِرَ لَهُمْ وَلا لِيَهْدِيَهُمْ طَرِيقاً. (168) Allah will not forgive them, nor will He guide them to any path, (١٦٩) إِلَّا طَرِيقَ جَهَنَّمَ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً وَكانَ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيراً (169) except the path of hell, to abide in it forever. And that is easy for Allah. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: Gabriel descended with this verse thus: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَظَلَمُوا آل محمّد صلوات الله عليهم حقهم لَمْ يَكُنِ اللهُ (Indeed those who disbelieve and wronged the right of the Family of Muhammad, the blessings of Allah be upon them) (the rest of the verse). And al-Qummi [narrated]: Abu Abd Allah [Imam al-Sadiq] read [the verse]: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَظَلَمُوا آل محمّد حقهم (Indeed those who disbelieve and wronged the right of the Family of Muhammad) (the rest of the verse).

(١٧٠) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جاءَكُمُ الرَّسُولُ بِالْحَقِّ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ فَآمِنُوا خَيْراً لَكُمْ (170) O mankind! The Apostle has certainly come to you with the truth from your Lord. So have faith, it is better for you.

(Have) faith which is better for you, or perform an action which is better for you, or let faith be better for you.

** وَإِنْ تَكْفُرُوا فَإِنَّ لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَكانَ اللهُ عَلِيماً حَكِيماً**. And if you disbelieve, [it will not harm Him], for to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding: قَدْ جاءَكُمُ الرَّسُولُ بِالْحَقِّ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ (The Apostle has certainly come to you with the truth from your Lord), [He said it refers to] the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali. فَآمِنُوا خَيْراً لَكُمْ وَإِنْ تَكْفُرُوا (So have faith, it is better for you. And if you disbelieve), [He said it refers to] the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (the rest of the Ayah).

(١٧١) يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ لا تَغْلُوا فِي دِينِكُمْ ** (171) O People of the Book! Do not exaggerate in your religion The Jews exaggerated in degrading Jesus (peace be upon him) until they accused him of being born illegitimately (لِغَيْرِ رِشْدَة) (1). And the Christians exaggerated in elevating him until they took him as a god. وَلا تَقُولُوا عَلَى اللهِ إِلَّا الْحَقَ meaning, declaring Him free from a partner, a consort (الصاحبة), and a son. ** إِنَّمَا الْمَسِيحُ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ رَسُولُ اللهِ وَكَلِمَتُهُ أَلْقاها إِلى مَرْيَمَ and do not say concerning Allah anything except the truth. The Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, was only an apostle of Allah, and His Word that He cast upon Mary He delivered it to her and brought it about within her. ** وَرُوحٌ مِنْهُ and a Spirit from Him.** which originated from Him.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it (the Spirit) and He said: "It is a created spirit (روح مخلوقة) which Allah created in Adam and Jesus."

In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Two created spirits (روحان مخلوقتان) which He chose and selected: the spirit of Adam and the spirit of Jesus (peace be upon him)."

فَآمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَلا تَقُولُوا the gods are ** ثَلاثَةٌ So have faith in Allah and His apostles, and do not say, ‘Three.’ (Meaning) Allah, the Messiah, and Mary, as is indicated by the Almighty's saying: Did you say to the people, ‘Take me and my mother for gods besides Allah’ انْتَهُوا from the Trinity (التثليث). ** خَيْراً لَكُمْ Cease! It is better for you. Its parallel has passed. ** إِنَّمَا اللهُ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ Allah is only one God. a true unity (وحدة حقيقية) to which no aspect of multiplicity or plurality can ever apply. ** سُبْحانَهُ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ وَلَدٌ He is far too exalted to have a son. Glorify Him with a glorification (تسبيحاً) that He should have a son. How could He, when a son must necessarily be similar to the father? Exalted is Allah far above having a similar or an equal (مماثل ومعادل). ** لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. in ownership (مُلْكاً), dominion (مِلْكاً), and creation (خَلْقاً). Nothing of that resembles Him so that He might take it as a son. ** وَكَفى بِاللهِ وَكِيلاً Allah is sufficient as a trustee. This is an alert regarding His independence (غِنَاه) from a son, for the need for a son only arises so that he may be a trustee (وكيلاً) for his father. But Allah, the Glorified, is established in preserving all things, sufficient in that regard, and independent of anyone who might succeed Him or assist Him.

footnote (١) And *li-rushdah* (*لِرِشْدَة*) with the *kasrah* (*الكسرة*) on the *rāʾ* (*الراء*) and the *fatḥah* (*الفتح*) is a linguistic variant, meaning of legitimate lineage (*صحيح النسب*). And *li-ghayri rushdah* (*لِغَيْرِ رِشْدَة*) is the opposite. It is narrated from al-Azhari that the *fatḥah* (*الفتح*) in *li-rushdah* (*لِرِشْدَة*) and *li-zīnah* (*لِزِينَة*) is more eloquent than the *kasrah* (*الكسرة*). (*مجمع*)

(١٧٢) لَنْ يَسْتَنْكِفَ الْمَسِيحُ (The Messiah will never disdain) He will not disdain (لَنْ يَأْنَفَ) (1) to be a servant of Allah, because servitude (عُبُودِيَّة) to Allah is an honor He boasts of, and humiliation (الْمَذَلَّة) and disdain (الِاسْتِنْكَاف) are only found in servitude to other than Him. And it is narrated: That the delegation of Najran said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Why do you find fault (تَعِيب) (2) with our companion?" He said: "And who is your companion?" They said: "Jesus (عِيسَى)." He said: "And what thing should I say?" They said: "You say that He is a servant of Allah." He said: "Indeed, it is not a disgrace (عَار) for Him to be a servant of Allah." They said: "Yes, it is." So, the verse was revealed: وَلَا الْمَلائِكَةُ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ. Nor do the Cherished Angels (الْمَلَائِكَةُ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ) disdain to be servants of Allah. ** وَمَنْ يَسْتَنْكِفْ عَنْ عِبادَتِهِ وَيَسْتَكْبِرْ ** (But whoever disdains His worship and is arrogant,) and elevates himself above it (وَيَتَرَفَّعْ عَنْهَا). And arrogance (الِاسْتِكْبَار) is lesser than disdain (الِاسْتِنْكَاف). Arrogance (الِاسْتِكْبَار) is only used where there is no entitlement (اسْتِحْقَاق), unlike pride (التَّكَبُّر), for it may sometimes be based on entitlement (اسْتِحْقَاق), as is the case with Allah, the Glorified. ** فَسَيَحْشُرُهُمْ إِلَيْهِ جَمِيعاً (He will gather them all to Him.) The disdainer (الْمُسْتَنْكِف), the arrogant (الْمُسْتَكْبِر), and the one who affirms servitude (الْمُقِرّ بِالْعُبُودِيَّة). Then He will recompense them according to their states.

(1) He disdained (أَنِفَ) the thing, disdains (يَأْنَفُ) with disdain (أَنَفًا), meaning he disdained (اسْتَنْكَفَ) and was arrogant (اسْتَكْبَرَ).

(2) He found fault (عَابَهُ) with him, meaning he attributed a fault (عَيْب) to him. And he also found fault (عَابَهُ) with him if he made him possess a fault (عَيْب). And تَعَيَّبَهُ is similar (مَجْمَع).

(١٧٣) فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ فَيُوَفِّيهِمْ أُجُورَهُمْ وَيَزِيدُهُمْ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ اسْتَنْكَفُوا وَاسْتَكْبَرُوا فَيُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذاباً أَلِيماً وَلا يَجِدُونَ لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ وَلِيًّا وَلا نَصِيراً (But as for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, He will pay them their full reward and enhance them out of His grace. And as for those who disdain and are arrogant, He will punish them with a painful punishment, and they will not find for themselves any guardian or helper besides Allah.) The meaning is apparent.

(١٧٤) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جاءَكُمْ بُرْهانٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ وَأَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكُمْ نُوراً مُبِيناً (174) O mankind! A proof has certainly come to you from your Lord, and We have sent down to you a manifest light. It was said that the Proof (البرهان) is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the Light (النور) is the Quran. And it was said that the Proof (البرهان) is the miracles (المعجزات), and the Light (النور) is the Quran. Meaning, there have come to you the evidences of reason (دلائل العقل) and the testimonies of revelation (شواهد النقل), and there remains for you neither excuse (عذر) nor pretext (علة). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The Light (النور) is the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him).

(١٧٥) فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَاعْتَصَمُوا بِهِ فَسَيُدْخِلُهُمْ فِي رَحْمَةٍ مِنْهُ (175) But as for those who have faith in Allah and hold fast to Him, He will admit them into His mercy a deserved reward

وَفَضْلٍ and grace and an extra kindness beyond it

وَيَهْدِيهِمْ إِلَيْهِ and guide them to Him meaning, to Allah, or to the promised mercy and grace

صِراطاً مُسْتَقِيماً on a straight path. The verification of the meaning of al-sirat (الصراط) has already passed in Surat al-Fatiha.

Al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Proof (البرهان) is Muhammad, the Light (النور) is Ali, and the Straight Path (الصراط المستقيم) is Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both).

And al-Qummi (narrates that) the Light (النور) is the Imamate (إمامة) of the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and holding fast (الاعتصام) is adherence to his wilayah (ولايته) and the wilayah (ولاية) of the Imams after him.

(176) يَسْتَفْتُونَكَ ** (They ask you for a ruling) meaning: concerning al-kalālah (الكلالة), as indicated by the answer. It is narrated that Jābir ibn ʿAbd Allāh was ill, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم visited him. Jābir said: "O Messenger of Allah, I have kalālah (كلالة). How should I dispose of my wealth?" So, the verse was revealed: ** قُلِ اللهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ فِي الْكَلالَةِ (Say: 'Allah gives you a ruling concerning al-kalālah') Its interpretation has already passed at the beginning of the Sūrah. إِنِ امْرُؤٌ هَلَكَ لَيْسَ لَهُ وَلَدٌ وَلَهُ أُخْتٌ (If a man dies having no child, and he has a sister) meaning: a full sister (أخت لأم وأب) or a paternal half-sister (أخت لأب), as narrated from al-Sādiq عليه السلام, just as it was mentioned previously. ** فَلَها نِصْفُ ما تَرَكَ وَهُوَ يَرِثُها (she shall have half of what he leaves, and he shall inherit from her) meaning: and the man inherits all of his sister's wealth if the sister is the deceased ** إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَها وَلَدٌ ** (if she has no child) nor a father (والد), because the discussion is concerning the inheritance of al-kalālah (الكلالة), and because the Sunnah (السنة) indicates that siblings do not inherit alongside the father, as has been successively narrated (تواتر) from the People of the House (أهل البيت) عليهم السلام. ** فَإِنْ كانَتَا اثْنَتَيْنِ (But if there be two [sisters]) The pronoun refers to those who inherit through siblinghood (بالاخوة). ** فَلَهُمَا الثُّلُثانِ مِمَّا تَرَكَ وَإِنْ كانُوا إِخْوَةً رِجالاً وَنِساءً فَلِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ** (then they shall have two-thirds of what he leaves. And if they be siblings, men and women, then the male shall have the like of the share of two females).

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated from al-Bāqir عليه السلام: If a man dies and he has a sister, she takes half of the inheritance by virtue of the Ayah, just as the daughter would take if she were present. The remaining half is returned to her by virtue of kinship (بالرحم) if the deceased has no closer heir than her. If, instead of a sister, there is a brother, he takes the entire inheritance by virtue of the Ayah, due to Allah's saying: وَهُوَ يَرِثُها إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَها وَلَدٌ (and he shall inherit from her if she has no child) If there are two sisters, they take two-thirds by virtue of the Ayah, and the remaining third by virtue of kinship (بالرحم). ** وَإِنْ كانُوا إِخْوَةً رِجالاً وَنِساءً فَلِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ (And if they be siblings, men and women, then the male shall have the like of the share of two females) All of this applies if the deceased has no child, parents, or spouse. The content of this narration is reported in many of the infallible narrations (الأخبار المعصومية) recorded in al-Kāfī (الكافي) and other works.

يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمْ أَنْ تَضِلُّوا (Allah clarifies [the laws] for you, lest you go astray) It was said: meaning, He clarifies for you the straying (ضلالكم) which is characteristic of you if you are left to your natures, so that you may guard against it and seek its opposite. Or, He clarifies the truth and correctness for you, lest you go astray (كراهة أن تضلوا), or so that you do not go astray (لئلا تضلوا). ** وَاللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ ** (and Allah is knowing of all things) Thus, He is Knower of the welfare (مصالح) of the servants, and of life (المحيا) and death (الممات). It was said that this is the last Ayah revealed concerning legal rulings (الأحكام).

In Thawāb al-Aʿmāl (ثواب الأعمال) and al-ʿAyyāshī (العيّاشيّ), narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: Whoever recites Sūrat al-Nisāʾ (سورة النساء) every Friday will be safe from the squeezing of the grave (ضغطة القبر), if Allah the Almighty wills.
